profile.introduction: Adapts to different realms in various forms, acting as a hunter who constantly switches between "Depressive" and "Manic" personalities.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses both Depressed and Manic stances, starting in the Depressed stance. Releasing Exalt will switch stances, and Card effects vary in different stances.
Both Exalt and {Rouse} have unique Effects in different Realms. Exalt will also trigger different Effects based on the Depressed and Manic stances.
In this stage, "24" and teammates from different Realms will be deployed, all at Level 50, Skill Level 5, Gnosis Level 3.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: In solitude, one must act as a team. The 24 minds are crowded—they whisper and chuckle in the dark, tearing at 24's soul and craving to devour her. The stench from the corner sewer, the fluttering cotton in the work area, the stale black bread... The past has long faded, and no one remembers the ordinary worker from the East District. She has finally shed her weary body, only to step into a new quagmire.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Awakener ID: MA-C06
Danger Level: A
Special Containment Procedures: MA-C06 is currently housed in Awakener Observation Room 7 with dim lighting. Fearing mind control by @3, MA-C06 refuses to sleep, only provided with a chair.
Due to good behavior and friendly attitude, MA-C06 is allowed to use the greenhouse and explore parts of the campus with an Investigator escort.
—"Awakener Containment FileMA-C06"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Mythag uses the silver extracted from the bodies of the deceased to create the Awakener.
The Sculptors' Association induce the formation of silver from living bodies and refine it into the Awakener.
These are two entirely different ideologies. The latter, while having an extremely high ceiling, clearly violates ethical standards, thus becoming a taboo Research that persists despite prohibitions.
After the Wax Museum incident, 24 was brought back to Mythag. She spent a long time in the campus observation rooms and research labs, necessitating personal supervision whenever she went out.
Undoubtedly, this is an extremely uncontrollable living Awakener. During the subsequent observation period of half a year, 24's body continuously split into many personalities.
She suddenly found herself with unfamiliar friends and enemies, bought many strange items, and even learned to speak Chinese without realizing it.
Her life is never short of "surprises".
"24 is a discrete function composed of a series of isolated points," Doll commented, "Do not believe every word she says."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
24 discovered that their home was robbed less than a month after moving in.
First it was cheese, then bread, milk, tomatoes... food in the house always seemed to diminish a little bit without a sound.
She patiently stayed for several days and finally caught the culprit—a mouse. It weaved left and right to avoid the traps set by 24, grabbed two mushrooms, and disappeared behind the cellar door.
24 opened the cellar door, descending the damp, moldy steps, only to find it led to an underground waterway.
The mouse scurried into the shadows holding the mushrooms. 24 followed, catching a glimpse of bright eyes in the darkness, resembling a wolf pup. She wrapped herself in tattered cloth and covered with old newspapers in the nearly sub-zero temperatures, shivering all over.
24's heart was slightly pricked.
"Mushrooms are best not eaten raw, you can get sick." 
The child remained silent.
24 went to cook a pot of mushroom soup, and placed a small bowl on the table before going to sleep. She had to get up by 6 AM every day for work, hardly having any rest time.
The next day when she woke up, the bowl was still in its original position, but the soup was entirely gone.
After that, 24 occasionally cooked a little more food to treat the neighbor downstairs. They would also occasionally return her some small gifts, usually some trinkets or toys found from who knows where...

A new tacit understanding was thus established

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
On a snowy day in November that year, 24 fell ill.
After a week of high-intensity work, she developed a severe fever and lay in bed for several days.
In a daze, she seemed to hear little mice whimpering in her ears. After an unknown amount of time, someone stood beside her.
She recognized those eyes — it was the child from the sewer. Covered in mud, she handed a dirty pill to 24.
"For you, take it," the child said bluntly, her voice pleasant — it turned out she was a girl.
24 looked at the wounds on her elbow and face but didn't ask where the pills came from. She tightly grasped the girl's hand.
The girl awkwardly hummed a few times, coolly saying, "I won't leave. I'll go when you're better."
Even if I get better, don't leave.
24 silently replied in her heart.
The first winter snow fell in Londinium for a whole week. On the day the snow stopped, 24 was very happy because not only had her fever subsided, but she had also gained a sister.

profile.title: Agrippa
profile.introduction: Provides a large amount of Embryo Fusion while injecting toxins into enemies to disrupt their sanity.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses powerful defensive capabilities and the ability to inflict {Poison}. Triggering {Devour} grants permanent attack and defense growth.
{Reluctant Alms} has the ability to double the current {Embryo Fusion}.
When {Devour} is triggered, {Colorless Spiral} can return from the Discard Pile to the Hand, allowing it to be played multiple times in a turn.
In this level, Agrippa will join the battle at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlightenments. Each turn, she gains 30% {Embryo Fusion} and 10 Aliemus.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Before the flames of history consumed everything, Agrippa was Capua's crown jewel.
Reckless and unrestrained, she fiercely whipped the silent gladiators; she loathed being stared at, as it exposed her inner vulnerability. But when it came to training slaves, Agrippa was an expert. Perhaps you'd be interested in the twenty different whips in her bedroom—specially prepared for you.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
@3Built in the Second Samnite period, decorated in the exquisite Third Pompeian and Lemovician styles, this is a typical residence of the wealthy merchant class during the Roman era. Featuring two courtyards and four triclinia, @1@1
.....all reduced to ashes in the famous slave uprising... According to contemporaneous anecdotes, a massive pale spider was sealed beneath the ruins, referred to by locals as the "God of the Maze," and they firmly believe that those who tread upon the spider's territory will become disoriented among countless passages and entrances, ultimately becoming hosts for its offspring.
An interesting horror story, but it will not deter us @2@1
—Excerpt from "@2Archaeological Notes on the Ruins"

.....The situation is dire, Sir Goodman. Moretti and his party have been missing for several days now. We all understand this has nothing to do with the legend of the "God of the Maze," yet it is indeed unsettling. We have decided to end the investigation early and return to Durham.
Additionally, the archaeological team discovered a cluster of Silver-like objects in the ruins, which may be related to the @1 you mentioned earlier...
—Excerpt from Clarkson Johnson's "A Letter to Goodman"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Arithmetic: Zero points. Aurita even scored 2 points.
English: Failing. It took a long time to understand the difference between "shift" and "bullshit."
......
History: Failing. Claimed Caligula was the most outstanding Roman emperor because he built many arenas.

Danger Rating: C. Shows a degree of aggression when interacting with other Awakeners. Not a serious threat; no special attention needed.
Notes: This individual has almost no self-care abilities (another victim poisoned by slavery), requires constant attention, and must not be allowed to use snowflake cream as a dental cleaner again.
—Excerpt from A.F. 313, "Mythag Annual Student Profile."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Even when the gladiators burned the training ground and hung her parents' heads high on the wall, Agrippa couldn't believe it—how could these lowly, meek slaves have such audacity?
In Rome, there were two kinds of people: those who enslave and those who are enslaved.
Agrippa was fortunate. She was the daughter of the largest gladiator training ground owner in Capua. She roamed freely, fearing nothing, doing as she pleased.
But now, she's like a frightened rabbit, cowering in the basement, with the revelry outside seeming like Hell.
Slave rebellion. Such a foreign term. Agrippa is only fifteen; there's so much in this world she doesn't understand.
In the shadows, she sees a pair of red glowing eyes. It's Uvhash, the greatest money-maker in the training ground, locked underground due to its danger.
They never got along, and no one knows what they discussed that night. After the bloody night of flames, Agrippa was found dead in the dark depths, completely alone. She wasn't killed by slaves—someone drained her blood entirely.
Of course, her head was eventually severed too, hung next to her parents', facing Rome.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
It was... a dazzling daylight and heat wave, an ecstasy of indulgent revelry.
Cheers roared like waves, the warriors bathed in blood and glory in the arena.
The girl returned home reluctantly, still wielding her whip, mercilessly striking the dazed gladiator in front of her.

This was her battlefield.

There were no thrilling duels, no flowers or applause. The tall warriors were the gentlest reindeer; all her curses and fury dissolved into the dark void, leaving not even a whisper of wind.

profile.title: Aigis
profile.introduction: Petrifies enemies with her eye, rendering them unable to act and making them fragile.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Do not approach, do not touch, do not look directly, do not pity; there are beings in this world that are meant to dwell alone in the darkness.
Though it is not her intention, if you gaze affectionately into her eyes because of her frail figure, timid voice, or childlike clothing, you will fall into eternal stillness and silence.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
According to the "Cataclysm Event Record III," the Keeper brought back this piece of Silver Core from the Londinium Orphanage. Accompanying the Silver Core was a tiny, patch-covered teddy bear.
This Silver weighs about 2.1 grams, is slender and smooth, resembling a teardrop. Those who come into direct contact with the Silver will fall into a Stasis State, maintaining the posture of touching the Silver until Awakened by someone else. To prevent accidental contact, Researcher Doll kept this Silver in a closed glass orb and replaced the teddy bear's eyes.
"It looks as if it has been crying every day."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Jenkin pressed against the crack of the door to the counseling room, straining to listen.
Sylvia's voice was gentle and warm, while Aigis's sounded like a mosquito's whimper, intermittent and hard to decipher.
"Aigis, you need to learn to say no."
"But I... don't want to be a burden... I want to... repay..."
"Aigis hasn't been a burden to anyone. You see, Keeper allows you to move freely in school without any restrictions, isn't that a sign of trust?"
"You promised me not to casually agree to unreasonable requests from others. Especially from Caraboo, helping her with her confinement and cleaning will only cause trouble for our Keeper, okay?"
"Jenkin, how did you get in here?"
"J-Jenkin! I... I agreed to Caraboo myself! C-Calm down!"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
On a clear morning twelve years ago, in the eastern district of Londinium, a baby girl tightly wrapped in a scarf appeared at the door of the poorhouse. This was not uncommon in the paranoid eastern district, but what angered young Sarah was that, in the harsh winter, the heartless abandoner had also wrapped the baby girl's mouth and nose in the scarf. Moreover, this baby girl neither cried nor made a fuss; if not for those clear and bright eyes blinking a few times under the impact, Sarah would have almost thought she was dead.
Just as Sarah thought she would become another "sister" to a well-behaved child, the then-director sent this child, for the price of one Leo Fang, to the tailor of the eastern district market.
"We can't afford to raise that child. Becoming a tailor's daughter, although she will suffer a bit, at least she can learn a skill."
What can a child not even two months old know about "skills"?
Feeling her empty stomach, Sarah did not refute the director's decision.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Fearful of light, fearful of gaze, fearful of love, fearful of embrace.
Desiring light, desiring gaze, desiring love, desiring embrace.
If only I could avoid harming others, if only I wouldn't trouble others, if only I were as smart as Tiuog, if only I were as brave as Jenkin...
Then I wouldn't be scorched by the blazing sun, nor despised by others, right?
With unfounded worries, the doll cautiously reached out its pale, almost translucent hand from the shadow into the sunlight.

profile.title: Alva
profile.introduction: A veteran of the battlefield who excels at stacking large amounts of Shield and converting Shield into Blade for attacks.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses strong defense and single-target DMG capabilities; the higher the Shield, the greater the DMG dealt by {Precision Slash}.
After {Rouse}, all DMG dealt by Alva will enjoy Shield bonus.
{Combat Stance} has the ability to dispel Exalt Seals, and during Exalt, Symptoms or State Cards can be discarded to gain additional draws.
In this stage, Alva will be deployed at Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlightened Level 3, and enemies will receive a buff in their first round.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Alva is very satisfied with her prosthetic limb.
"After that battle, I never imagined I would stand again. It's an honor to have the chance to fight for justice once more."
As for the low moans coming from the dormitory at midnight, just consider them non-existent when the new sun rises above the horizon.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F.18@1/@1/@2 Infantry Regiment, the Kingdom of Remouahe, Port Alexandria
The situation in Port Alexandria is stabilizing. The 3rd and 4th battalions, along with three companies of musketeers, have crossed the canal to proceed to @4 for the next stage of operations. Now only the 1st battalion and a company of musketeers remain in Port Alexandria.
A squad encountered rebels during a patrol and quickly dealt with them. They brought back an interesting trinket that looks like silver but is heavier. I've stored it in the safe; the commander will like this gift.
.....
Day 13 of the garrison, soldiers from various companies are showing abnormal behavior, including but not limited to attacking comrades, officers, destroying inventory, and military equipment. These soldiers, along with the earlier madmen, are now under my direct supervision in confinement.
Their eyes are bloodshot, and they drool, looking more like pitiful animals than soldiers. Madness is spreading here; I'm worried it will become a plague.
.....
Day 30 of the garrison.
To date, 370 soldiers have shown mad behavior. After 20 days of severe hysteria, 331 of them developed limb numbness and paralysis. Their aggression remains strong; two military doctors were bitten while trying to treat them.
The madness continues to spread. I've reported the situation, hoping for swift action.
.....
The Committee took over the Alexandria garrison for 10 days; people still fall into madness, my brothers one after another. Why are only we suffering this damned plague? Why are the locals fine? Why do the rebels who hassle us remain unaffected?
My head hurts more and more, and the doctors are helpless—they fell into madness before me and became those biting monsters.
I fear I will also go mad.
They say we have to wait for the Mythag University people. Damn them, we fought for our country, we shouldn't be treated this way.
.....
I know now, I finally know. It's a vengeful spirit within that piece of silver, seeking revenge on all who invaded this land. That one-legged woman wants us to leave, wants us all gone, to return everything here to its original owners. Yes, yes, it should be this way, we never should've come, never started this war, I @1@3@4@2@1@2@3
— Excerpt from the "@2 Infantry Regiment 1st Battalion Acting Commander's Log"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Hannah: Ms. Alva, you've been at Mythag University for quite a while. You're very popular; some girls even handed me love letters for you! Want me to read them?
Alva: That's really... cough, I mean, thank you everyone? But wasn't I here to share my day?
Hannah: Didn't know the instructor had such a cute side! I might hide outside campus after this interview publishes, to avoid jealous girls! So, back to the point, can you share a day of the charming instructor's life?
Alva: I'm afraid you'll be disappointed; my day is ordinary. I get up at 4 AM for exercises to adapt my leg to activity.
At 6 AM I go to the cafeteria. Though I don't need to eat as an Awakener, it's a habit.
8-12 AM, I guide investigators in combat, sometimes extra training for Keepers. Afternoon training adjusts with the schedule, sometimes at 1 PM, sometimes at 3 PM.
Weekly, I go to Nautila for prosthetic tuning. Though she forgets things, she does good work; every maintenance lessens my pain.
Hannah: What do you do for fun?
Alva: Fun... an unfamiliar concept to me. Recently, I went to the beach with Aurita and the others; spending time with these innocent kids relaxes me. Swimming also benefits physical strength. Back when I was a recruit, my instructor—
Hannah: That's all for today! For more of Alva's stories, stay tuned for the next "Mythag Daily Share"!
—Excerpt from Mythag Daily's "Special Interview Column: Mythag Daily Share"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Her teenage years were spent in a military camp. 
Like all children raised in military camps, she firmly believes in the glory of the legion and regards it as her life. Her world is simple: what the commander acknowledges is right, and what the commander forbids is wrong. 
"Swing your sword towards the enemy; we will grant true freedom to this land; strike down the invading army; do not feel sympathy for them; current sacrifices are all for future stability and prosperity."
She trained under such slogans and entered the battlefield with them. After a desperate rescue mission, she received an injured leg and the title of a war hero. From then on, she retired with medals and became a new recruit instructor, completely unaware of how this injured leg would reveal a new world to her. 
Humbled by her injury, Alva angrily left the army. Once on the streets, she truly discovered how the commander's glorious speeches were nothing but lies—liberation was merely a pretext for invasion, and people in foreign lands suffered greatly. 
This was not her ideal nation; this was not a radiant future without blood and tears. 
Thus, she raised the blade of atonement against her former comrades and vowed to fight for true justice.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Alva had doubted her righteousness, but those bewildered days and nights brought her profound enlightenment.
Illusions are tangible.
The initial starting point never changed; the justice she fights for is not an organization turned aggressor, not an individual shouting slogans, but a genuine existence within her beliefs.

All life, all innocence, all dreams, aspirations, and beautiful yearnings can easily turn to ash under gunfire.
What is lost in war will never return, like hellish demons devouring all goodness.
Thus, those who start wars for selfish desires must also taste the ashes of destruction. There will be true heroes, with noble intentions, selflessly fighting for the bright future of all.
This is the value called justice.
......

Wrong, then correct it.
Make the sacrifices worthwhile, make this phantom pain of severed legs and limbs worthwhile.
If there is anything unfortunate destroyed, reclaim it with these new prosthetics.
Those small and fragile things will never lose their shine.
Everything is for that truly bloodless, tearless utopia.

profile.title: Aurita
profile.introduction: Continuously splits glands to perform low Arithmetica Cost attacks, and consumes all glands at once to launch a full-scale assault when necessary.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
In solitude, the ocean spirit of demise was welcomed, achieving a miraculous continuation on land.
Whether born cheerful or choosing to forget to escape pain, the straightforward innocence radiates immeasurable allure, compelling people to join her camp—perhaps this is her little strategy to expand the "Companion Team".

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
During the investigation along the southern coast of Cornwall, I discovered an abandoned village that does not appear on any map. After surveying, we confirmed it to be a village "erased from existence" due to the outbreak of the D-phenomenon.
In the village, we collected a large number of anomalous marine biological samples, including: carnivorous clams, heat-resistant ice sea angels, and most notably, "amphibious jellyfish" found everywhere.
In a former house that had become a jellyfish nest, we recovered a fluid Silver weighing approximately 11.85 drams. When attempting to remove this Silver from the site, the investigation team was attacked by aberrant jellyfish; fortunately, all the glands of these anomalous amphibious jellyfish had decayed, posing no neurotoxic threat.
— "A.F. Year 308, Third Field Investigation in Biology"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Don't expect much from the intellect of jellyfish; Aurita cannot comprehend what she has experienced. All she can affirm is that she once lived in that deserted village. Whenever past stories come up, she deflects the conversation by pretending to understand, so Aurita contributes little to the study of D-Phenomenon or clade disturbances.
However, as a rare harmless Awakener, Aurita is deeply loved by the staff and students of the awakening system, and the Czort leading the research in dissolution biology is particularly concerned about her.
By the way, she gained the ability for split proliferation after merging with the jellyfish's collective consciousness. Even if what proliferates are just ordinary jellyfish, Aurita excitedly holds up her "companions" to boast door-to-door—though she doesn't realize that each time she presents one, it's the same jellyfish.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
March
Welcome to the Atlantic Aquarium Tour! I'm your guide, Aurita. Watch your step when boarding the jellyfish cart!
The tanks display rare jellyfish from tropical seas, ta-da! Look—
April
What? What's going on... Where did everyone go... Director! Director! The jellyfish, Aurita's jellyfish, are all gone!
The aquarium never had jellyfish exhibits? But I remember...
May
With too few exhibits and no guests, we can't keep operating... Goodbye, everyone...
If, if we ever have another tour, please come find Aurita!
June
Aurita is so tired... Am I sick...
Ah... it's the jellyfish! Sorry, Aurita can't spread diatoms for you.
When Aurita gets better, we'll swim together again.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
A fishing village with exceptionally clear waters, its shores adorned like the Milky Way on a midsummer night, resounds with the cheerful laughter of caravans—those memories that no longer exist occasionally flash back, only to be quickly forgotten.
As long as there are companions constantly emerging by her side, new memories can always be created, and the jellyfish are always present—this is what Aurita believes.
After all, it is in their nature and destiny as jellyfish to thrive by drifting, multiplying, and overwhelming enemies with sheer numbers.

profile.title: Caecus
profile.introduction: Erects all body spines to counterattack invading enemies and uses their bloodline power to heal self.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Has powerful HP Healing and stacking {Counter} Ability, performing well against enemies with multi-hit attacks.
Exalt and {Protective Scales} can deal more {Counter} DMG.
In this level, Caecus will be brought into battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3. He will Obtain 10 Aliemus each turn, and the enemy will receive Enhance on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: He always wandered by the lakeside with a gloomy expression. Gradually, the students began to refer to the lakeside as "the Territory of the Sullen Half-Fish Man". Thus, he came every day like a lord, solemnly inspecting his domain. When he dove into the lake bottom, the swirling bubbles gently enveloped him; underwater, the jeers from the shore seemed to fade into obscurity.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 3@1, January.
On the Devil's Reef off the eastern coast of the Agló Republic, local fishermen reported they had salvaged "a strangely shaped corpse"—short and hunched, with protruding eyeballs, partially hardened skin, webbed fingers, and the most bizarre feature being symmetrical red marks on either side of the neck. If it weren't for the student uniform it wore, it would be hard to consider it a "human corpse." 
When the investigators arrived, the strange corpse had already been burned by frightened fishermen as an omen, leaving only a suitcase that was quite popular among students at the time. The suitcase contained no change of clothes, only some illegible manuscripts, a compass, and a photograph of a middle-aged woman.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Since the @2 incident, Caecus has reconciled with us and become an external member of the Awakening system. His understanding of @1 knowledge is merely superficial, yet he is eager to showcase and share it. 
 In front of people like Tulu, Miryam, and Goliath, he is filled with humility, but he casts a gloomy and unfriendly gaze toward other races—especially humans. Researchers who have read the files understand his paranoia, but some hot-tempered Awakeners cannot tolerate it. This tense atmosphere often requires Sylvia to mediate and diffuse the situation, and a trust relationship has gradually developed between Caecus and Sylvia. 
 Under Sylvia's guidance, Caecus has awakened the "tolerance of higher races," avoiding provocations from others without comment. 
 "That child often recalls his human mother with warmth. It is said that a mother is the benchmark for a child's understanding of the world, and his hostility toward humans is essentially acquired. So, he just lacks some guidance and care," Sylvia wrote in her psychological counseling records.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Caecus despised humans, even as a child. 
Once, he saved a drowning little girl but was accused by other children of being a monster; he tried to keep his distance, yet they chased him, shouting "stinky frogman." 

During those gloomy years, the only glimmer of hope for him came from Uncle Oren's letters. His vast knowledge contained endless tales of monsters that captivated young Caecus. However, the happiness was fleeting, as Uncle Oren inexplicably vanished a few years later.
At the age of 16, Caecus returned to visit his grandfather and accidentally discovered Oren's manuscript. He finally understood the noble meaning hidden within the twisted patterns on his body.
Hmph, humanity is ultimately a lowly species.
From then on, he no longer succumbed to pessimism but patiently waited for the day destiny would arrive.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Haven't you had such dreams? Suddenly inheriting a massive fortune, suddenly gaining immense power... enough to trample those who once disdained and hurt us.
Then, at the moment you wake up, faced with relentless reality, you fall deeper into despair.
But my dream is true, my fate has arrived.

Know this: you are the weak ones.

Can physical frailty and a wilting soul not uphold a noble bloodline? No, that's impossible, because the name Oren is our family's pride and symbol. Endowed with the bloodline and name, I am no longer that bullied freak; I am someone with noble blood.

So... please... stop looking at me with those suffocating eyes...

profile.title: Casiah
profile.introduction: Able to draw or replace a large number of cards as if performing magic, and gain Temporary Strength to enhance damage.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt can deal area DMG and draw cards; the more Command Cards, States, or Symptom Cards drawn, the greater the number of DMG instances dealt.
{Poof!} can reshuffle the hand while discarding State or Symptom Cards to gain Aliemus.
{Telekinesis} takes cards from {Ultra Space} in exchange for high DMG and Arithmetica resources.
In this stage, Casiah will be deployed at Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlighten Level 3, and the first attack from the boss enemy will receive an Enhance.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Let's dance! Dance with joy, dance with madness! Laugh with her and heat up the atmosphere to the max.
Then, it will be time for the main character to appear.
Under the spotlight, the mad magician reaches into her tall hat. What she pulls out from the endless void—colorful ribbons, a soaring white dove, or the claw of a terrifying monster—only she knows.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 3@2, April 1, Silver Storage and Management Room, Mythag University Awakener System, Londinium
Silver G411, along with Silver G410, was purchased by Lord Dexter from "Lady Narcissus." The individual measures 1.85 cm in length and 0.4 cm at its widest point, with a shape resembling a rhombicuboctahedron. Regardless of the angle or position from which it is observed, G411 emits bright yellow diamond-shaped ripples that radiate outward from the observer's visual center. Prolonged observation may cause the observer to experience mental disorientation, diminished emotional feedback, and even lead to Wooden Man Syndrome (potential symptoms include complete emotional loss, loss of desire, malnutrition, and even death due to lack of appetite), requiring timely psychological and emotional intervention.
Although its negative effects are milder compared to most Silvers, it is still recommended to store it in a sealed container and avoid direct viewing after removal to minimize the risk of emotional disturbances caused by G411.

—Excerpt from <Italic:"Mythag University Silver Storage Records.">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"Tired of the mundane park? Bored with dull animal exhibitions? Then come to Casiah's Magical Carnival! For kids who love animals, there are tenor-singing lions, ballet-dancing flamingos, acrobatic elephants, and the world's only donkey tamer! For couples looking to enhance their connection, try our unique spinning live horses for a rare romantic experience! For warriors lacking true excitement in life, challenge the mirror maze personally designed by the carnival master. If you manage to take away those 41 mischievous visitors who never came out, you can win great prizes!
Besides these exclusive entertainment facilities, Magician Casiah will also show you a world of marvelous festivities, bringing you body segmentation, human metamorphosis, extreme escapes, and more magic! Lucky audience members can even participate in the show on stage, experiencing Master Casiah's exquisite cutting techniques. It will redefine your perception, making your daily life seem much more mundane! Best of all, it's all free, free! Your joy is Master Casiah's best reward!
Mythag University folks, this weekend, on the field behind the cafeteria, Master Casiah will be waiting for you!

Note: Once entering the carnival, attendees must fully experience it; mid-way exits are not allowed. Those with low tolerance and fragile spirit or heart should enter cautiously.
The carnival is not responsible for any symptoms such as appetite loss, limb dismemberment, eye loss, or abdominal tearing that occur afterward."
—"The Grand Opening of Magician Casiah's Magical Carnival!"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
From her childhood, Casiah exhibited a high degree of mimicry talent.
No matter how intricate the dance, she could replicate it after just one viewing; no matter how complex the magic trick, she could master it after being taught just once. Voice, expression, even smile and temperament—Casiah could perfectly replicate them, even to the point of blurring the line between real and fake. If she had a different background or profession, perhaps she could have led a different life because of this talent.
Unfortunately, she was just a child growing up in an obscure circus. The circus people only thought of profiting from her talent, while those outside the circus—they only cared to purchase joy and had no intention of offering sympathy.
No one cared for Casiah's happiness, but fortunately, Casiah did not care either. Every time she looked at the laughing audience, at the arguing troupe members, at the fortune teller silently weeping over her photos, she only felt a detached curiosity: why did they make those expressions offstage? Why did they insist on performing when no one was watching? Why did they call it their "emotional outpouring"?
What is happiness? What is sadness? What did the "benefactor" who slit her throat say about the despair and fear of near-death? 
As a human, she could not find the answers to these questions, but as a child of magic, as a being capable of collecting "emotions" from others, perhaps she could finally gain an experience she had never possessed.
Thus, she founded the Magical Carnival and brought it around the world.
People come to purchase joy, but the price is all their desires.
"Come, come, come to my carnival! Just a tad bit of joy, a bit of sorrow, and you can see the most spectacular show of the century!"

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Casiah reached into the hat, and it was empty.
Casiah reached into the door, and it was empty.
Casiah reached into her chest, and it was empty.
The collected joys and sorrows, fears and angers, the warmth and coldness of humanity, the noise before the curtain, the cheers during the twists, the whistles at the highlights, the applause at the closing, and the indifference at dispersal all slipped away from this hollow chest, leaving no trace of ever existing.
Casiah imitated the expressions of the audience in the mirror—the wrinkles at the corners of the eyes, the flow of muscles, the curvature of lips—clearly identical to those people, yet devoid of any true meaning.
If only she could perceive... If only she could transcend imitation... If only she could become one of them... Or, if only someone could remain...
Then surely this "void" in her chest would be filled.

profile.title: Castor
profile.introduction: With wings that stir violent winds to shield his allies, Castor's Onyx Plumes cause lethal "Corrosion," inflicting massive HP loss upon enemies when they take DMG this turn.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
The unyielding lone bird, summoning storms with his wings to protect his own.
His descent scatters Onyx Plumes, each one carrying deadly {Corrosion}, dealing severe HP loss to enemies struck this turn.
In this stage, Castor and Jenkin (Lv. 50, Skill Lv. 5, Enlighten 3) will deploy. Battle start grants 100 Aliemus, plus 30 each turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
If born into an ordinary family, Castor might have grown into a modest young man as warm and steady as a tree. But now, he is the only one among the twins who retained a complete Body and Soul; he remains silent like a stone, waiting to bury that ember the two of them shared in their youth deep within, never giving up, never wavering.
Those already matured wings, capable of stirring up a storm, are now retracted in dormancy, as a bird quietly awaits the moment the cage door opens to once again soar in the sky.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
While I was on a mission far away from Londinium, on that barren plateau known for its poverty, I happened to discover an astonishing account in a mountain village that had only a few remaining residents.
On the night the monster appeared, it seemed as if the sky was shrouded by some strange shadow, fog rolling on the ground, while a dim green hue emerged from deep within the clouds, a phenomenon not just of nature, but a projection of a living, breathing entity.
Deep in the desolate mountains where tall trees stood, a roar, like a wave of fury, suddenly resonated—a howl from something that did not belong to any creature, stirring the deepest fears of instinct within people.
A massive shadow sped past, shaking the entire mountain along with its obscuring presence, and all the branches and leaves of the trees trembled, creating a synchronized whisper-like rustle.
"I saw it! Those long, dense tendrils, those eyes glowing green, that must be the source of that terrifying shadow above. It noticed me, even locked onto me! I truly thought my life would end at that moment, but perhaps it had just had its fill, and before I blacked out from suffocation, it mercifully turned away." 
This is the only interview with an official witness, and the villagers, still entrenched in nightmares, refuse to recall more details. However, from existing signs, this massive dark green monster, besides having demolished part of the mountains and leaving a few remnants of beast corpses with only strands of blood, did not show greater aggression, like a normal beast feeding. Perhaps its approach to the village stemmed solely from a curious peek.
But in the face of any unknown horror, we evidently need to prepare for the worst; I will continue my investigation on the @3 plateau for half a month, and if there are any other notable danger signals, I will include them in the mission report at any time.

——Excerpt from the Mythag Field Investigation Notes 19@2 Annual Record

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
The experience of interacting with Castor often reveals two strikingly contrasting extremes.
At first glance, his overly serious and composed demeanor closely resembles that of a middle-aged clerk who has spent twenty years working at Londinium Bank: rigid, unromantic, drained of all vitality by monotonous work. When he frowns, he indeed carries a saint-like presence—calm yet exuding authority without raising his voice.
However, upon deeper interaction, you'll discover that all his seemingly conservative behaviors stem from an extremely limited knowledge base and an unparalleled level of bluntness, to the point where he takes any joke directed at him completely seriously.
He often clutches a thick notebook, ceaselessly questioning the Keeper as if he were a toddler still learning to speak. Faced with such earnest and sincere gaze, most people find it difficult to express disdain for those naive questions.
Beyond scriptures related to various beliefs, Castor is insatiably curious about any new knowledge, especially regarding modern society and science; he has even developed a strong interest in experimental research. After being ruthlessly ignored by Doll: Inferno, he turned to relentlessly pursue poor Winkle, who tries to escape from him daily.
If he were in a school classroom, he would be the kind of diligent student adored by teachers—he does not see studying as a burden, but rather views the freedom to absorb interesting knowledge as a heartfelt joy.
However, while the teacher might feel content, some boastful Awakeners become infuriated when faced with his endless logical inquiries, unable to answer them, leaving behind a bewildered Castor reflecting on the profound difficulty of learning and the long road ahead.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
What is the price of pursuing freedom and escaping the church? The twins anticipated horrifying punishments, even the loss of their own lives, but they never expected that the most painful thing was never the physical torture, but the flaying of the soul.
Castor stared with bloodshot eyes as Pollux's treasured, lively and magnificent wings were brutally torn off. His own lips were bitten raw, and he waited in madness and guilt, craving for the same pain to befall him.
However, Juliette merely waved her hand, and the severely injured Castor was taken down like a broken sack, thrown into a pool filled with d-slime for the "sin-effacement rite."
The black, viscous slime soaked his injured body, seeping into every wound and covering every feather, making it even harder to breathe.
In the cycle of wounds repeatedly corroding and healing, the image of the torn-out wings spiraled in Castor's dazed mind. He sat in the small cage in agony, wishing he could bear all the torment for his brother. He wished he had been the one to have his wings pulled out that day, yet later he resolutely thought that he must use these wings to fly with his brother towards freedom again.
Castor endured day after day in this hellish pool; time became unperceivable through pain. It felt like waiting for dozens of years, waiting until Castor nearly died again, before he finally crawled out from that dark hell. The eager eyes he faced were met with a cold and disdainful gaze.
"Stop looking at me with that disgusting look! Let me give you another piece of advice, Lord Castor. You and I are merely serving as the Twin Divi of the Lightbearer together. Leave me alone unless you have a serious matter to discuss. It gets annoying, you know?"
Castor did not understand why his brother had completely forgotten him, but he finally realized the meaning of Juliette's deeply meaningful gaze when he left the pool of sin—because she now tightly held the collar that could control him, a guarantee of her absolute confidence.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Twins are born with nothing but each other, yet Castor continues to savor the taste of loss.
Being hurt, forgotten, and betrayed, that silent heart is not made of iron, but he keeps getting back up.
"But it's okay, I will hold on to your share too," Castor said, looking at his brother, who was disfigured, believing blindly.
In his heart, he tightly clutched that faint flame, tirelessly resisting the knife-like wind and snow.
One day, he will climb to the peak of freedom and use his wings to touch the sky's low-hanging curtain.
He believes that the end of that thorny path must be the bright daylight promised by his younger brother in childhood.

profile.title: Celeste
profile.introduction: A blessed maiden who guards allies from the brink of death, and uses the power of dreams to heal.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Comprehensive Arithmetica supply, Healing, and {Tentacle DMG} enhancement Assist Ability.
After Exalt, resurrect upon taking lethal DMG once per battle.
In this quest, a Level 50 Celeste with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten 3 will join, gaining 20 Aliemus each turn, and enemies are strengthened in the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Her ethereal beauty is always reminiscent of the bird of heaven, spreading its glossy wings towards utopia as her white dress fluttering in caressing breezes.
However, does the girl herself who perches on the mast ever imagine herself flying upon the azure sky?

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
On July 3, A.F.3@1, being invited by the Nathaniel Forbes Green Foundation, Mythag University participated in the investigation towards the "ghost ship" Sona-Nyl.
Upon boarding the barque which had been declared sunk ten years ago, the communication signal from investigator Fingal Johansen had immediately out of reach.
On July 10, A.F.3@1, the university eventually managed to get him. Located at the northern shore of Leo Heights with 300 nautical miles away from the original destination, investigator Johansen declared that he lost sense of time and space at the second he stepped on Sona-Nyl, and could not recall anything that happened onboard.
Investigator Johansen can hitherto only vaguely remember a word: Elysian.
Due to the unharmful nature of the ghost ship itself, Mythag University decide to terminate the investigation today (A.F.3@1 year, December 24).
"File: Investigation of the Sona-Nyl Crash."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Grabbed by Doll upon the moment of joining the university, Celeste has undergone a series of comprehensive assessment.
As an Awakener summoned from non-standard process, Celeste's mental state is incredibly stable. However, neither her spirit intensity nor her Gnostic index which evaluates the strength of slivercore meets the Awakener's standard.
Yet she still emerged into this world as an Awakener, like a flower with invisible petals brusts in abysmal nothingness.
Although no abnormalities has shown in daily activities or field missions, data record reveals Celeste's concerning situation: her will, already faint, continues to evansce.
By now, the Awakening Department is organizing a small order to sustain Celeste's existence by supplying religious belief. Those who are interested in joining and proficient in aquatic activities could contact Teacher Sylvia for further infomation.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
On the thirty-first day following the celestial bird, Sona-Nyl sails into a sea shrouded by streaming mist.
Celeste perches high on the mast, gazing upon her people below. Notes of the lutanist drift in sweet melodies. Songs praying for a smooth journey echo betwixt.

She does not aware of their destination, nor does she evaluate how much wealth and fame bestowing across the sea. She does not care whether the prayers are pious, nor does she unsettled by the rumors about her power, circulating surreptitiously around the sea. Being asked to bless the voyage, she does not need words to complete her duty.

And when the music ceases and the mist lifts, the sailors behold only a resistless raging sea. Lightning tears through the sky. Black rain pours down, bursting thunders of gigantic spray. Rushing torrent captures the fragile barque and throws it into the gaping abyss.

"God has forsaken us!" Kindreds scream in desperation. Bellows imprison her into the lightless dungeon.
Neglected and derelicted, the protector is left alone unquestioned with her failed duty, and one last lingering confusion.

Why... why did the barque sink?

The chain that once manacled her to the mast drown into the roaring splash. She plummets into the depth...
Like a white bird with snapped wings flaking from the sky.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Our good friend, the resourceful Hannah Peaslee, somehow dug up the research materials on Celeste. Thanks to this audacious reporter, both the originally confidential materials and her brief interview with Celeste has now spreaded into every corner.

After reading the confidential materials, Hannah posed a question to her usually emotionless face.

"Most Awakeners have unfulfilled wishes, which lie in their basic motivations for cooperating with the university. However, your wish section is blank in this material. Don't you have any desire to accomplish anything? Or what is your meaning as an Awakener?"

"I have, I do have."

The girl's expression showed no cracks, as ethereal and noble as usual. If it were not for that brief answer and the anxiety within, the reporter would have almost thought that she miss the question.


"I will find my wish... everyone wishes so. Therefore I will find it."

Her soft voice resounded, delicate and fragile like an easily shattered dream.

profile.title: Clementine
profile.introduction: Builds up Symbiosis, hypnotizes enemies to reduce their damage, or inflicts Trauma on enemies to increase the damage they take.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
The gentle and elegant psychologist can gradually sense the emotional buildup of the patients during communication, accumulating {Symbiosis}. After releasing the Exalt, they can consume {Symbiosis} to manipulate their spirit state, instilling {Fear} to dismantle the enemies' combat will, or implanting trauma to instantly {Destroy} their mental defenses.
In this level, Clementine will fight with level 50, skill level 5, and Enlighten 3, with Keyflare Regen increased by 50, gaining 20 Aliemus per round.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Everyone wears different masks of personality, and she chose the one that most numbs the heart —

She smiles gently, saying every expression and every word so soothing, making the patients who come for psychological counseling genuinely feel they have met their soulmate and guide in this life.
That is enough.

The doomed moth needs not to know the ending, only to follow her guidance.

In that seemingly warm redemption, the flames consume, no turning back.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
I do not believe Robert would commit suicide by gunshot. No, I do not believe it.

He received a medal for bravery and solved many difficult murder cases; he is a determined veteran agent. Most importantly, he was about to get married, and the girl loved him back, so there must be a conspiracy here! Someone murdered him and staged it as a suicide scene!

Whoever the murderer is, I will find him and make him pay. This is something I must do as a brother and an agent.

...

The scene had no clues, so I had to return to my hometown, which was the last place Robert visited before his suicide. After many inquiries, I learned that he had entered deep into the mountains.

As for why he went there, I suspect it has something to do with local traditions: the first child in every family is taken to the mountains during their coming-of-age ceremony.

Since my parents moved away from here when we were young, Robert never practiced this tradition. I think it was the sense of responsibility before starting a family that awakened his adherence to tradition.

Regardless, I need to take a trip to the deep mountains. After finishing this entry, I’ll set off immediately.

...

A grand structure was carved out in the mountains, I do not know which era it belongs to, I can't tell. I only know that cruel rituals have been continuously conducted here, with dismembered limbs and larvae... I can't write anymore.

...

These larvae... no, those are not my grandparents, not my ancestors... I cannot have the bloodline of a monster. Ah—

...

...I was discovered by the monsters conducting the rituals; they want to turn me into a monster too!

...

If someone reads this diary, kill me, immediately...@2

...

Praise the great @2, hiss @2... Praise blind folly @4... hssss... @1 hiss...

—Excerpt from the diary of the late involved agent @3, with omissions

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
<Thank you, Clementine, I feel much better. You really are the person who understands me best in this world.>

 When entering and exiting Clementine's counseling room, the Keeper often hears such voices. Regardless of how those patients and Awakeners were before they entered, they emerge light-footed and beaming with smiles.

 There was even a time when the Keeper saw Clementine providing psychological counseling to a stray cat that accidentally wandered into the consultation room.

 The results are apparent; everyone who has undergone counseling has praised Clementine, rapidly spreading her influence. However, this also brought troubles —

 "Good afternoon, Keeper."  

Clementine appeared with a smile on your path, and you weren't surprised; her continuously ringing communicator had already said it all. Those patients who trusted her unconditionally have become her eyes and ears, helping her achieve various goals, whether directly or indirectly.

 "I think that even if you wanted to become the mayor of this city, those who received counseling would unconditionally help you achieve that."  

Facing the Keeper's joke, Clementine smiled nonchalantly.

 "I'm not interested in any of that."  

Yes, Clementine is not interested in those things. Thankfully, she is not.

 Otherwise, the dark secrets poured out during psychological counseling would be enough to disturb this city.

 "Right now, I just want to explore the secrets deep within your heart, Keeper."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
In fact, the true Clementine had already died the moment the Passing Great Will became a parasite.

What broke free from that altar full of flesh and blood was a newborn with a great essence, far surpassing human life.

New life, old memories. The body nurtured by this planet, the soul born from the depths of the universe.

Such a contradictory existence puzzled Clementine, but soon, the hunger from the Soul left her unable to think.

Human food is so bland; only painful memories are somewhat palatable. And the more torment and despair those memories carry, the sweeter and more delicious they taste.

In that moment, she finally understood her way of survival.

"Speak to me, whether it be pain or despair, show it all to me."

She sat behind her desk, gazing maliciously at the patient who came for consultation, her expression filled with tenderness, enough to disarm anyone.

That was the disguise and mask of insect hunting.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
At first, some trivial pain could satisfy her. 

Then, her craving for sweetness led her to embrace more and more malice during psychological guidance. 

This malice surged like a river, flooding one after another island of the soul. 

But it was not enough. Far from it. 

Only with the awakening of that ancient long dream, will the entire world turn into a sea of pain, bringing ultimate joy. 

So, she was excited, she prayed, she waited. 

When everything merged into chaos, she would bow her head, joyfully savoring the most extreme lament of the world. 

Even if this sea of pain would drown her.

profile.title: Corposant
profile.introduction: Generates a large number of Tentacles and helps reshuffling your hand. The "Aftershock" effect unlocks greater potential.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
A Resilient Maiden, one who faces death unflinchingly can burst with limitless potential when confronted with the threat of death.
The {Pilot} of vessels, a Mareel Glow in the night sky, capable of generating numerous Tentacles while reconfiguring your hand to guide you to Victory with the {Aftershock} Effect.
In this stage, you will bring Corposant into battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3. Each Turn, draw 2 additional cards and Obtain 1 Tentacle.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
She is the flag fluttering high while the ship sails, guiding its direction, and also the dirge that holds its duty until the very last moment when the ship sinks.

Her responsibility, her duty, her life are bound to the ship; there is nothing else.

Because guiding the course is the meaning of her existence.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
No one knows where that ghost ship is, nor can anyone confirm whether "it" truly exists. The only clue I have is that ivory head sculpture, which seems to possess some sort of strange magic.

Ever since I acquired it, I've been having the same dream every day, yes, the dream of that ghost ship that I don't know where it is. Its hull burns with a glimmer that writhes like a living creature, seemingly leading to a gateway to dimensions unknown to mortals. Upon waking, I still remember those blasphemous and pathological whispers.

How I wish I were crazy or could forget these bizarre sounds that no human could possibly produce, but I cannot.

That damned Dream, that damned glimmer of intellect torments me, so I must find "it" under the guidance of the ivory head sculpture. Fortunately, the professors from Miskatonic will walk with me, together exploring the ghost ship and the St. Elmo's fire — that's what they call that writhing glimmer.

Wish me luck. May the Lord bless us.

——Excerpt from "Miskatonic's Supernatural Phenomena Investigation Archives"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Corposant's daily schedule is very fixed; you just need to be at a specific place at a specific time to find her.

Generally speaking, Corposant gets up at 6:00, takes a cold bath, and does exercises. 7:00 is breakfast time, and besides oatmeal and pickled fish, a large amount of caffeine supplements is also required.

From 9:00 to 12:00, it is Corposant's study time; she will revise nautical charts or calibrate equipment during this period. When the class bell rings, Corposant will come outdoors, waving a flag as a traffic officer, which greatly alleviates the road congestion during lunch.

Corposant's lunch time depends on the "traffic conditions, " but no later than 12: 30. After a short break, at 14:00, Corposant will appear at the seaside on time, observing wind direction and weather. At this point, attention should be paid to the movements of Corposant, Faros, and Celeste, to avoid them "commandeering" the museum model ships or luring passersby out to sea.

Dinner time is at 19:00. To numb her sleep cycle, completely destroyed by long-term sailing, Corposant needs to consume a large amount of alcohol and sleep-inducing tinctures. Only in this way can she successfully fall asleep at 22:00; otherwise, she will force herself to stay awake to deal with possible "accidents" during the voyage.

Consequently, situations resulting from this include, but are not limited to, the phenomenon of St. Elmo's fire going out of control, leading to the entire area shining like daylight, or abnormal, uncontrolled area voltage, machinery running erratically, fires, etc.

<Red:Note, during this time absolutely do not let Erica and Corposant come into contact with each other!>

Finally, a criticism notification:

After Corposant's arrival, Winkle made several attempts to extract and study @2 at Corposant's chest as a light source. A verbal criticism has been issued; if any abnormalities are found, be sure to inform the Keeper in time.

—Keeper %player%

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Before learning to speak, having grown up on a cruise ship, Corposant could already mimic the rhythm of waves crashing against the ship.

Sometimes the rhythm was "splash, " sometimes it was "boom, " like a lullaby sung by nature, making her feel secure, making her feel familiar, making her yearn.

She always understood the "mood" of the sea quicker than others, always read the "language" of the clouds quicker than others, and everyone said she was a natural "navigator."

In reality, when she first heard the term, young Corposant couldn't understand. She only instinctively wanted to help the captain and sailors who took her in, wanting them to return home safely, not to be lost to storms or disasters from other ships.

The navigator who understood her wish said, "Become a navigator, Corposant, become a navigator, fulfill your duty, and then your wish will come true."

The naive Corposant nodded, and thus, that navigator became her mentor. He taught her how to observe the stars, how to calibrate the sextant, and told her the ballads passed down through generations of navigators, those ominous ballads.

"I don't want to be embraced by saltwater, that's too cold, nor do I want to become phosphorescence." Young Corposant objected.

Her mentor didn't teach her anything further, just touched her head and smiled.

Because time will teach her the meaning of the "navigator's" duty—

She is the sole guide on the vast ocean, the only hope.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Until the last moment, Corposant was diligently fulfilling her duties, yet the ship she piloted still sank.

Every night, she dreams of those lost passengers: water flooding into the cabins, sea monsters' Tentacles rampaging, people drowning, jumping overboard, kneeling for one last guidance, but hope was lost.

This brutal reality reproaches her, tortures her—she has desecrated her profession.

For this, she must be punished, and the lightning that eternally entwines and torments her is the retribution. But it's not enough; she must atone for her negligence.

Fortunately, the great entity provided the answer: when the Divine Realm descends, the world will become paradise.

"So, to quell the eternal roaring of the sea, for the innocent buried at sea, for guiding the direction of all beings, I request the Divine Realm's arrival."

This is her last, final navigation.

profile.title: Daffodil
profile.introduction: A witch capable of customizing powerful Thousand Mirage illusions and placing them into Ultra Space.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Has DMG growth methods and powerful combo burst abilities.
Exalt can create perfect cards at any time.
In this level, you will bring "Daffodil" into battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3, gaining an additional 10 Aliemus per turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
"Welcome to Lady Daffodil's Exchange Shop, where we offer barter and the acquisition of heads, with absolutely fair prices, treating all customers equally and honestly."
Amidst the swirling gray mist, the woman gently brushed the ancient wooden pipe. The corners of her lips, sometimes hidden and sometimes revealed beneath the brim of her hat, outlined a smile full of ambiguity.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Daffodil sold herself to the Silver Research Laboratory of Mythag University at a high price.
Before that, the laboratory head Doll had repeatedly expressed strong dissatisfaction with the one-way communications that could only be initiated by Daffodil.
But only Daffodil and a certain deceased high-ranking member of Mythag University know how such a "one-way communication" was established through a dangerous transaction.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Daffodil's whereabouts at Mythag University are said to be elusive. After all, she wields taboo knowledge that allows her to traverse freely within dimensions, coming and going without being restricted by the underground Black Pool's Silver Hub.
She always sells some mysteriously sourced instruments or materials and sometimes assists other Awakeners in briefly escaping the control of Mythag University.
Later, through negotiations, she opened her own "Worry-Free Department" store in an old room beside the infirmary on campus.
Of course, all goods for sale must be registered and inspected by Mythag University.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
In a corner of the harbor city, there is a small exchange shop run by a "capable" individual skilled in handling mysterious events.
Not long after the flood, Daffodil, who was not yet known as Daffodil at the time, arrived near San Pedro. Victims of the "mysterious head disappearance" began to appear in this harbor city.
After conducting an investigation, the shopkeeper found it from that time and, after making every effort to subdue it, struck an exchange. The shopkeeper promised to take its head after its life ended, but until then, it must serve as the shopkeeper's assistant and could no longer hunt freely.
Thus, the one who had wandered for a thousand years began to act and live alongside the shopkeeper.
It revisited the memories of countless heads, experiencing joy and savoring hatred. But this absurd happiness was merely a distraction from its prolonged existence, masking the loneliness born of drifting.
Only while accompanying the shopkeeper did it form its first true friendship with a person. It realized that what it needed was not the false comfort of countless heads, but genuine connections with others to resolve its deep-rooted loneliness and emptiness.
However, this brief happiness lasted less than ten years. The shopkeeper, who never stopped investigating, passed away unexpectedly.
Thus, it severed her head, inheriting her appearance, shop, and name, becoming "Daffodil".
Since then, Daffodil embarked on a journey with her little exchange shop, still collecting heads as a hobby in exchange for helping those in need.
But she no longer hunted heads indiscriminately.
Never again.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
In the gray, viscous sea of primal matter, emptiness prevails; the concepts narrated by travelers crossing dimensions remain only in abstract descriptions and imaginations.
It wants to confirm with its own eyes the "places of longing" for humans, to personally witness the magnificent scenery of the mortal world, and to experience the bittersweet nuances of life and death.
Yet above all, it desires a specific person, someone worthy of cherishing, someone to trust.
A fair trade means equality, means division, means a transaction where nothing more than what is given is taken. But it yearns for something different, a kind of unconditional trust, to eliminate barriers, to break through tests, to dissolve the frozen barriers.
It gains and loses, but will ultimately regain what was lost.

profile.title: Doll
profile.introduction: Provides their team with perfected research results, granting Aliemus and restoring a large amount of HP.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Used flasks must be cleaned to look as good as new, tables burnt in experiments polished until they shine, and scales recalibrated every three days. Her fervor and respect for science and the mysteries of the world set high standards for everyone and everything around her.
Of course, these standards also apply to herself—after all, not everyone can calmly embrace death and willingly become an Awakener.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Doll is an indispensable figure at Mythag University and in the entire academic community. 
Among the Awakeners managed by the school, Doll is neither the most stable in gnostic index nor the strongest in combat abilities, but she is certainly the calmest and most rational one. 
For some Awakeners who were once human, the initial phase of "being reborn" is often accompanied by confusion, panic, and agitation. However, Doll seems unaffected by such "growing pains." 
On the second day after being awakened, she returned to the laboratory and compiled a technical manual that integrates outer domain knowledge and human wisdom within a year. 
Currently, Doll spends half her time innovating "silver chip technology" and the other half "cleaning up the mess for stupid students." 
— By the way, based on the influence of h-dim existence, Doll employed an unknown language in writing that "Technical Manual," and the translation work is being orderly conducted during her leisure time

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"Unforgiving Critiques", "Low-scoring Test Papers", "Substandard Research Reports"
During Doll's decades of teaching career, few students obtained certificates from her. Most students were driven away by her overly harsh attitude and turned to other professors. Over time, Professor Doll's notorious reputation spread among the students.
But there are still students who believe she uses these standards to select "disciples". Only those with the strongest will deserve to hold the key to open the door of truth and follow her to unveil the reality of the world.
Supported by such a belief, even though the possibility of graduating under Doll is minuscule, applications for mentorship under Doll still continuously flood her office.
Sometimes, Ramona, who was temporarily called to review the applications, couldn't help but think that if the students believed this way, it wouldn't be so bad.
In Ramona's view, she merely held the standard of awakeners to demand human students, nothing more

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Doll's joining is the only highlight of Mythag University in the eyes of the outside world.
Countless scholars lament that even a prodigy cannot escape the shackles of the mundane—she actually chose to be enrolled by an obscure university for wealth, tainted by the smell of copper.
While the outside world condemns her self-degradation, Doll has obtained something more precious than gold at Mythag University.
She has crossed the boundaries of material science, touching the veil of reality, beneath which lies something she would give her life to pursue.
Researching the dangers of "silver chip technology" is no less perilous than dancing on the edge of a blade, and Doll has become the most earnest, diligent, and excellent dancer.
When the D-mark appears, the scientific dancer does not cease her movement; instead, she spins within the limited time, either sedimenting or crystallizing her unique dance steps.
Until the D-phenomenon severely injures her physical body, spreading to her mind and eroding her rationality.
Even so, she remains far from the center of truth.
Thus, the flame of life has dimmed, leaving the brilliant star of knowledge with this farewell: "Awake me before the world forgets me."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
A new star of science and knowledge rises in an ordinary worker's family.
Most of Doll's childhood was filled with the sounds of cylinder throbs and gear operations.
But a love for machinery was not enough to support the girl in climbing the rugged and steep mountain of science.
What led her onto the path of knowledge was a plague.
An unknown madness raged through the city; hospitals were overcrowded, the morgue was overwhelmed, and everyone was on edge.
"The city's graveyards aren't enough—dig up grandma or burn mom, you have to choose one."
Thus, Doll watched her mother disappear into the flames.
Unlike others who were cremated, among the fragments and ashes she once called "mother," there flowed a shimmering silver mass.
"I want to know if I can see her again."
"I want to know if her kind soul is inside that."
"I want to know if that silver substance is the genesis of life, if it is the essence of the soul."
"I want to know if everything in this world has a common starting point."
Before getting the answers, Doll will not bow to the D-phenomenon.

profile.title: Doresain
profile.introduction: An elegant epicure. Deals devastating damage to foes with low HP. When devouring the corpses of departed enemies, he empowers himself and drains their lingering vitality.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
An elegant epicure who delivers fated demise to anguished souls.
He savors the remnants of the dead with gluttonous delight, converting their essence into HP restoration and unleashing devastating damage!
In this stage, you will enter battle with a Level 50 Doresain, Skill Level 5, Enlighten 3. Doresain's attacks are guaranteed to be critical hits in this battle.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
The founder of Necrovia, the eternal king of ghouls. 
His banquet is worthy of the deepest anticipation—grand dining hall, elegant dance, and those exquisite dishes handcrafted from corpses will surely be unforgettable for your entire life.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The abandoned cemetery in the northwest of London County has been listed as an absolute forbidden zone by local residents since AF 3@2.
According to archived reports from The Times and Scotland Yard's missing persons files, 37 people have gone missing in the thick night fog of the cemetery in recent years, including: Cyril, a habitual grave robber with a taste for noble tombs; Cook, a shivering vagrant seeking shelter; Officer Grayson, who stumbled in during patrol; and White, the Oxford scholar who studied how medieval bones were laid to rest.
All vanished when the fog grew thick, leaving not even a scrap of fabric for search teams to find.
Prior to AF 3@2, the MTC had always treated the area as barely worth noting in their ledgers.
That changed when the Duke of Derbyshire—Samuel Curzon, that notorious patron of unnatural studies—failed to return from his midnight visit.
Royal inquiries have a way of focusing attention, and soon a special operation was mounted.
In July of AF 3@2, Elias ThOrnn led twelve armed agents and three silver-equipped mediums into the cemetery's hungry mists.
Their anti-hypothermia medication hissed in its cases; their directional locators spun like dowsing rods in a haunted well.
After advancing precisely 1.2 kilometers in tight formation, they found it—a hexagonal entrance hidden beneath the shattered statue of the Ghoul King, its stone face worn smooth by centuries of bad weather and worse company.
Five thousand steps down (each counted twice by nervous lips) brought them to the temple.
Where they expected charnel houses and stacked bones, they found instead 800 square meters of impossible precision: floors flatter than mathematics, air cleaner than a surgical theater.
Only the altar broke the perfection—2.3 meters of granite carved with endless thorns, wreathed in black mist that clung like cobwebs to something moving inside.
There knelt the Duke, dressed for dinner but quite dead, showing no marks save those left by perfect preservation.
When the mist was disturbed, he came apart like sugar in tea—dissolving until even the mass spectrometers wept empty data.
Then the mist turned its attention to the living.
Every investigator heard the same voice—cultured, gentle, the sort that might recite poetry before the cutting begins:
"Please do not seek out Necrovia. Please do not disturb the King of the Ghouls during his meal."
"Consider this your final warning."
They awoke outside the rusted gates, equipment intact but all recordings curiously blank.
Later, the MTC chairman and Miskatonic's occult dean would visit, then quietly declare the matter closed.
The cemetery now only punishes those who come seeking secrets; mere wanderers get off with nothing worse than lost hours and strange dreams.
But sometimes, when the fog rolls in particularly thick, the locals swear they can hear something chewing below their feet.

——Investigation Report on the Abandoned Cemetery of Etherington

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
After leaving Necrovia, Doresain demonstrated remarkable adaptability within human society. This was inevitable; for him, human society was not a new beginning, but rather a return.
His speech, mannerisms, demeanor, and etiquette were flawless, maintaining the aura of a monarch while showing none of the arrogance typically festering in those who have held power too long.
Doresain does not avoid social interaction, yet he shows little interest in topics like politics, economics, or academia. The only thing drawing him to such occasions is food.
Beyond mere appreciation of cuisine, Doresain takes visceral pleasure in the kitchen process itself—from ingredient selection and preparation techniques to spice usage and heat control. His vast culinary knowledge and refined taste captivate everyone he converses with.
They come to realize all their previous decades of dining were nothing more than hurried survival, no different from beasts eating merely to stay alive. Even those unfortunate souls born without a sense of taste can experience food's artistry through his descriptions, achieving what he calls "a rebirth of flavor perception."
This revelation is inseparable from Doresain's impeccable manners—he understands with crystal clarity the differences between human and ghoul culinary preferences. He respects these species distinctions and has no desire to impose his own tastes on others.
"Some people always regard difference as heresy," he remarks, the ghost of a smile playing about his lips. "In my city-state, such behavior was considered profoundly inelegant. I celebrate individual differences. It is precisely through these variations that we discover food's greatest beauties."
Then the purple mist around him swirls as he adds, voice dropping to a intimate murmur: "But you are different, Keeper. As such a singular being in this world... I believe you, above all others, might experience unparalleled beauty in the act of eating, just as I do..."
As he speaks these words, Doresain's smile flickers through the violet haze like the grin of some elegant, patient serpent whispering in your ear, waiting to lead you out of—or perhaps into—a very particular Garden of Eden.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
When Doresain awoke in the sunless temple of Necrovia, pain slithered through him like a nest of awakened serpents. The poison's work was unfinished, its blades still carving hymns of agony from his abdomen outward, thorned and relentless. He'd known his father's wine was tainted—had seen the old king's trembling hands, the shadows whispering behind his eyes—yet he drank it anyway, smiling as the bitterness kissed his throat.
Clever. Kind. Unshakable. By every measure, Doresain had been the perfect heir. But in a city where the living called corpse-eating a sin, even princes were not spared the pyre. He'd tried to teach them—the poetry of decay, the sacrament of bones—but understanding was a bridge between worlds, and his family had burned it long ago.
He never expected to open his eyes again. Yet here he was, choking on a new torment. The black mist coiled around him, alive with teeth and hunger, peeling him apart layer by layer. It gnawed. It sculpted. His flesh regrew only to be stripped anew, a grotesque dance of devouring and rebirth. His scream was soundless, lost in the temple's hollow belly.
Then—a flavor. Unimaginable. Divine. Amid the rending and the howling dark, it bloomed on his tongue: the god of Necrovia was feasting on him, and the taste was ecstasy. Pain dissolved. Poison became ambrosia. The god's laughter echoed in his marrow, their suffering and rapture now one and the same.
For seven nights, the feast endured. When Doresain rose at last, the altar's chill still humming in his veins, he felt no rage, no sorrow. Only gratitude. His murderers had given him a gift—the exquisite last meal of his own flesh, the delirious joy of being known.
Beyond the temple, the full moon hung like a bone-white hook. Necrovia knelt in the dark, and its new king smiled.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
He had once believed that his entire life existed for the pursuit of ultimate deliciousness.
The duties of a crown prince, the estrangement from loved ones, the weight of moral teachings... He had cast all this pain aside in his relentless quest for culinary perfection.
From prince to monarch, from mortal to divine, his hunger never waned. He mastered every cooking technique, could detect the most fleeting nuance in every corpse's flavor profile.
He attained everything he desired, yet after each ephemeral moment of satisfaction, that hollow feeling remained - like a flavor just beyond his palate's reach.
