It wasn't until he consumed Celina's corpse and glimpsed her lingering soul that understanding came.
The stories etched in flesh before death's arrival, the unfinished yearnings that clung to bones - these were the missing ingredients he'd been chasing, the true essence he'd been tasting without realizing.
"Eating is remembrance; every feast a reunion between departed and living" —these are the words carved into Necrovia's throne. He will grant those who seek his protection remembrance and eternal rest.

profile.title: Erica
profile.introduction: Provides temporary Burst Strength or Alert, making them a great asset for both offense and defense.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Doll conducted extensive research on her but found no components in her torso, limbs, head, or pelvis that support vocalization. How she speaks remains one of Mythag's many mysteries.
In most cases, she communicates normally with others, but sometimes, the mysterious vocal component seems to have a mind of its own, uttering chilling and eerie words that send shivers down one's spine.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
On A.F. 297, @1, @1 03:47, an unexplained explosion occurred southeast of @3 Republic @1@2@1 town. The shockwave triggered a 3.8 magnitude earthquake in the area, noticeable shaking reached villages 5 kilometers away from @1@2@1 town.
After the incident, @1@2@1 town lost contact with other regions, and surrounding residents could not recall the town's location. Based on this, the Committee dispatched members for investigation, and dissolution material was detected at the edge of @1@2@1 town.
After sealing off the small town, Mythag University investigators performed dissolution body clearing operations and recovered the E-1929 Silver from a strongly dissolved area suspected to be the explosion's center.
E-1929 is particularly unique; its Gnostic index has been in an irregular fluctuation state for an extended period and emits a ticking sound like gears, only maintaining brief stability when linked with mechanical fragments recovered from the strong dissolution area. Based on this characteristic, the Mythag University Awakening Department wrapped E-1929 in mechanical fragments and quickly awakened it once the Gnostic Index tended to stabilize.
—<Italic:"E-1929 Silver Acquisition and Awakening Report Summary">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Yesterday at 6 PM, room CB-101, located in the basement of the Prince Building at Mythag University, was damaged by unknown individuals. The reinforced door was severely damaged, and there were black lightning-like scorch marks on both the interior and exterior walls, leaving the scene in disarray.
"Instantaneous super strong currents can lead to such markings, " Dr. Adams from the Electrical Engineering department commented on the scorch marks on the wall, "This requires an enormous voltage. If it were an ordinary device, it would be quite large and hard to conceal. But if it were silver... anything is possible."
Given that silver is under strict supervision within the awakening department, it is hard to imagine someone could use silver privately without detection, thus more attention turns to the Awakeners who possess silver energy.
"I would say, it was definitely her," a student from the awakening department who wished to remain anonymous told the reporter. When asked specifically who it was, he appeared somewhat nervous, "It's 'her', the one known as 'Tick-Tock'! Haven't you heard the continuous current sound from her body? Whenever I think of electricity, I think of her, no doubt about it." 
Many students expressed similar suspicions. However, this Awakener, who acted like a mechanical puppet with a strange smile, was not without support; several investigators publicly backed Erica and urged everyone to focus on facts and evidence, warning against causing harm to others due to personal biases.
In response, Dr. Sokolova, the deputy head of the awakening department, declined to comment. The incident is still under further investigation.
— Excerpt from "Mythag Daily", "Initial Investigation of the CB-101 Room Explosion Incident"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Hannah: In your view, is there any difference between the automaton you once were and the you now?
Erica: The former me retained "emotions." 
Hannah: What kind of emotions specifically, if you are willing to share?
Erica: I'm sorry, I don't understand your question.
Hannah: Uh, that is, if you feel this question is too personal, you don't have to answer.
Erica: I cannot perceive "willingness or not," I'm very sorry.
Hannah: Then... could you tell me what your emotions were at that time?
Erica: The corporeal me survived the explosion: happiness, information.
Realizing my spirit was transferred and preserved onto the automaton: nervousness, excitement.
Noticing consciousness gradually slipping out of control and a strong desire for destruction arising: nervousness, fear, protection.
Hannah: Protection?
Erica: I'm very sorry, detailed information has been lost.
Hannah: Then let's change the question; this is something our readers wish to know as well. Why do you not have emotions now? What happened?
Erica: For unknown reasons, I was unable to restore sanity through the spirit link. The university took necessary measures to suppress all emotions with Silver devices, reducing aggressiveness and danger while retaining intelligence and utility.
—Excerpt from "Mythag Daily," "Awakeners' Faculty Interview: Tick-Tock Person"

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Hannah: Do you remember what happened before your disassembly?
Erica: The automaton's behavior began to spiral out of control, consciousness experienced fractures, and during those fractures, it would destroy everything. Everything would perish beneath the currents and flames.
Hannah: Erica?
Erica: ...
Hannah: I can't be that lucky to encounter the "Tick-Tock Effect," right... Erica?
Erica: The head component is operational again; I apologize for the inconvenience earlier.
As I just mentioned, the automaton recognized its increasing threat to humans, and because it still regarded itself as human at that time, it was in great pain.
Hannah: You mentioned protection before; does that mean protecting humans?
Erica: Yes, the automaton hoped to protect.
Hannah: Then why didn't you tell me that just now?
Erica: ...
Hannah: Do I need to change the question?
Erica: No. I am no longer burdened by emotions and can answer.
Upon realizing its own danger, the automaton attempted to short-circuit its body before the consciousness fragmentation to minimize self-inflicted damage.
In the end, the protection failed, and the human friend died.
The automaton felt sadness, regret, and despair.
The automaton self-destructed to ensure it would cause no more destruction, and then the Silver was recovered by Mythag University, creating me, Erica, body number EA307881.
—Excerpt from a recording by Hannah Piszley titled "0711, Tick-Tock Person Exclusive Interview"

profile.title: Faint
profile.introduction: Applies high Death Resistance and leeches the rival's Strength to defend against attacks.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt can provide permanent {Death Resistance} to your team.
After triggering {Death Resistance}, both DEF and Healing will be enhanced.
{Rouse} and Command Cards can both obtain significant {Counter} and weaken enemies' {STR}, providing an advantage against multiple enemies and multiple hits.
In this level, Faint will join the battle at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten 3. Each turn, she will gain 30% {Embryo Fusion} and 10 Aliemus. Enemies will receive Enhance in the first two turns.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Faint is a faded, hard-to-hatch seed.
People pity her for her flaws and love her for her weakness.
Knowing she chose to remain in the shell, now turned to dust, star dust.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
In June of the 282nd year of the Alluvial Era, following an investigation application by the Mystia Treaty Committee, we dispatched a team of investigators to the New Leo area.
Since the meteor crash event a year ago, the abnormal mortality rate in the region has remained high.
Additionally, investigators discovered several mutated plants at the crash site. Instrumental analysis confirmed that these plants contained high levels of radiation, likely brought by the meteor.
However, no extraterrestrial minerals were found in the crater. Villagers testified that, on the third day after the meteor fell, a group of scientists in white coats cut and removed the meteor.
(Note: Suspected to belong to a civilian scientific organization; their identity remains unclear.)
After coordination between the Examination Committee and the local government, all residents were relocated, and the area was officially declared a no-living zone.
According to Mythag University's Physics Laboratory, the living ban must remain in effect for at least 80 years.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Faint saw the student secretly crying in the laboratory.
This wasn't her favorite student, but it was the one who liked her the most.
The female student never minded Faint's resistance and aloofness, nor was she afraid of Faint's uncontrollable abilities.
She would spend her living expenses on buying all the sweets, just because she was worried that Faint didn't have enough energy; she would wear the clunky protective suit just to comb Faint's tangled hair.
Faint thought... to repay her.
So, Faint did what she could.
—If she was just worried about failing exams and cried, then threatening others to give up their grades would be enough, wouldn't it?

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Among these sisters, one of them should never have been born.
On their home planet, a now desolate planet in what earthlings would call the Lyra constellation, Faint and Tinct spent half their lives exchanging bitter words.
But who is the unwanted child?
Faint, the older sister, is powerful yet uncontrollable; the life force she seizes seems to flow away like a river, leaving her only able to barely survive by licking the remaining dewdrops.
Tinct, the younger sister, is beautiful yet struggles to be independent; her life is as stubborn as a floating weed, yet rooted nowhere, causing her to revolve around her sister.
Perhaps they both believe they are the one who should not exist. It's just that Faint made her decision earlier—threatening self-termination, she forced her sister onto the journey of departure.
"Leave, Tinct. Don't let me hold you back."
"You shall sing among the stars, becoming the most eye-catching color."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Do I have the right to have wishes?
If so, can I become a part of my sister?
To make her whole, to make us whole.
That way, I can express "love" without reservation, and she can embrace "love" without distraction.
Dear Tinct... this is what I, as the firstborn child, should do for you, right?

profile.title: Faros
profile.introduction: Infects enemies with Viperous Venom-imbued Tentacles to humiliate them and uses their devoted faith to grant their team Cards, indicating the path forward.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt and {Rouse} provides quick card shuffling, improving your team's margin for error.
{Deep Currents} makes Tentacles inflict {Poison} on all enemies with each attack. Combine this with {Ocean of Elation} to inflict a large amount of {Poison} on enemies at once.
In this stage, you will enter battle with Faros at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten 3. The enemy will gain Enhance on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Beware, siren's mesmerizing charm lurks under her seemingly calm and rational words. Never show curiousity about the maybe different appearance beneath her malformed hemlet; never forget her long-lasting dissatisfaction with that humanoid body.
Her gleaming light once lured moths to the molten warmth. Her burning fire once guided the lost to their doom. But now, only confusion flickers in her lighthouse, overlooking the disorienting sea.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The past three years have seen a large number of shipwreck incidents in the Monge Triangle sea area, accompanied by several cases of totally missing.
According to a sailor's testimony, a "woman with a glowing head" has been witnessed on the deck of the victim vessel during the rescue operation.
The field investigators rushed to the Monge Triangle sea area, but his ship had lost contact after arrivial. When the reinforcements finally found the spearhead ship, it had already lost power, drifting by the current towards the center of the Monge Triangle. All crew members were severely dehydrated and feeble. Massive hallucinations with acute intensity have been reported.
During the medical examination of the crew, a Silvercore was found among Investigator Ivors' personal belongings. The investigator himself consistently denied any contact between the ship and any Awakener.
The Silvercore was later sealed by the Awakener Department, numbered 6291-S-S-01, scheduled to use as teaching materials for pre-investigators in A.F. 315.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Like a real lighthouse, most of the time her presence is nothing more than a steady glow at a corner, peaceful, isolated. Her words only illuminates when confusion stirs the air, while her guidances clearer than nautical navigation.
Only when she meets with other Aequor visitors does the light reveal her shadow, twisting under paranoid madness.
In contrast to her serene and intellectual appearance, Faros has unexpectedly profound knowledge in ocean voyages. Despite the unresolved suspicions of abducting and sacrificing sailors, she has assisted the university in revising numerous nautical documents under the Keeper's supervision.
According to feedback from sailors of cooperative vessels, Faros's revisions, though effective, often (though not clear whether she is intentionally or not) steer the course towards the Monge Triangle.
The university has then prohibited Faros from accessing any ocean-related materials.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
The radiance of devotion illuminates her pilgrimage. A glorious ascension for the most devout follower of her true lord. A golden promise for the most loyal subject of her king.
It collapses. The Divine Realm is a sinking lie, a fortress without foundation, a vine drifting away from its soil. Her dolor billows. Her desolation turmoils. The remaining faith in the Divine Realm cannot keeps Faros afloat anymore. The poor wanderer eventually drowned.
Diving deep into the sea, her love and hatred agitates into swelling waves. The water whirls and spumes and eddies, splendor as the ceremony fireworks in her memory, a festival almost like a solace.
Her final torched gaze before being beheaded by the hero, must have been cling to the Divine Realm she couldn't forgo.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Return to her home, trace back to her origin, reunion with her lost dreamshe doesn't care how does her wish reflect in people's words. Even her body has altered to an Awakener, tides of her homeland still clamor in her soul.
The breath of sea-bottom volcanoes dissipated from her ears. The ocean's dreams writhing with ecstasies obscured from her eyes. Only her obsession with the Divine Realm still gleaming, more glorious than ever.
Mind her docility, a mere temporarily disguise. Her king's throne calls to her, like a lighthouse reaches through the aeons; her sacrifice will always be everything, like a wick's devotion to the light.

profile.title: Doll: Inferno
profile.introduction: Provides healing and a large amount of Aliemus to your team. In boss battles, it activates the Engine of Destruction to end its enemies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
A skilled researcher who provides reliable battlefield support, capable of both strengthening allies and weakening enemies while generating large amounts of Aliemus.
During boss fights, she gradually builds power by accumulating {Finale} stacks, eventually unleashing the devastating {Illusion's End} attack. Once activated, she becomes more aggressive until the battle concludes.
For this stage, you have access to Doll: Inferno and Nymphaea, both set at Level 50 with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten 3. At turn start, Doll: Inferno automatically gains 50 additional Aliemus and 1 stack of {Finale}.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Genius and madness are separated by a fine line. Once the brightest genius of Mythag, her downfall was like a collapsing building, leaving only the most deranged fiend crawling out from the ruins of reason.
She routinely donned the cloak of science, pouring life, humanity, love, and destruction into the crucible, concocting a poison to annihilate the world.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Before all the truths were revealed, no one suspected that Doll was the true mastermind behind those shocking events.
After all, her presence in Mythag and the Dissolution Front had always been like a pillar and a beacon, an unwavering support for everyone.
When countless stars in the human night sky were dissolved and scattered, this unshakable beacon of humanity was cruelly extinguished, becoming a force in the shadows aligned with darkness.
Tornadoes, floods, earthquakes... These inefficient killings clearly did not align with a genius's plan; she used her undiminished intellect to transform the Silver chip technology she once took pride in into a deadly weapon to "reshape the world," delivering waves of destruction to unseen corners of Mythag.
When she was finally captured, the doll's body could no longer be precisely controlled by her corroded brain. Her once calm face was now filled with a twisted and eerie expression, as if meeting her gaze would drag you into the abyss of madness.
Everyone unanimously chose to ignore her occasional vague mutterings, even though they might reveal the truth about the world.
Because her story once again proved that humans ultimately need meaning to survive.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Due to the fact that the Genesis Awakener, formerly known as the "Brain in a jar," has a history of breaking free from the S355 restrainer, it has been relocated by Dean Czort and placed in a new special dissolution restrainer.
This restrainer is located deep within the Black Pool. To ensure that there are no other instruments or items within a 30-meter radius of the restrainer, the Black Pool monitoring team has arranged for at least one patrol every 60 minutes.
During monitoring, it has been noted that this Awakener remains conscious and almost never engages in sleep activities. The Awakener once attempted to narrate to the patrol members about its previous illegal Silver chip modification experiments, and a team member fell into madness after brief communication.
Currently, all patrol teams consist of three members, who are strictly prohibited from making contact or conversing with the Awakener and must wear soundproof earmuffs throughout the patrol.
......
The confidentiality restriction level of this Awakener has reached the highest level of Awakener management. No one informed of the details is allowed to disclose information about this Awakener inside or outside of Mythag, and without the dean's approval and personal escort, visits are prohibited.
— Excerpted from "Mythag Black Pool Monitoring Team Report Records"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
As someone at the forefront of Dissolution research, Doll has long been acutely aware of the encroaching footsteps of annihilation steadily pursuing her from behind. Moreover, she is all too familiar with the concept of Dissolution itself. 
Through her relentless yet often fruitless pursuit of truth, Doll gradually comes to realize that human bodies may possess an inherent blindness—those bound to reality can never perceive the scenery of the other shore, no matter how hard they try. 
So, as the black sludge dissolves the body, will it also guide the soul to the true other shore? 
She sits silently alongside the ancient tablets' maxims, allowing Dissolution to erode her mind. Regardless of what lies at the end of this path, she can no longer turn back. 
Fortunate yet unfortunate, she has wagered her very existence for the chance to enter the inner side of the world. In an instant, reality, illusion, and destruction simultaneously engulf her. 
In the blink of an eye, she witnesses everything in the universe—the essence of Dissolution, the truth of the world—all that she has yearned for in life is here. 
All scientific beliefs and rational convictions dissolve into bubbles, and the once-unshakable spiritual barrier crumbles abruptly. 
The genius known as Doll has vanished; what remains now is merely a vessel occupied by nothingness.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
All meaning dissipates like sand, the world's false curtain fades in her eyes, and everything returns to the most primordial darkness.  
In this final performance on the pitch-black stage, the dancer of science still dances with all her might.  
She no longer cares about being slowly devoured by dissolution, as the ending of the play was written from the very beginning.  
As in the past, she will unreservedly dedicate the power of genius to the world in a manner closest to the truth.  
If you ask her gently, she will insist that she still holds love for humanity and the world; she wants to end all suffering in the world and bring people back to their true, original home.

profile.title: Helot: Catena
profile.introduction: Uses unique techniques to combo enemies, finishing off rivals with Bleed damage.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Flexibly use the chain to strike enemies, dealing DMG while applying a large amount of {Bleed}.
Pre-accumulate hand cards and look for opportunities to release Exalt to gain powerful Buffs, and use the {Devour} effect to reduce the Arithmetica Cost of all your Cards.
In this level, you will bring Helot: Catena at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and with 3 Enlighten. The initial HP is 40%, and at the end of each turn, you will gain 50 Aliemus and 100% {Embryo Fusion} Level.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Since the day she entered the prison, her memories have been stained with blood. 
The once mundane and warm recollections now feel like false dreams, blurred and cracked, turning into sharp Fragments embedded in her Caro. 
She can no longer recall the gentle embrace or caring faces; only eternal hatred and Question remain in her heart. 
Pain, humiliation, Betrayal... What caused all this suffering? 
She asks herself daily, but until freedom arrives, no one answers. 
So, she decides to seek the answers herself, interrogating every dirty heart torn from their screaming chests.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
I seem to be involved in some supernatural event, recording all my findings here...
We were finishing our archaeology at Evrotas when I woke up and found the rest of the team had vanished, and the town looked like it reverted to a thousand years ago overnight.
Ancient ruins had risen, restoring the town to its original state, including countless residents turned to skeletons.
Excitement, shock, and fear gripped my mind. Despite having seen countless skeletons, I tremble at the sight before me. Anyone experiencing this would be even more terrified.
Everyone in this town seemed to have been brutally massacred by one person. Skeletons lay in the streets, in houses. The town gate is inexplicably tightly closed, and bodies trying to escape but brutally killed piled up by the gate, forming a small hill of bones.
I found no exceptions, men, women, old people, children... so many were killed by a massive blunt weapon, their bodies or heads smashed. Staring at the dry bones, I almost saw the blood flowing.
What happened here?
...
I searched aimlessly, found no clues until I entered the prison.
The sight there was particularly gruesome, the perpetrator destroyed the entire prison in great anger. The walls had twisted, brownish words, seeming to be written in blood by someone dying, I carefully distinguished, it seemed to read "evil spirit".
Evil spirit? Does this refer to the killer, or did the town truly experience some supernatural event?
Indeed, slaughtering the entire town alone is unimaginable, only a true ghost could ruthlessly take so many lives.
After leaving the prison, the town seemed to have subtly changed, I could smell a faint bloody scent.
While I sought a way out, the sudden sound of chains echoed through the town, as if someone was dragging something heavy forward.
Evil spirit! It's the evil spirit... it's after me! I ran desperately, but the horrifying chain sound followed me everywhere.
I was exhausted, hid under a table in a home, locked the door, but the chain sound still chased me. I heard it coming closer...
I wish this were just a dream, that everything would disappear when I wake up, wait... the sound seems to be gone, maybe the spirit has left?
...
Researcher Note: The normal handwriting ends here, only three twisted words remain, roughly deciphered as:
The door opened
—Excerpt from a deceased archaeologist's diary in the Evrotas Archaeological Team

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Those who meet her for the first time have no desire to approach or communicate, as their survival instincts fervently warn them to stay away.
She is completely mad, or rather, a wild beast through and through.
If given the choice again, she might prefer to be a beast rather than endure everything as a human.
She trusts no one, and when her scarlet eyes gaze upon you, what you see is not suspicion but indifference and murderous intent.
She has long since scorned suspicion, seeing all people as equally base.
If you need advice on dealing with her, do not attempt to tame a true beast, for it is anything but docile. At the slightest opportunity, her claws and fangs will pierce your throat.
Leave her presence before she focuses on you, and pray that her next prey is not you.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
She grew up into a kind and gentle individual under the care and guidance of that lady, resembling a true noblewoman. However, due to an act of kindness towards a slave, she was ruthlessly abandoned by her "parents." 
The Spartans never accepted her, and all her kin harbored deep-seated hatred toward her. 
In an instant, she became a betrayer in everyone's eyes, losing her sense of self. 
In the prison, a strong malice emanated from everyone, as if it were a contagious disease, even though they had never met. 
The torment in the dark seemed eternal, and she became neurotically fearful of anyone approaching, unable to sleep each day, finding solace only when fainting, allowing her to close her eyes in peace; yet upon waking, she plunged back into endless nightmares. 
What exactly did she do wrong? How did things come to this? 
Her resentment found no outlet, unable to comprehend the logic of fate. 
Was it because she was guilty? 
But if she were guilty, then the guilt of everyone else would be tenfold. 
The accumulated hatred transformed into bitterness, endless anger burning through her veins day and night, until one day it finally burned down the cage that trapped her. 
The chains that had entwined her for years now wrapped around the necks of those who shouted, and the chains had long since melded with her body; they were truly a handy weapon. She gripped the blood-stained chains to pursue each filthy soul. 
Red, red, warm red, stinking red. 
Look, she knew it all along; no one's soul is pure white.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Her life's experiences seemed crafted to bring her suffering. Kindness, tolerance, compassion, trust—all the tender aspects of her humanity were torn away, discarded along with gory flesh. 
The word "forgiveness" was absent from her vocabulary; only the blood and hatred of others could resolve her own. 
Blood for blood, the fairest law revered by both warriors and slaves. 
A volcano that had simmered for a thousand years erupted in one night, and a hand crawled out of the underworld onto human soil. 
It was a vengeful spirit, seeking to flood the world with rivers of crimson.

profile.title: Ramona: Timeworn
profile.introduction: Draws strength from past commands, then unleashes a Predetermined Strike to deal massive single-instance damage.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
She calculated the speed of her sword, the angle to avoid attacks.
She calculated the feasibility of escape plans and the probability of the world moving toward the future.
Until the very end, she never stopped calculating.
Just as she never stopped walking this desolate path.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
■ File Number: AF315-C01
■ Awakener Codename: @2 Walker
This Awakener was first observed in A.F. 315, @1.
During the initial "Artificial Domain" experiment, influenced by the psychic resonance of the homogenous object, the "Artificial Domain" based on the Spirit Realm of our university's @2-level student Ramona Dexter unexpectedly broke through dimensional boundaries.
At that time, the Keeper confirmed the existence of the world outside dimensions for the first time in that "Artificial Domain" and also confirmed how @2's power would affect Ramona Dexter.
Thereafter, researches on the "Genesis Awakeners" was officially launched by the Awakener Sciences Research Institute.
Meanwhile, the planning of the teaching plan for new Power Control course for Ramona Dexter is also on the agenda.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"Ramona" shows great restraint in appearing. As she mentioned, she only manifests in this world when called by the Keeper.
She always comes and goes swiftly, seemingly not wanting to engage further with our world beyond battle.
Is it fear of the nightmares that plague her? Or fear of longing for the things she has lost... We cannot know.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
She had an enviable birth: an orphan raised as the sole heir—how fortunate.
She had remarkable wisdom: speaking at one, debating at two, beginning her enlightenment at three.
She had admirable talent: a photographic memory and mastery of the sword. At an incredibly young age, she passed Mythag University's rigorous preparatory courses with full marks.
She had companions she could rely on, with whom she could venture into darkness and the unknown.
<Italic:She had...>

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
When occupying Ramona's body, "Ramona" has never walked through the campus.
Lotan's boldness, Ogier's shy loyalty, William's calm composure, Doll's strict seriousness... No one remembers these moments more than she does.
But she always kept in mind: these beautiful things no longer belong to her.
Now, she needs to focus entirely on protecting another destined person... This is also to protect another precarious world and another innocent version of herself.

profile.title: Goliath
profile.introduction: Swings a heavy blade with the power of a great blade to slay enemies. Their Tentacle damage and Strength make them even more powerful.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
{Blade's Dominion} and {Decapitation Damage} have high DMG and multiple {STR} buffs, capable of dealing massive DMG to single and group enemies.
{Decapitation Damage} can also return Arithmetica Cost when attacking low-HP enemies or causing a kill.
Exalt can choose to {Usurp} or {Dormancy}, selecting the timing for growth or burst to deal more Damage.
In this level, Goliath will be brought into battle at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlighten.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: He is but the giant. His silhouette aweing, his power indestructible. His weapon, a colossal blade that no one else can lift. His victories, gleaming like oiled fur on the blade's fang, nourished by countless wars and savage  sacrifices. His trophies, a mountain of heads severed from his enemies and kins, crowned by his own on the top.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
On April 2, A.F.3@2, a temple remain in the Ashdod region was vandalized.
As the only one who had university education, I, a mere visitor, was invited by the regional administrator to participate in the architectural assessment of the ruins. Haha, I should never let them know that my "university" isn't quite what it seems.
On the afternoon of April 3, I entered the center of the temple. Ancient Semitics used it as a place of worship, and their pagan belief once flourished among the pillars before lost in history.
Yet what appeared before me was a sculpture that could be mistaken by no one. I clearly knew that the folds on its clothes should indicate the texture of striped linen.
However, its both hands were broken, its head severed from the body, and its lower limbs shattered on the threshold. Mucilage covered everywhere like the trace of a sticky slug. Upon inspection, there were few peculiar scratches buried within, accompanied by a giant, webbed footprint.
The pagan belief may still circulate nearby, but a formal investigation is not urgently necessary. I will extend my stay in the Ashdod region.
Fin@1@2sen, "An account of a trip around five cities of the Philistines"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Goliath is tyrannical and ruthless, but his power is undeniable. According to Miss Erica's battle records, his brutal brawn can no doubt gurantee his advantage even in isolated and vicious situations. I believe that Goliath does not care about the level of his partner's ability just because no one can hold a candle to him in his world.
However, everytime Goliath faces Miryam, fear always seeps from his eyes uncontrollably. It's hard to imagine what kind of existence would evoke such awe from this despotic ruler and brutal former-king.
Additionally, he has shown unusual concern for Miss Murphy. Sometimes he was witnessed to wander around the university's surrounding maze, maybe waiting for Murphy to return from her mission. Sometimes he would distantly observe Murphy's condition, seemingly worrying about possible injuries during her tasks.
Miss Peaslee also notices this. Once she said to me half-jokingly, "Hey, don't you think he's trying to be an ordinary father?"
"Observation record from an intern researcher"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
A scar jagged across his neck. A declaration of death for his people, while a coronation of majestic power for him.
When exile marks his failure, bitterness drown his breath; when despration hollows for surrender, and tides clamor for sacrificehe prays to the God for the power. His transcendent power.
Thus the God's apostle accepts his prayer, severing his head as an offering.
Thus the God's apostle acknowledges his prayer, emplacing the remnants of immortal on his exposed cervical spine.
Thus the God's apostle answers his prayer, leading him into the sanctum of eternity.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Beheading death's cold embrace, he survived and transformed. The glitter on his blade fades. The mask of apostle conceals his savage expression.
He did revere instinctively when that miracle manifested. But no god could capture his fear, and no cult could earn his faith.
A giant should not yield to others. A warrior should not lower his head.
His past already wreathed in dust, his war still longing for final triumph. The desire for conquest run through his veins, etching into bones like a sigil bearing the bestowed power. Perhaps, one day, death will end his dominion. Perhaps, one day, he will rise to the sole supremacy.

profile.title: Hameln
profile.introduction: His Rondino cards deal damage and allow your cards to take effect multiple times, while also generating a large amount of "Insight".
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Hameln elegantly manipulates notes to reduce card Arithmetica Costs and allow them to take effect multiple times.
His performance delivers continuous {Pierce DMG}, weakening the enemy's will and dismantling their defenses.
In this trial, try Lv. 50, Skill Lv. 5, Enlighten 3 Hameln, who gains 35 additional Aliemus per turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
At first glance, Hameln always reminded you of a kind of Eastern porcelain—white, luxurious, with graceful curves and a wave-like gentle sheen, yet somewhat cold to the touch.
Beneath that respectable, meticulous exterior lies a soul of extreme, empty purity. His entire life revolved around a single thought, like a machine programmed with one command, devoid of all other responses.
To him, everything was merely a sequence of musical notes.
Play, play, endlessly play.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 31@1, @1, @2, a famous tycoon who loved collecting music was found dead in his study by his butler late at night. There were no visible wounds or signs of poisoning on his body, and a strangely satisfied smile lingered on his lips.
Following extensive examination by forensic experts, the death was ruled an accidental one, as the room was locked with no way to get inside.
However, doctors could not provide a reasonable explanation for why this robust tycoon, who was in good health and even enjoyed horseback riding, suddenly died on such an ordinary and quiet night. Under pressure from his family, the police handed over the case to the Committee.
Initially intending to quietly resolve the matter, the Committee unexpectedly detected traces of Gnosis on scattered sheet music found on the tycoon's desk. The eerie sheet music was sent to Mythag posthaste, where research identified it as fragments of the lost second half of the famous "Requiem in A Minor." The final page of the score remained incomplete, with numerous frantic drafts scattered around, seemingly attempts by the tycoon to complete it.
Scholars at Mythag speculated that this attempt to complete the score caused the tycoon's descent into madness and eventual death, completing the awakening ceremony in an unconventional manner and prompting its immediate sealing.
For a long time, Mythag found no evidence of this unknown Awakener appearing or causing harm, causing the case to gradually fade from investigators' minds and into obscurity.
Only scholars in the field of music continued to yearn for the sealed half-sheet, hoping for the legendary composer to emerge.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Hameln did not always appear before students. Even though he was willing to attend Mythag's musical events and guide the less adept students, most of the time, he played alone in a soundproof room or in secluded woods.
Except for official events, he called all of his playing simply "practice," as if the practice was endless, and the perfect performance would never arrive.
 People praised his work, "Such a diligent and humble master," they sung, intoxicated by its melody.
Yet, Hameln never knew humility; his words were always straightforward and overly harsh.
Such an obsessive pursuit of perfection in music made his classes a primary source of stress for students, earning him the title the "devil's teacher."
If, for some reason, you wished to get his attention, simply play the wrong note, and his frowning expression would be upon you almost immediately.
He bore no grudge against those he "tormented," merely imposing his passionate, impossibly perfect standard upon them, even if it was much milder compared to what he demanded of himself.
He was like a horse with a carrot dangling overhead, forever striving for somethung unattainable.
Except for pausing for the elegant dancer, he rarely initiated contact with other Awakeners. However, he always displayed a surprising patience with requests and initiating conversation.
Perhaps it wasn't indifference that hindered him, but too narrow a mind.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Talent seemed to be a rare favor of destiny. Hameln had never witnessed it in anyone around him during his long adolescence. It became a vague image—a symbol driving him forward under his father's whip.
He firmly believed that relentless diligence was the endless ladder to the perfection just out of reach. Even when he stood at the pinnacle of the world stage, it remained an unchanging truth.
This was until he met someone that could stand beside him.
"What a dazzling genius!" they cried: this gift of so-called talent was not within him, but another.
From festering envy and jealousy, he suddenly felt an indescribable confusion and helplessness.
Without such talent, can I still reach the perfect end?
The rungs of the ladder of diligence had suddenly turned to ashes, while the door to perfection remained ever distant.
He often lingered outside the crowds that swarmed this genius, deliberately aloof.
But whenever he looked in that direction, he always silently rehearsed his greetings.
Those mediocre insects always surrounded them, murmuring malicious whispers.
He was far too clumsy, silent for so long without being able to argue.
Words meant little, he thought. Let music be his medium—for only music is the perfect language; surely they would understand.
However, fate was a fickle beast, and would always come to reclaim the gifts it had so generously given.
Geniuses die young, and yet the mediocre still stand.
In the past, provocations and scheming had never shaken him in the slightest.
Yet at this moment, looking at the stack of unsent, unsatisfactory music scores, he could only shed tears of anger.
Hameln suddenly understood: before reaching this perfect kingdom of heaven, he must use all means available in world below.
Fortunately, compared to those mediocre people, he already possessed the intellect to do it.
After all, in his eyes, everything was nothing more than a calculation, like notes arranged on a score.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
His life was monotonous and dull, with loneliness as a contant companion, alongside feelings of resentment, confusion and hatred that had long been digested since before he could even remember.
Now, he has become one with music, transcending mere passion—unavoidable, inescapable.
All that remains to him is music.
Keep moving upward; do not look back at those nine hundred and ninety-nine steps behind.
After all, he has already touched the ultimate melody, the destined flute.
Accept this endless flow of music, and let it saturate your soul and dreams.
For he has long since used his own soul as an instrument to resonate with yours.

profile.title: Helot
profile.introduction: An avenger who continuously restrains and builds up Strength, delivering a single devastating strike to bring an End to the enemy.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt significantly boosts "Strike" DMG in this turn.
The more "Strike" cards you have in hand, the higher the DMG of {Shattering Strike} will be, and the greater the {STR} bonus it enjoys. Combine "Strike" with Exalt to deal massive DMG in one go.
In this level, Helot will join the battle at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlightenments. At the start of the Battle, you will Obtain 3 "Strike" cards, gain 30% {Embryo Fusion} each turn, 10 Aliemus, and 6 {STR}. The enemy will gain Enhance on their first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: She was not born to drink blood. Once, she also basked in fleeting warmth, like a blossoming flower. Betrayal and imprisonment fueled the flames of rebellion. The fire of vengeance scorched Maysia. Consumed by endless hatred, her heart is now closed to everyone...

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The Evrotas archaeological team has been plagued by a mysterious illness for ten days, with no signs of improvement. 
The illness strikes without warning but with overwhelming force. Patients typically suffer from severe phantasms, accompanied by loss of bodily sensation and sensory deficits, which worsen over time, eventually leading to paralysis of one side or even the entire body. Although the contagion appears to be confined to the archaeological site on the west bank of the Evrotas River, with most patients being workers and resident scholars, authorities cannot rule out the possibility of further spread. 
A worker who had previously worked at the archaeological site told reporters that symptoms of the illness emerged fifteen days ago—on the very day the ancient shackles were unearthed. Readers familiar with the related archaeological news will undoubtedly recall the well-preserved, rust-free shackles that clearly belonged to an ancient civilization. They arrived in the Kingdom of Leo yesterday and have been handed over to Mythag University for further research. The connection between the illness and the shackles carries a mysterious significance. 
"Ever since we dug it up, something has felt off at the camp," the worker told reporters. "Many people have been having strange dreams, dreaming of a girl locked in a cage by chains, and some at midnight... This is a curse—the curse of the shackles! There can be no other explanation." 
As of the report date, relevant departments have taken control of the area, strictly prohibiting any personnel from entering or leaving. The Evrotas archaeological operation has now been completely suspended. 
—"The Ancient Curse? Strange Illness Sweeps the Evrotas Archaeological Team."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Helot is a sharp, cold blade.
Awakeners who have worked with her say she's an effective weapon, strong and always mission-ready. Once given a kill order, no matter how powerful the enemy, she will break through and take their head.
But that's all.
She might protect her team, but it's just following orders, not out of care. She doesn't trust them; they can't trust her to guard their backs.
What they don't know is that to her, turning a blade on allies is despicable, contemptible, and deserving of punishment.
She knows the evil of betrayal and would never turn her sword against comrades.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
The girl is dressed in a simple dress, her skin still stings from the rough washing earlier. She doesn't understand why soldiers brought her here; the noble master's house is no place for slaves to come and go freely, and she is far from the age to serve here.
The lady in the snowy white gown sits upright in the chair, and the girl lowers her head, trembling from fear and nervousness.
"I wasn't wrong after all..." the lady says, "So much like, really so like."
Like? Does that mean she resembles someone?
"Very good, very well." The lady claps her hands happily, "It's you. From today on, I will be your mother, do you remember?"
Mother? But she already has a mother. Her mother is at home, crying out loud when she was taken away.
What will happen to her mother?
Confusion and misunderstanding swirl in the girl's mind; she wants to ask for answers but also understands that the master's words are commands for slaves, and a slave that disobeys commands will be severely punished.
"I remember, Madam."
"What a good child. Let me think... I'll call you Helot, does that name please you, Helot? You must like it very much. Come, put on this dress and show it to mommy."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
The lady high above took her hand, promising to give her all the love in the world.
She believed in the lady's gentleness and the master's indulgence, trusted every smile and every hug, unaware that in their eyes, she was merely a pet—something to be discarded the moment it posed any threat.
They personally threw her into a cage, shattering her trust. But neither the warden's whip nor the torment from her kin hurt as much as the betrayal from her loved ones.
Hate, such hate! She hated them for their lies that won her genuine heart, hated them for using her life to create this painful farce. If trusting others led to this, then she would forever rely only on herself.
Her trust belongs solely to herself.

profile.title: Horla
profile.introduction: Different Emotions provide powerful buffs and alter the effect of her Exalt.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Horla's 4 Psalm cards feature distinct effects, Trigger a {Leap} to enter one of four emotional states: {Happiness}, {Anger}, {Grief}, or {Fear}.
Each state grants unique bonuses to all Awakeners, with Horla's Exalt receiving enhanced effects.
In this trial, try out Level 50 Horla and Lotan with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten Level 3. The enemy will gain enhancements on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Rose's spike pierces the poetry page, leaving transparent bloodstains. The scent of alcohol fills the air, and colorless flames spread everywhere.
In that blaze, Horla holds the last poem, silently marking the end of the concealed genius poet's path.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
At the beginning of A.F. 3@2, during the renovation of the city named Cité, an anomaly was discovered in a fire-damaged garden: ashes climbed the burnt walls of the greenhouse like vines, and the ash roses blooming on the vines shimmered with a metallic hue.
Following the trail of the vines, an investigator from Mythag University retrieved this Silver. The moment she touched the silver powder, her fingertips were scorched black without any pain, but tears inexplicably rolled down her cheeks.
The weeping came without reason and dissipated into the ashes in an instant.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
It seems that Horla possesses a peculiar magic, drawing teachers and students who yearn for poetry and beauty to her side involuntarily.
Horla doesn't like to publish her poems, but the students of Mythag still manage to catch glimpses of her verses. The lines flowing from her pen often sweep across the campus in no time.
As the morning dew rises, someone softly recites her lines under the bell tower; at dusk, Nymphaea sits on a bench in Myda Square, flipping through her collection; after nightfall, students gather in the auditorium to rehearse the chapters she wrote.
However, even when graciously invited, Horla rarely pays attention to these activities, let alone participate in them personally. The mournful tone of those words and tearful occasions often leave her feeling lost.
"Have I spread new sorrow again?"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
This is a city of flowers and poetry, where a lingering love poem is equivalent to a freshly cut rose and can be exchanged for a week's food for a family of three. The emergence of a masterpiece also signifies that a talented poet will rise to fame.
That person used to be a gardener who spent her days with fertilizer and dead branches, relishing the flattery and admiration brought by elegant verses. She no longer tended to gardening matters, even locking the greenhouse and forbidding anyone from entering the flower room.
When asked by others, she would only reply that there was "Inspiration" hidden in the greenhouse.
They were born insignificant, the children of noble retainers. With the establishment of the Constante Federation, the old nobles were purged, and they lost their place to stay.
That was a maid who grew up accompanying the young lady but showed her talent for writing early on — but how could a servant read poetry?
Surrounded by an ever-changing environment, the talent for poetry could not provide the girl with survival capabilities. Everything made the girl even more out of place and more melancholic and sad.
Until the gardener extended her hand — that hand, cut by thorns, bestowed upon her a garden. In this tiny kingdom, they spent the happiest time of their lives.
The peaceful life began to change with a poem received from the gardener.
Her friend's talent was so moving that she wanted more people to see this poem dedicated to a friend.
The shy and melancholic friend was unwilling to sign, so the gardener signed her name in the signature place — "If published, it would also be convenient for them to send reward money."
The gardener's change also started from the first draft fee.
She could not understand why she suddenly went from protector to burden.
She could not understand why her friend could effortlessly gain all the love and praise, while her own hard work was defenseless before talent.
Blinded by desire and jealousy, she began to use her friend's trust to deceive her creations. The deceitful behavior could not be turned back. The seed was buried in the gardener's heart from the beginning, and as the reputation of the talented poet spread, it began to sprout, blooming into a bud during a street interview — the fervent fans asked the gardener questions about those secret and lingering emotions in the poems, which the gardener could not read and was soaked in sweat, hurriedly leaving the scene. Returning to the flower room late at night, faced with her friend's concerned gaze, the panicking flowers bloomed at that moment.
After a fire shocking Cité, the talented poet announced her retreat, disappearing with the wealth and fame she earned in a short time.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
"Horla, from today onward, I will treasure all your poems. In the future, I'll trade my flowers for your poems!"
"Colette, you will definitely come back to save me...right?"

profile.title: Jenkin
profile.introduction: With a high Crit. Rate and Crit. Damage, Brown and the swarm of rats are her key allies in fighting multiple enemies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
{Mice Assemble} consumes Arithmetica to generate {Swarm Impact}. The more Arithmetica consumed, the higher the number of hits, allowing for high DMG by saving Arithmetica.
Every time you play "{Get 'em! Brown!}," both {Get 'em! Brown!} and {Swarm Impact} deal increased Damage.
Exalt and {Rouse} can increase Jenkin's Crit. Rate and Crit. DMG, further enhancing her DMG output.
In this stage, bring a level 50 Jenkin with Skill level 5 and Enlighten 3, receiving 1 {Insight} per turn, while the enemy is strengthened on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Everyone quickly accepted a fact: where there's Jenkin, there's Brown, and where there's Brown, there's Jenkin. And if Brown acts alone, don't doubt it—Jenkin is behind it.
"What do you mean I stole it? I have an alibi!"
The girl said, holding the item "taken" by Brown.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 315, @2, @1, 08:13 a.m..
The third team of the committee's on-site response and members of the Dissolution Item Control and Management (DISM) β7 group arrived at Corroded Area L-E-933 (formerly 2 Southwark Street, Rogers Wax Museum) to take over the task from the first team and DISM α2 group, conducting area neutralization work.
09:24 a.m..
DISM member James experienced symptoms of headache, nausea, and dizziness while clearing the wax museum's underground area.
10:47 a.m..
Third team member Roland, DISM members Zachary and Bolton fled the underground area. They reported being engulfed by darkness into a starry void, where tangible colors intertwined, both beautiful and chilling. These colors then formed countless mice that attacked them.
Bolton refused to continue and returned to the committee's Londinium office. Third team member Roland and DISM Zachary accompanied third team leader Fisher back into the underground area for further inspection.
13:03 a.m..
After a search, third team leader Fisher found and recovered Silver L3202 from the remaining corroded wax in the underground area. It has been handed over to Mythag University for further processing.
—<Italic:"Neutralization Report of Corroded Area L-E-933">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Jenkin's nimble hands once made people worry she'd become a regular feature in the Mythag University Daily—not as an interviewee, but as a prime suspect in thefts.
Though she did become a regular, it wasn't due to old habits—just enough cupcakes can make even a street survival expert enjoy a peaceful retirement with her rat buddy.
The real reason she frequented the paper? The editor paid too well.
Shh—keep it down, you never know where she might be, ready to trade info with Hannah for more sweets and cakes

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
"I'm not an orphan," said six-year-old Jenkin. "My mom just went to work. She'll pick me up after she's done."
She never wavered, nor did she dare to. This belief was her protective charm, waking her from nightmares filled with black slime. It was her only comfort whenever she tried to recall her mother's face but could only see a black shadow.
Through spring, summer, autumn, and winter, she stood by the welfare home's gate. Only on the morning the home disbanded did she wipe away her invisible tears and leave with her mouse friend.
"I am an orphan," said ten-year-old Jenkin. "I don't have parents. I only have Brown."
She told this to herself and the mouse perched on her shoulder. It was true—people might be wary of children suddenly approaching; East End street children were infamous throughout Londinium—but no one suspected mice scurrying by their feet. Through this tacit cooperation, the girl occasionally enjoyed a full meal and dreamed of fireplaces and pastries in the sewers.
But the cold winter spared no one, bringing illness and high fever, with death standing resolutely beside her. The girl lay dying, while mice scurried anxiously around her, hoping for any attention at all.
A young woman stopped and wrapped the girl warmly in her embrace.
"I'm not an orphan," said thirteen-year-old Jenkin. "I have my sister Mason and my good friend Brown!"
The girl stood before an old house, holding the mouse close, her face bright with a radiant smile.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Street life is not difficult for her.
When hungry, she looks for work; if there are no jobs, she makes a living with her own hands. Her coordination with Brown is so tacit that they can sustain a life of food and drink without anyone else's help.
"We don't need anyone," she swore with her mouse partner, "we only need each other." 
But every snowy night, she cannot help but glance at those windows lit with dim yellow lights, watching those who embrace each other by the meager dinner, blessing one another.
She truly does not need a concealed roof, does not require luxurious high beds and soft pillows; she indeed has trustworthy partners, but she is still curious what it would feel like to have a home for a long time?
This question was answered when Mason adopted her.

profile.title: Karen
profile.introduction: Provides your team with a large amount of Arithmetica, restores HP with Cuisine, and uses Creepy Cuisine to poison and kill enemies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses powerful HP Healing and flexible Arithmetica supply ability.
Both {Toad Stew} and {Silent Fealty} have {Retain}. Each {Retain} turn will obtain different growth.
Exalt, {Toad Stew}, and {Marvelous Cuisine} can all inflict {Poison} on all enemies.
In this level, Karen will be deployed at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlighten. The enemy's first Attack will receive Enhance.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
"I want to wear purple crystals as black as wine." 
"I want to don a luxurious gown made of ostrich feathers." 
"I want to be the only light at the banquet." 
The maid blinked her amber eyes. She had many more beautiful wishes, but she could no longer voice them. Before her frozen body loomed an endless green flame, silently blooming in the dark night.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 1@2/@1/@1 10:31 AM, the police received a report of multiple bloodstains discovered within @1@1 Manor. Detective Belford immediately headed to the scene, began an investigation, and found an unidentified bluish-green slime within the bloodstains.
13:44, Detective Belford submitted the report, and the Mystia Treaty Committee promptly took over the case.
14:25, the seventh response team of the committee arrived at the manor, controlled the related personnel, and sealed off the site. Based on the situation at the scene, the team leader speculated that there were at least five victims within the manor, and multiple instances of D-phenomenon were observed.
18:00, investigators from Mythag University arrived at the scene, confirmed the existence of the D-phenomenon, and entered the manor with the Awakener for cleanup and investigation.
23:11, the cleanup of the manor was completed. Investigators discovered a large number of mystical documents and ritual materials in the basement and study's secret room, and found an incomplete Awakener. The Awakener identified herself as "Maid Karen," the chief maid of @3 Duke. When discovered, the Awakener was dressed in a maid's uniform, adorned with various styles of jewelry, and was working very hard to clean the basement. This Awakener had a human form, bluish-green skin, and semi-transparent adhesive-like extremities, suspected to be an incomplete or erroneously summoned product. The slime secreted from her matched in texture and color with the substance found on the severed limbs.
The investigators questioned her, and Maid Karen admitted to @1 facts and expressed that everything was to gain her master's attention. Upon learning that @3 Duke would be arrested, she murmured, "Then where is my new master?" and then disassembled into gel, leaving behind a Silver.
01:37, the team leader of the seventh response team interrogated the related personnel and detained @1@1 Manor's owner, @3 Duke, according to the "Treaty." 
03:09, investigators returned to Mythag University and logged the K-033 Silver into the database.
—"Incident Report of @1@1 Manor"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Uniforms must be steamed and ironed, quilts must be hand washed, the fireplace must be spotless in summer and always have a fire burning in winter. Wooden floors must gleam, bookshelves must be dust-free, and black tea must always be kept at the right temperature for easy enjoyment.
For her noble, special, and unique new master—the Keeper who has given her life anew—she can do anything. She is the best maid in the world and must certainly be able to complete every task perfectly, refining every detail.
Her master's praise always makes her extraordinarily happy, but it's not enough, still not enough. What can she do to gain more attention from her master? What can she do to make herself more eye-catching?
"It must be the lack of earrings, necklaces, bracelets, rings, and hair ornaments!"She thrust the broom into the ground and muttered to herself, "As long as I have shiny jewelry decorations, Karen will surely become cuter, and everyone will enjoy looking at Karen even more!"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Soft black hair, full cheeks, eyes as bright as gemstones, and rosy lips—from childhood, Karen had always enjoyed special favor due to her natural beauty. By the time she reached adulthood, the gifts and flowers bestowed upon her by men daily could pile into a small mountain at her doorstep. Thus, she was not at all surprised when the palace steward announced she would be brought to the royal court to be trained as a noble handmaiden to the King. Was this not only right? She was born to be envied, born to be extraordinary, and born to be the center of attention.

Yet, beauty was the most common currency within the palace walls. No matter how breathtaking a flower from a small town might be, it appeared ordinary and rustic in such a place. Her appearance could no longer buy her special treatment; even the opportunity she craved most—to serve in the Great Hall during the Centennial Feast—was snatched away by another girl.

Why? Whatever that girl possessed, Karen possessed as well. Why was it not her?

Then, her eyes fell upon the silver ornaments hanging from the other girl’s ears, and she understood.
On the day of the banquet, she drowned the girl in the lake and donned the silver-white earrings herself. Just as she had envisioned, she became the most radiant among the handmaidens, even earning the privilege of serving at the King’s side. The gazes of envy and jealousy from those around her filled her with an exquisite thrill—yes, yes, this was how it was meant to be. This was the happiest day of her life.

Shortly thereafter, she met her end within the emerald mist that swallowed the entire royal city.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Ruby earrings, sapphire necklaces, beautiful hair accessories woven from garnet and malachite... How dazzling, how beautiful, how full of charm they are! Wherever they appear, people flock to them.
These jewelry pendants are not mere "decorative items," no, they are the true stars of the world. Noble ladies wearing turquoise necklaces are immortalized in songs by poets; elderly ladies adorned with emeralds are seen as symbols of nobility. Even those lowborn girls, with a cheap bracelet on their wrist, can earn the endearing title of "Pearl Girl," attracting countless gazes.
Even dry branches and putrid bones can become objects of everyone's desire when adorned with a string of beautiful jewels.
If she could wear those necklaces and earrings, those bracelets and headpieces, surely she would become the center of attention as well.

profile.title: Kathigu-Ra
profile.introduction: The blazing sun that burns everything with scorching flames, consuming more Arithmetica to greatly enhance damage output.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Kathigu-Ra, {The Living Flame}, engulfs all opposition in fire.
Lingering {Fiamma} converts to {Combust} stacks, causing unstoppable eruptions at threshold.
In this stage, Kathigu-Ra will enter battle with Level 50, Skill level 5, Enlighten 3, gaining 5 Arithmetica, 30 Aliemus, and 50 {STR} per turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Kathigu-ra excels at using her signature smile to draw out the cruel and crazy chill from the deepest reaches of awareness in all observers.
Those two inseparable twin guns, with the powder dust swirling around the barrels, warn all those ill-intentioned petty criminals that the sacrifice of the nameless is the cornerstone of all greatness.
However, anyone who has dealt with her will understand that hunting these ordinary, unsunlit people is merely her pastime during boring daily life.
After all, when she truly opens fire, the gun barrels are void of gunpowder.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
■File Number: DIOL-A3-1
■Awakener Code Name: ■■■■
A.F.3@2, this Awakener initiated contact with Mythag.

"Sun! Sun! I saw the Sun! No! No!! Destroy it! Or we will all die!"

According to eyewitness accounts, the involved personnel shouted the above statement upon discovering this Awakener, and then ordered relevant units to attack it.

Due to the incorrect judgment of the involved personnel, this Awakener displayed extreme aggression, directly resulting in the destruction of 31 anti-Awakener units, 76 standard equipment sets, and 2 academic buildings, with total property damage estimated at @2@4. The case has now been transferred to the relevant department for accountability procedures.

Post-incident investigation revealed that this individual had been brainwashed by the Church of the Lightbearers. For subsequent details, please refer to related documents, as it will not be elaborated in this file.

After a period of communication, negotiation personnel successfully convinced the Awakener to abandon its aggressive stance. Subsequently, the Awakener handed a letter signed by Lord Dexter to the school administration, claiming it was the last possession of a missing Investigator. Later confirmation by Lord Dexter verified the authenticity of the letter.

It is currently known that, through the mediation of Professor Doll, this Awakener has reached a certain agreement with the school administration. In exchange for some unspecified compensation, the Awakener has agreed to undertake certain commissions that the school finds difficult or inconvenient to handle directly.

Given the destructive capability demonstrated by this Awakener, despite displaying the highest level of stability and communicability in subsequent conversations, the Committee has decided to include it in the DIOL and will continue updating its file under the highest protocol.

May the Silver Key guide us.

Recorder: L. Nadia

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
■File Number: DIOL-A3-9
■Awakener Code: ■■■■

From continuous observation, it appears this Awakener does not show any unusual interest in Mythag—she seems almost indifferent. She only comes to campus when accepting commissions and leaves quickly afterward.
Although the returned investigation report indeed records several instances of her lingering on campus for extended periods,

further investigation revealed that her primary reason for staying was helping students resolve certain issues. It has been determined there is no security threat involved, so no intervention is necessary.

One point worth noting is that this Awakener appears unusually interested in the Keeper. She has repeatedly brought the Keeper @3 items classified as contraband and invited the Keeper to various dinners attended by unknown individuals. Considering the Keeper's importance to Mythag, if this Awakener attempts any dangerous action against the Keeper, at least four γ-class armed personnel must be arranged to ensure the Keeper's safe evacuation.

Recorder: K. Trak

(Small handwriting on the back of the file)
Hey, buddy. Or perhaps sister—it doesn't matter.
The last poor bastard quit, and now I'm stuck with this damn project.
If you're reading this, it means you're not some lazy good-for-nothing, but rather someone observant. Give yourself a round of applause.
Listen, although we keep saying we're "monitoring her secretly," I feel like she already knows about us—it's just that she chooses to ignore it.
I don't understand why we are monitoring someone like her. Although our first encounter had some misunderstandings, that was entirely caused by cultists, and had nothing to do with her personally.
Besides, her relationship with the students—especially the Keeper—is like a grandmother buying candy for her grandchild! Surely nobody constantly suspects their own grandmother of planning to sacrifice them to cultists... Well, actually, maybe somewhere that really is the case—but that's not important!
What matters is, if she truly has ulterior motives, I guarantee whatever she intends to do has already begun unnoticed, long before the Committee slowly drags out its ridiculous countermeasures.
Phew, feels good to vent. Anyway, I've decided to remove her from this list before the Committee does something unforgivable to her.
And once those bastards really piss her off—I promise it won't just be two buildings destroyed next time. I hope I'm dead by then, or else I'll probably die a very painful death.
If you're the one who will be watching her next, remember—not any rash actions. If you don't like her, just treat her like a mercenary hired to handle dirty work for the school. Unless she clearly shows signs of instability, under no circumstances should you provoke direct confrontation!
Of course, if you want a little gift... I doubt she'll mind that you once monitored her. Go ahead and ask boldly. Honestly, the snacks in her pockets are actually pretty tasty.
—Karapolis Trak, your fellow friend who curses the Committee alongside you.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
■File Number: DIOL-A3-27
■Awakener Code Name: ■■■■

Based on the behavior patterns, decision-making style, and attitude toward various tasks of this Awakener (hereinafter referred to as Kathigu-Ra), investigators conducted relevant speculations and later launched detailed investigations. The following content is now being synchronized:
Kathigu-Ra is an orphan from Region @2; their parents abandoned them during early childhood.
Kathigu-Ra survived in that region by unknown means and took in many unfortunate children. It should be noted that this process involved extensive use of bloody and violent methods. In one well-documented case, the subject brutally murdered all family members of one of the individuals they had taken in.
For a long time afterward, Kathigu-Ra and their subordinates mainly earned income through secretive commissions such as gangland killings, factional conflicts, looting stolen goods, and other black-on-black criminal activities.
According to local testimonies and combined with data from that area, it has been preliminarily inferred that there was a specific point in time when a group centered around Kathigu-Ra and the children they had taken in successfully consolidated control over the majority of the region's criminal organizations.
Currently, we still cannot determine exactly how Kathigu-Ra became an Awakener, but the timeframe aligns with the destruction of the Lantern Church outpost in that area. Given Kathigu-Ra's evident disdain for the Church of the Lightbearers, it is reasonably speculated that their transformation into an Awakener may have been connected to the Church.
According to incomplete statistics, up to now, Kathigu-Ra has destroyed 43 Lantern Church outposts, including 7 major outposts, 12 divine-summoning altars, and 17 research facilities.

Based on the above investigation report and the daily behavioral patterns of this Awakener, the Committee, under the proposal of Honorary Chairman K. Trak, has re-evaluated the threat level posed by Kathigu-Ra. The evaluation results are recorded as follows:
After deliberation by all committee members, this Awakener poses no direct or indirect threat to Mythag. Provided the current cooperation treaty remains unchanged, no intervention regarding its existence or actions is necessary.
Therefore, it is hereby decided that Kathigu-Ra shall be removed from DIOL, and all related investigation activities shall be suspended immediately. All file records shall be fully sealed, and no personnel below Ω clearance are permitted access.

Recorder: K. Trak
(Small handwriting at the bottom of the document)
Congratulations! Finally managed to get those overly cautious fools to cancel this damned project. Not bad for all the time I wasted pushing things forward.
This concludes the final record concerning Lady Kathigu-Ra. Unless something unexpected occurs, no further documentation will be added under this file number. So whoever you are, feel free to close this cursed folder now, step outside for some fresh air, and focus your attention on more deserving subjects!
— Karapolis Trak, your good friend who has become a member of the idle class.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
(Small text at the beginning of the file)
Damn it, how did you find this file? I clearly forbade anyone from accessing it... Well, since you're able to see this file, it means your clearance is far above mine, which indicates you're one of Them, so naturally I have no authority to stop you from knowing these things.

However, please keep in mind, this file should not exist anywhere. The following content is not any formal investigation, but rather an unofficial product composed by myself through secondary inferences based on certain real situations.

I must seriously remind you that the process of compiling this file contains a large amount of investigative methods that cannot be described in detail, subjective assumptions from personal thinking, and even acts of accepting bribes from investigated subjects. If you are not bothered by my aforementioned actions, please continue reading. I guarantee with my reputation that the following content is undeniably authentic.

■Subject Number: ■■■■-A■■-SSS
■Awakener Codename: Lord of Combustion
"Honestly, I don't really like fighting."
I was quite surprised to hear such an answer, considering in all the reports, investigators would use words like "madness," "joy," and "elation" when describing her in combat.
I originally thought her attitude toward fighting was "not initiating, not hating, not refusing," never expecting her to actually feel disgusted by the act itself.
As the conversation continued deeper, I gradually began to understand certain things. When I asked if I could record these words, Kathigu-Ra generously gave me permission to do so.

"You must understand, for most people in this world, simply being alive is already a luxury."
I expressed agreement with this statement. Although most people I've encountered live comfortable lives—I could even go to the biggest opera house in Lentienni anytime I wanted—I clearly know the hidden truth of this world, and how many people are carrying burdens behind my comfortable life.

"Therefore, in many cases, intense fighting spirit is the price you pay to stay alive."
"We don't get to choose how we live. Once you step into the mud, it's nearly impossible to pull yourself out again."
I originally wanted to argue against this viewpoint, but upon carefully recalling several cases, I found they were all people who could hardly be considered ordinary. As for those nameless nobodies, they mostly only exist within the memoirs of others.
"Perhaps some naive person might say, if that's the case, then why not just not do it from the beginning? That idea sounds good. I once carved it onto a certain tombstone to commemorate someone who strictly followed that principle."
"To protect everyone in this cruel world, I must appear strong and mad enough to make the ever-present malice hesitate."
"Of course, those emotions I display during battle are not fake. I truly enjoy the sensations battle brings me."
"It might be a bit hard to understand, but it's like how you might enjoy the content of your job, but not the act of working itself."
Upon hearing this explanation, I immediately understood her meaning. Indeed, I really enjoy prying into others' privacy to satisfy my own guilty curiosity, but that doesn't mean I enjoy having to drag myself out of bed at eight o'clock every morning and follow orders to stay somewhere the entire day.

"When I was first abandoned, I was just like many other girls—lost, hesitant, and clueless."
"I hoped to receive help from others, but the world gave me nothing but countless betrayals and pains."
"Gradually, I began using cruelty and madness to forge armor against malice, adapting to everything in this world like drinking poison to quench thirst."
"Until finally, they melted within the ceaseless flames, merging with my flesh and blood."
As if to prove her words just now, Kathigu-Ra suddenly gave me an extremely cruel, evil, and mad smile, so much that I spilled the pale yellow liquid in my cup all over myself.
Oh, maybe it's a good thing, because this way, if I ever lose muscle control and accidentally spill something, I can naturally explain it as just the drink I spilled earlier.
In the end, Kathigu-Ra never directly answered my question, but I think my previous judgment was correct—she simply shouldn't exist within DIOL.
—Recorder Karapolis Trak

"My original wish was simply to have a home."
