"A home without betrayal, without pain, filled with laughter and joy."
"Unfortunately, I didn't understand until that day."
"This was the most luxurious delusion in this cruel world."
—Kathigu-Ra

profile.title: Leigh
profile.introduction: Converts pain into strength, allowing for significant healing when HP is low.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt can restore a significant amount of HP, with more healing at lower HP levels.
After {Rouse}, playing your own Command Card can enhance {Embryo Fusion}, thus obtaining more {Embryo} to provide resource support for the {Caro} team.
In this level, Leigh will be brought into battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: Shh... Don't panic. It's just the sound of rotting rats in the damp cellar. From the deepest abyss, a crimson desire emerges. The imprisoned souls yearn for blood, just as the blind long for light and the hedonists crave lust. Pleasing her is simple: scold and humiliate her to your heart's content.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
......
Experiment Date: A.F. @1, @2
Experiment Objective: Observe and record the mutation response of dissolution patients after organ transplantation
Experiment Steps: @3 @4
Transplantation experiment on the deceased brother of the subject to observe rejection between direct relatives. The unfortunate individual was shot dead while evading police capture. @3 The experiment was highly successful; the wound turned red and sprouted tentacles... blood gushed out like rubies.
@3 @3
The subject demonstrated remarkable endurance, refusing morphine injections.
Post-experiment, her bloodlust intensified, and her temperament became... indescribable, not a type I am adept at handling.
—Excerpt from Herbert's "Dissolution Experiment Records, Group #4"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Staying away from the library basement, the cemetery, and Leigh has always been the consensus among students.
A pale and frail girl enjoys provoking others and then happily savoring their anger, like the serpent from the Garden of Eden, timely offering a secretive lure: Hurt me... Come on...
Most of the time, she receives glares, confusion, and curses in return. Only once did she finally get what she wanted—Uvhash unhesitatingly beat her up and even took a tooth from her. He is someone with no curiosity about others, seeing everything as it should be.
Watching the warrior dash away gracefully, Leigh smiled contentedly as the soft morning light bathed her. Yet another sunny day.
I still have plenty of teeth; take them all... Anyone, take them all...

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
A.F. @2, @1
Dear Lord Cavendish:

Please forgive me. I took the liberty of asking Butler Frey to bring me paper and pen. I am struggling to maintain my last shred of sanity and dignity as I write this letter. Suffering from a grave illness, I have not seen Mother, Bobby, or you, and this longing is enough to drive me mad.
It has been...353 days since I was locked in the basement? I'm not sure; perhaps it's 326 days.
It's freezing here, and there's an overwhelming stench of decay everywhere. My wounds are oozing black slime, and the rats have gnawed them beyond recognition. But it's fine; pain is good—it keeps one alert.
"An evil vampire," "bringing shame to the family"... isolating me was undoubtedly a wise decision. My soul has long been controlled by a devil. As I write this letter, I have just drained the blood of a deceased homeless man. Butler Frey said you commanded him to fetch me from the street. Although the taste was somewhat off, I'm still glad to know that you care about me.
Also... could you provide me with a blanket? I currently have no clothing to cover myself with; the winter in Londinium is unbearably cold.
Even now, I fondly recall the times we spent together at the Worthing estate during our childhood.

Your sincere sister,
Leigh
—Excerpt from "Kensington Vampire Incident: Case Summary C-601"

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
The taste of rust spread in the air.
That is... the taste of "food".
The girl slowly opened her eyes. After being injected with a large dose of sedatives for a long time, her mind was in chaos. Lying in front of her was a Beagle with its throat bitten off, apparently called... Bambi? Bobby?
Just as the girl was lost in memories, a sharp pain shot through her abdomen — she was attacked by "food". At that moment, the bound homeless man trembled, seemingly shouting loudly.
"Vampire! Monster... monster!!"
However, the girl paid no heed, only absentmindedly stroking the blood on her abdomen.
A large amount of warm blood poured out of the wound, like slippery satin, joyfully spilling everywhere.
It turns out, she could still feel pain, she could still bleed.
The girl silently shed tears. She extended her frail hands soaked in blood, gently caressing the "prey's" cheek.
"Next, could you please use a little more strength?"

profile.title: Lily
profile.introduction: Converts the pain it endures into fury to eliminate enemies or into HP to aid allies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Has powerful Defense abilities that gets stronger as HP drops.
The more pain, the more {Madness}; unleashing lethal {STR} in desperate situations.
In this quest, a level 50 Lily with Skill level 5 and Enlighten 3 will join the battle.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Yearning for an entity she calls "Mommy," this girl obediently follows her "Master"'s commands.
Her true name lost to the world, she is known only by the form she now inhabits: a delicate white filament, reminiscent of a lily, blooming at the center of a giant eyeball made of black sludge. Hence, they named her Lily.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
When trying to query records of Lily Oswood, Erica triggered 81 error alerts. As someone who was once forgotten by the world, Lily's birth records and medical history have turned into a handful of black sludge.
But we can still observe her silver shape through special instruments: delicate twists, resembling strands of intertwined hair—more accurately, like a child's lanugo.
It is particularly noteworthy that Lily always expresses a special attachment to linkers, and perhaps identifying herself as "mother" rather than calling herself a "companion" or "master" may help to bridge a quicker relationship with her

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
The aftereffects of incomplete awakening, coupled with the psychological impact of switching Links, have caused significant regression in Lily's character and mentality. Her silence on campus is evident to all.
Apart from staying close to the Keeper and Awakener Lotan, Lily is rarely seen alone around the campus. She doesn't attend classes nor join other Awakeners in their games. When not in battle, she quietly awaits orders in her dorm.
Lily's room window faces the Silver Key Tomb at the back of the campus. As the sun sets, she stands by the window, staring blankly at the tomb bathed in faint yellow light.
The Silver Key-shaped tombstones cast long shadows in the setting sun, and Lily stands in silence, just like the tombstones outside.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
"Mom, Mom, Lily didn't cry when she got the shot!"
"Lily is so brave!"
"Yeah, Lily doesn't hurt. Thinking about Mom coming back to be with Lily makes it feel like it doesn't hurt!"
...
"Master, give me an order."
"Lie down on the operating table; the operation will start in fifteen minutes. Did you take your medicine on time?"
"I did, Master."
"Cut here. It doesn't hurt, right?"
"... It doesn't hurt, Master."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
If only she had not awakened, those who are lost in the abyss should not gaze directly at the dazzling sun wheel.  
She should be a numb experiment material, someone buried by this world, and should not possess the perception of pain or the desire to be loved.  
Her mother's lingering attachment created the tragedy of her life, while Lily is still caught in the confusion of "whether to obey the arrangement of the world."  
Perhaps one day, she will find the answer from someone who defies fate

storySections.title: Story: V
storySections.content:
The small soul, which should have been lost to the river of history, relied on her mother's wish, the abundant spiritual energy from establishing the magic circle with @1@2@1, and the fusion with the giant creature frozen beneath Elworth, resurrecting as the monster "Lily".
Due to incomplete awakening, Lily at the beginning had no consciousness, merely a combat doll that obeyed orders.
Francis Oswood, a member of the Sculptors' Association, used Lily as a prototype to conduct illegal experiments lasting @2 years. From Francis's diary, it was known that Lily's state back then was extremely unstable, often going out of control and causing localized destruction, which forced Francis to frequently change residences.
With the intake of the gnosis elixir and the implantation of the silver chip, Lily regained her perception and some memories on an undocumented day. After some twists and turns, Francis took over the almost bankrupt Lai Hospital and transformed it into a testing ground.
During the "Hellhound" incident in Lai Town No. L-776, her mother, Francis Oswood, was trapped in her self-created anomalous dimension and lost contact.

profile.title: Liz
profile.introduction: Constantly grows the emerald flame in Ultra Space or in hand, then unleashes Exalt to release it all at once, burning away enemies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
At turn end, if "Green Flame" is in your hand or the {Ultra Space}, it will upgrade to {Corrupted Green Flame}. After upgrading, it can deal higher DMG and apply {Poison}.
When facing multi-hit Damage, you can gain multiple {Green Flames} through {Moth-Trapping Fire}.
Exalt can release all Liz's cards in hand without Arithmetica, accumulating a large number of {Green Flames} to deal extremely high burst Damage.
In this level, you will bring "Liz" at level 50, skill level 5, and enlightenment level 3 into battle, gaining 7 {STR} and 15 {Chaos} per turn, and after the start of the {Ultra Round}, an additional 100 {Chaos}. The enemies will receive an Enhance on their first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: She dances within the embrace of green flames, their dim flickering light falling across the dancer's young, devotion-filled face. With every turn, the emerald fire ignites the souls of the wicked, burning away every impurity upon the stage.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 31@1, December, the police received a report from the manager of the second theater in @2 Garden. The reporter claimed that there had been four small-scale fires in the theater within a week, and rumors of the "Ballet Ghost" were rampant among the members of the dance troupe renting the theater.
To ensure the smooth premiere of the ballet "Swan's Lament," the police launched a joint investigation with the West District Fire Department.
The day before the performance, Mythag University finally took over the investigation. The investigators noted traces of Gnosis residue behind several fire points. Eventually, Investigator @3 discovered an Awakener in the underground ruins of the second theater.
This Awakener was not a mindless individual; she identified herself as "Liz." After communication, the Awakener Liz eventually admitted to her arson crime. After the premiere of "Swan's Lament," Investigator @3 took Liz back to Mythag University for formal incorporation.

According to historical records, when the fire broke out at the second theater in @2 Garden on A.F. 29@1, a ballet was being performed. Among those who perished in the flames were a number of nobles in the audience, as well as a young prima ballerina named "Elizabeth Sur."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Liz is a reliable Awakener; although her attitude cannot be called "enthusiastic", she shows considerable cooperation with all school affairs.
The only concern is that, though Liz spends every night in the dance classroom, no one has seen her true dance performance.
As an excellent ballet dancer, Liz's name has appeared in several related publications. Unfortunately, due to the impact of the D-phenomenon, the information department of the awakening system could only determine that Liz had stepped onto the stage of the second theater in A.F. 29@1.
School reporter Hannah Peasley proposed gathering dance enthusiasts on campus, using their passion for dance to "open Awakener Liz's heart".
Currently, this proposal has yet to be approved by the Awakener Management Office.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Day 45 of joining the dance troupe, I stood on stage as the lead performer.
The scorching lights hit the hem of my skirt, and the tiny diamonds reflected a dazzling beauty. The glare made it hard to see, but the thunderous applause told me my performance was more brilliant than any gem.
In the constant spinning, all the dust in the world is purified by the dance, sending the beauty of dance and art to the world. This is my wish and mission as a dancer—

But who are those people lurking in the dark corners beneath the stage?

Lisette, the girl known for her carefree and upright nature, why is she whispering with that notorious lord?
Angelica, the genius who always looks down on other dancers, why is she lifting her skirt for that infamous playboy?

Such disgrace... such filth! Oh God, my muse, please tell me, what must I do to guard my stage from the darkness, what light is needed to dispel this filth—

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
This is the Promised Land, an artistic utopia, the ultimate paradise—dance should be the dancer's home, maintaining eternal purity.
As long as I burn, filth cannot taint our Church... This is my mission, my hope... Allow me to burn brightly, and I will cleanse all impurity for everyone.

profile.title: Lotan
profile.introduction: Deals multiple instances of "Strike" damage in succession and summons a world-ending beast to devour enemies.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Lotan has so many labels attached to her that no one knows how to approach this black-clad swordswoman.
Too humble, and she will shatter your knees for being boring; too arrogant, and she will slash your insolence with her greatsword; too tough, and she will soften your soul through battle.
But perhaps, this is the arrogance of the planet's first-born: as a former Guardian, she deserves her pride.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 180, as the Northern Hemisphere experiences a severe drought, the Korasuyu River sees its flow disrupted.
Local residents unearthed a monstrous skeleton from the riverbed's silt. Since the Korasuyu River signifies "chaos" in local culture, the skeleton was named the "Bone of Chaos."
Marine biologists identified it as the skeleton of a great winged whale, approximately 32 feet long and entirely covered in a silvery substance. The following year, this skeleton was auctioned at the Isis auction house for 130 million Leo pounds.
The trading firm refused to disclose the true identity of the buyer, but rumors suggest that the skeleton was transported to a private university on the outskirts of Londinium.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
At Mythag University, the campus legend that "preparatory students must fight Lotan to become investigators" has intensified, pushing students' fear of the black-clad swordswoman to its peak. Many students specializing in theoretical research break out in cold sweats at the mere sight of her black skirt, afraid of locking eyes with the "battle maniac" and perishing on the training grounds.
Editor-in-chief Hannah Peasley conducted a brief special interview with the person concerned, during which Miss Lotan made the following statements:
"I don't have that much time to play house with babies."
"Soft-shelled shrimp do not deserve to be my opponents—are you one of them? Or do you have the courage to face Chaos?"

— As of the date of this publication, the editor-in-chief has never left the student dormitory. This edition of the "M University Newspaper" has been printed and distributed by intern editor Howard.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
When it comes to choosing opponents, Lotan has her own standards.
Most scholarly warriors have read themselves into a corner, focusing solely on theoretical skills, which is rather annoying.
The elites in the army are often constrained by tedious formalities and moral codes, making them dull and uninspiring.
Only those rare talents, born with combat instincts and blessed by divine grace, can earn her favor.
The giant beasts, as ancient as the planet itself, suppress their battle desires and wait almost devoutly for their chosen opponents to reach their peak—what an extraordinarily gifted youth he is. He commands the power that originates from Aequor, and his fierce soul is capable of linking with the outworld Gods; given time, he will surely grow into a coveted powerhouse.
However, perhaps the passage of time has dulled her judgment; this time, the Spirit of Chaos has misjudged. For various reasons, Lotan does not wish to discuss this with anyone again.
All we know is that from then on, she sneers at anyone who shows "no ambition" or "no desire for progress."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Born from Chaos, she embodies pure chaotic power, an untamed free soul, and an undefined order.
She craves challenges and battles; only by unleashing her overwhelming destructive urge can she fulfill her creator's sole wish.
—To safeguard the planet until the end, she has long yearned for a fierce Battle.

profile.title: Miryam
profile.introduction: She uses her power as the High Priestess to command more Tentacles, and wields the Sacrificial Dagger to poison enemies.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Command Cards possess unique {Ritual} effects that convert {Sacrament} to enhance them.
Using Exalt not only grants more {Sacrament}, but also yields different effects based on your Tentacle Stances.
The three {Rouse} abilities play sequentially, each offering distinct effects, ultimately granting continuous acquisition of the powerful buff card {Divine Realm's Illusion}.
In this stage, Miryam and Aurita (Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlighten Level 3) are deployed. Maximum Hand Size +2. Miryam starts with 35 Aliemus and gains 20 Aliemus each turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Draped in black, Miryam's emaciated body was concealed beneath her robes.
Silent, she bore a countenance of devout sorrow, much like the Virgin in a mural, exuding endless mystery.
She poured her entire being into waiting for a special moment.
It is almost here; the appointed day draws near.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The rumors of humans landing in the polar regions have intensified this century. In response, Mythag University and the Mystia Treaty Committee have collaborated to form a joint investigation team.
In December of A.F. 3@1, the joint investigation team ventured deep into the Elworth ice fields with the aim of recovering potential mystical artifacts before the arrival of secular expedition teams.
......
On December 10, A.F. 3@1, Investigator Johansson accidentally fell into an ice cave, discovering a hollow space beneath the ice field. The altitude is too low; the melting ice will submerge the hollow within three days. We must complete our exploration before temperatures continue to rise.
On December 12, A.F. 3@1, there are reasons to believe that a cult has long used the hollow as a sacrificial pit. At the bottom of the cave, we discovered countless humanoid skeletons. Yes, humanoid—with cartilaginous fins connected to their skulls.
......
On December 13, A.F. 3@1, damn, damn, damn, damn! At the peak of the bone mountain, the investigator discovered the recent remains of a human!!!
The cult may not have yet withdrawn from the polar region. Due to the Committee's foolish "persistence," no Awakener investigators were permitted to accompany us. We must leave immediately!
——"Motion on Blockading the Wild Sea Route: Part One"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Miryam led a simple, ascetic life on campus: solitude, contemplation, silent prayer, cleaning, and labor, repeating daily.
She was indifferent to others' awe, caution, discussions, and curiosity. In the campus far from her homeland, she seemed to have found a new form of spiritual practice.
In fact, she didn't mind conversations. If you were willing to spare some time, she would narrate the greatness of that immortal deity, the distant prehistoric civilizations, and the eternal kingdom ruled by the wise young king. Every time she spoke of it, her eyes would reveal a compassion that the listener could not grasp.
Notably, Miryam showed unusual devotion and respect for Tulu, despite Tulu's indifferent attitude.
If it became necessary to negotiate with Miryam, seeking Tulu's help might bring about a favorable change in the situation.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
She was born before recorded human history, in an ancient civilization that can no longer be verified.
There, the people from the stars built glorious realms and temples dedicated to immortal gods. She grew up immersed in faith, devout from a young age, determined to become an apostle of the gods.
However, divine callings are not easily attained; she yearned for miracles and wonders but never witnessed any.
It was not until Tulu ascended to the throne that she finally rose to the position of high priestess—an unforgettable moment filled with profound ecstasy and joy that shook her very spirit.
She finally witnessed a miracle and understood the truth: this young king was the embodiment of the immortal she had longed for.
Years later, a catastrophic flood destroyed their civilization. She heard the final prophecy about immortality from Tulu and returned to the temple to pray until her last breath.
After countless ages, new humans repopulated the land, and the events of prehistory were forgotten.
Thus, Miryam's trials had only just begun.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
The unenlightened are pitiful, for they cannot comprehend the true essence of immortality. Worldly conflicts are meaningless, as they are but shallow diversions in the grand scheme of things.
I have gazed upon it, I have tasted it, and I have long understood my purpose.
But, oh, how cruel is fate! Why did you... not choose your most loyal Priest once more?
—It must be that you are bestowing upon me a trial, testing my faith. In the farthest reaches from my homeland, in moments devoid of your grace, my faith has endured through millennia, unwavering.
I could not be the one to welcome your rebirth, but I have prepared everything for it. I will seek out a new eternal realm for you... The mix of resentment and honor weighs heavily upon me, and I must bear this responsibility alone...
Forgive my ugly jealousy. As long as I can once again witness your majesty, as long as I can rebuild your kingdom, I am willing to do anything.

profile.title: Mouchette
profile.introduction: A lively maiden wielding a massive axe. She transforms into a frenzied warrior in battle, unleashing even greater combat power in adversity.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
A lively maiden wielding a massive axe. She transforms into a frenzied warrior in battle, unleashing even greater combat power in adversity.
When working in tandem with her allies, Mouchette's Battle Intent is greatly stimulated, and her offensive becomes increasingly fierce with the use of Strikes.
In this stage, a Level 50 Mouchette with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten Level 3 will join the battle, gaining 50 Aliemus, drawing 3 cards, and obtaining 3 Arithmetica at turn start.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Strictly speaking, she does not belong to this world on the brink of collapse, but she appreciates it.
She admires the magnificent spectacle of this world burning, appreciates the suffering and breakdown of these humans in despair.
Faith is dead, society has come to a halt, morals have crumbled, and nature no longer needs to disguise itself as a boring "normal person" —
she can finally unleash the beast within her and reveal her true self, becoming her authentic self.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
On January 17, A.F.3, after receiving the notification of the review meeting regarding the human sacrifice held by the Sect of Astral Dawn in region @4, I immediately set out to investigate the area.
By the time I arrived, it was already evening, and the area was shrouded in thick fog for unknown reasons, making visibility nearly impossible.
I did not carry any devices to disperse the fog, but considering the seriousness of the situation, I rushed into the mist anyway. If time could rewind, I would never have made such a reckless move, because, because there were terrifying @2 in the fog...
When those indescribable, twisted beings attacked me at inhuman speed, I couldn't react at all. My body went rigid, unable to move.
But ultimately, I did not die. That girl, the enigmatic girl from the fog, saved me.
She may have shouted "Tornado"? I didn't catch it clearly; I only saw an axe flying out of the fog, cleaving the head of that terrifying creature. Then the girl took the axe and slung it over her shoulder.
"It's very dangerous in the fog; if you don't want to die, leave this place."
As she said this, her expression was smiling, a sweet smile of a young girl, but for some reason, I found that smile even more chilling than the monsters in the fog.
A human, or rather something with a human form, traversing through the mist filled with monsters, yet remaining unscathed while maintaining that "sweet" smile...
In fear, I finally got up and screamed, distancing myself from her.
Soon, the thick fog occupied my line of sight, and I fell into a state where I could see nothing again. But her laughter, her innocent, youthful laughter, kept echoing in the fog.
— "File: Investigation of the Sect of Astral Dawn in Region @4"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
In the eyes of most students, Mouchette is definitely the most suitable person to be friends with. She always wears a smile, enthusiastically engages in conversations, and is unlikely to refuse any requests made to her, which is why she often gets invited to go shopping, chat, or eat.
But only a few know her true nature. In front of these people, she no longer has to pretend to be friendly or hide her contempt for rules and order.
"What friends? Just a way to pass the time out of boredom. If I don't do this, I'll be seen as a problem student and taken to see Principal Kellett for questioning—what a hassle."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Her life seemed to always be full of surprises: first, she encountered the rampant unknown fog, and then she was inexplicably summoned to another world.
When the summoning ritual was completed and she first set foot on the land of this other world, Mouchette saw the familiar thick fog, and within it, the screams of people.
Those were the sacrifices fleeing from the cultists.
Sacrifices? Cultists? What... exciting and fresh vocabulary.
This represented a new world, running a jungle game of survival of the fittest, and coincidentally, she had never been the weak one.
Calmly, she hunted the cultists in the fog, happily even humming a tune.
"One by one, and again... in the unknown fog, I vent freely ♪"
"Knock open your skull, delve into your brain, let's pass the time in this chase ♪"
Amidst this casual humming, Mouchette sensed the hollow hunger within her—
this mad world could not fill the void in her heart, but it could alleviate her soul's hunger for destruction and madness.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
One day, she will return to her own world.

But before that, she wants to see the conclusion of this world—

Will the one known as the "Keeper" be consumed by this chaotic and terrifying world, or will they shine brightly, bringing true redemption and light to this crumbling world?

This is a mystery, a challenge, a gamble.

And she wants to know the answer.

profile.title: Murphy
profile.introduction: Withstands a Sacrifice Ritual to resist high enemy damage and commands Tentacles to launch a full-scale attack.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses unique protective abilities that transform enemy DMG into {Sacrifice}.
Using cards can grant a number of Tentacles that ignore the Tentacle Limit.
Flexibly overdrawing {Sacrifice} to exchange for DEF can provide more powerful short-term combat power.
In this stage, "Murphy" will be deployed at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Proud, willful, self-centered... Many who have interacted with her would describe her personality in such ways. She seemed to have always been treated like the center of the universe in her previous living environment, perhaps it was this meticulous care that cultivated her spoiled nature.
"Hey! What are you looking at? Didn't I say that if I call you three times, you must respond to this young lady!"
--- One must be very careful even when reading the documents.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Year 3@2 Summer, legends of sea monsters spread across the Irian Isles.
Fishermen claimed to have spotted a five-story-tall cephalopod near the shore. Its skin was deep crimson, with black concentric rings on its tentacles.
I heard this rumor while verifying suspected D-phenomenon in Mianland with Senior Johansen. The next day, the senior set sail for the Irian Isles, and I wrote this report in the Amirante Ocean.
On the third day of sailing, vast fish carcasses appeared: mackerel, flounder, dolphins, and even a pregnant sperm whale—I swear! Old sailors blamed oxygen depletion caused by overbreeding, but I felt like we were missing something...
On the eighth day of sailing, my seasickness worsened, and severe nausea made it nearly impossible to eat. Despite nearing the Irian Isles, Johansen insisted on returning@3... I didn't understand, but I trusted the senior.
—Bale Columbus "Year 3@2 Summer Internship Report"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
After being Awakened, we finally learned what kind of life Murphy led in the Order.
She spends a lot of time on her appearance before going out, always carrying a parasol. Afternoon tea is at 4 PM, accompanied by milk tea and strawberry jam scones. Maintaining cleanliness and elegance, living like a lady—this is her destiny, and she insists on living as such no matter where she is.
(Note from the Archivist: I think her grabbing beef pudding with her hands is far from ladylike...)
Even without servants to command, she lives exactly as she wishes, which is somewhat comforting.
Due to a certain Event, she rarely interacts with Awakeners Miriam and Goliath, often ignoring them. She displays a complex expression when meeting Awakener Tulu.
Talking about the past makes her irritable. Perhaps she just needs more time to heal.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
"You bear a noble mission, the hope of His return to the world."
"Be good, obey... When He wakes again, everyone will dwell in the eternal realm."
The secret girl, whose birth could not be revealed, became the high priest's treasure for some reason. The once-neglected infant transformed into the saint linked to destiny and a mysterious civilization.
The ascetic mother raised her; worship and adoration surrounded her. Whatever she desired, someone would offer without hesitation.
Had the D-phenomenon not occurred, had the vessel of God not weakened, she would have simply succeeded the high priest, leading the followers in an orderly manner.
Yet, everything she gained came at a heavy price—a fate she never wished for, predestined to end inescapably.
To become the Mother of God, bearing the prophesied child—the glory of the saint demanded her willing sacrifice of body and life.
The day a second heart began beating in her chest, her soul turned into a surging torrent, destined to vanish with time.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
The arrogance of disregarding others is merely an empty show of force. Beneath that elegant dress lies a rebellious heart known only to herself.
Trapped in a beautiful cage crafted by sacred narratives, Murphy understands better than anyone the hypocrisy of humans and gods: the gaze of the congregation upon her is nothing but fear and pity, and the care shown by her "nurturers" is merely for a sacrificial offering. The end goal of her mission will not be glory, but her own death — she already knows the outcome. How could she be lost in the distant kingdom painted by doctrines?
The plans for deification, the revival of the old king, so many unrealistic expectations placed upon her — yet no one has ever cared whether she is willing to accept a doomed fate for an uncertain future.
The canary does not wish to remain forever trapped in a cage; thus it flaps its wings toward the thorny fence. Determined to wager her life to break free from the shackles of destiny, she must now decide the direction of her flight herself.
"My name is Murphy... don't think you can order me around! I know what I should do and what I shouldn't do."

profile.title: Murphy: Fauxborn
profile.introduction: As the Divine Mother, she grants blessings and sanctuary to her subjects. Her ultimate power allows her to consecrate the lives of her enemies as a sacrifice.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
As the Divine Mother revered by Seafarers, she establishes a new order to protect all her subjects, significantly enhancing the base effect of the {Aequor Realm}.
Inverted Fate grants her supreme power, allowing her to consecrate the lives of ther enemies as a {Sacrifice} to the god. However, a taboo power often comes with a steep price...
In this Stage, "Murphy: Fauxborn"and "Tulu" will be deployed, both at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten Level 3.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
She always arrogantly refers to herself as the "Divine Mother," coldly and hostily keeping everyone outside her safe perimeter. It's difficult for those meeting her for the first time to connect her with the spoiled little princess.
"But they are essentially the same person," the Keeper, however, stubbornly insists to everyone. "You'd know if you ever saw her wolf down a cupcake alone."

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Recorder: Erich von Werner
Date: November 7, 19@1
Coordinates: 68°14′S, 67°10′W
Due to an unusual number of floating sperm whale carcasses in our route, we were forced to land on this cursed island of ice. Below a glacial waterfall in the south shore fjord, the expedition team discovered the remains of a fifty-foot-long cephalopod. Its body was a deep indigo blue, densely covered in phosphorescent, luminous blue concentric rings, the edges of which secreted a thin layer of mucus. After we sampled and purified the substance, an unsettling quantity of blue crystals precipitated from the mucus.
The suckers on the creature's arms exceeded two feet in diameter, and the inner walls of the suckers were etched with patterns resembling an unknown script. The main nerve cord was in a semi-transparent, gelatinous state, maintaining a faint pulse even in the -30°C environment (see attached Figure D-7). Dr. Harris, the accompanying biologist, attempted to collect a tissue sample from the nerve cord, but the moment the scalpel made contact, the entire carcass instantly disintegrated into powder. Indigo-blue dust became visible, suspended throughout the laboratory.

During the voyage home, Dr. Harris developed persistent delirium. He repeatedly drew geometric shapes combining spirals and polyhedrons on the cabin walls, screaming, "They are spawning beneath the Antarctic ice sheet!" On the morning of the second day, a patrolling crew member found him drowned in the ballast water tank. Autopsy revealed his alveoli were filled with indigo-blue microorganisms (identical to the octopus corpse dust). The captain ordered the incineration of all clothing that had come into contact with the samples.
May no one else ever touch the nightmare lurking beneath the ice.

— Appendix No. 37, Antarctic Expedition Log

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
After being awakened, Murphy: Fauxborn frequently has a dream of escaping. In the end of that dream, she holds her "Mother's" dagger and plunges it through her own chest under the brilliant moonlight. The dream then abruptly ends, and the moment she opens her eyes, they meet the gaze of the ivory statue at the foot of her bed.
In Mythag, Murphy: Fauxborn acts like a younger version of her "Nurturer." She wears white mourning clothes year-round, rarely laughs or speaks lightly with others. Only when the conversation turns to her preference for sweets does a crack appear in her flawless, saintly facade.
<Italic:(Archivist's Annotation: I hope next time she wishes to enjoy cupcakes, she will ask for it immediately, rather than leaving a note requesting delivery to her room after everyone's afternoon tea has ended.)
She maintains a distance from any Awakener with relation to the Seafarers. Unlike her other-world counterpart, she never deliberately avoids contact with them; however, the frozen, emotionless expression and thinly veiled hostility in her sarcasm subconsciously drive away those who don't want a confrontation.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Murphy: Fauxborn has died once before.
It was not a willing step through the threshold called "Divine Descent"; she witnessed the "God" being born from her own chest. How ridiculous: it was a hideous, unconscious, and unknowing mass, yet she was forced to give up her vibrant, splendid life for it.
More ironically, the twisted fate remained relentless. At the moment the God was born into the world, she accepted the unwilling sacrifice through intense pain. After a long period resembling sleep, Murphy opened her eyes in astonishment, only to find the scene before her identical to her final moments.
"Murphy" died. "Murphy: Fauxborn" was Awakened.
Her Linker is a squirming blood clot, deaf and mute, that she herself gave birth to.
The God gives birth to the Princess, the Princess re-conceives the God, and the God reawakens her remains. A pathological fate clings tightly to her, an endless, unbreakable cycle. Her devout mother is ecstatic, the Seafarers prostrate themselves in prayer, and her dignity is restored, more glorious than ever before.
But the original "Murphy" is truly gone forever. The girl who once, with a burst of foolish innocence, attempted to rebel against an unfair destiny, now embraces the crown of vanity she once despised and boards a cruise ship that can never reach the other shore.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
"I am the Daughter of God, and simultaneously the Bearer of God."
This is how she confirms her authority. Only by maintaining this universally admired dignity can she preserve a precarious balance. So what if it's hypocrisy? So what if it's a delusion? Who isn't living in the "present" moment?
Murphy holds the swaddling cloth, which conceals the grotesque Divine Fetus, and acts as the God's proxy, speaking one lie after another:
"He has descended." "The Divine Realm will rise." "Please wait for the next moment of Stellar Alignment."
Rather than letting others' hypocrisy fabricate an illusion, she would rather decorate this prison with her own hands to a likeness she finds acceptable. As long as the "Mother," "Companions," and "Servants" she desires are still by her side, whether it is superficial deference or insincere mystification, she accepts it and finds it sweet.
"My name is Murphy, the Princess who bore the God. Regarding Lemuria, regarding the Seafarers' long-cherished wish... you need not inquire. I have my own arrangements."

profile.title: Nautila
profile.introduction: Her sturdy shell bolsters your team's defense, using spikes on the shell to counterattack incoming rivals.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses strong defensive and stacking {Counter} abilities, which become even more powerful after {Rouse}.
The higher the Current Shield, the more Shield gained from Exalt; the higher the Current {Counter}, the greater the AoE DMG dealt by Exalt.
In this level, Nautila and Doll will join the battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten 3, gaining 10 Aliemus per turn, with enemies receiving Enhance on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
She seems to be constantly circling around the motorcycle named "Phantasm": tightening a screw here, adjusting a pipe there... It's an endless project, and although many believe she has done the best repairs, she still immerses herself in it day after day. 
The reason lies in that vague feeling of "something is still missing".

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F. 3@2, a D-phenomenon occurred in coastal towns of the Agló Republic, spreading outward in a spiral from the port, affecting an area hundreds of meters wide and causing @1 deaths.
Investigations traced the phenomenon back to a fishing boat that set out on A.F. 3@2 @1 @1@1, catching a silver-patterned cod-like creature.
The creature's soft body ruptured when cut by a sharp tool, releasing fluid laden with shell fragments and other residues. Upon reaching the port, shell-like growths had appeared among the catch but were ignored until a fisherman perceived himself as a shell, twisting his body and ending up hospitalized. Only then was the event reported.
Mythag personnel later located the source of the phenomenon at @4 cannery, removed it, and secured Silver O-36.
—<Italic:"Awakening System Silver Acquisition Report: O-36">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Three months after being Awakened, Investigators and Keepers threw a party for Nautila. Not to celebrate her arrival, but to celebrate that she finally remembered the name "Mythag University."
Although she forgot it again the next day, parties can always be held again. Who knows? Maybe in another three months, she will truly remember.
It's hard to say whether Nautila's goldfish-like memory is a side effect of Dissolution or a result of Silver deficiency. However, short-term memory loss isn't entirely a bad thing. For a diligent mechanic, forgetting which screw was tightened means tightening them all over again—ensuring the equipment maintained by her is more durable.
Doll really likes her for it; after all, not everyone has the skill to make machinery last for a decade with just one repair.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Whenever night falls, Nautila will burrow into the storeroom with her mother, pick up various tools, and piece together a unicycle called Phantasm.  
"Why not just buy one directly?" the young girl looked up and asked her mother, who was holding the tools, "Then we could go out and adventure right away."  
Her mother held her hand, teaching her how to weld metal quickly and well while saying:  
"Machines, like people, each have different temperaments and personalities. Only those who truly understand us can spend longer time with us."  
"Like friends?"  
"Except that machines are made by us—that's right, like friends."  
The huge wheels must be sturdy, and the engine must be durable, because once they set off, they won't easily turn back. They want to travel the world together and complete a grand adventure.  
Illness took her mother away, leaving behind a workshop full of tools but void of life. Nautila held the unfinished Phantasm, determined to complete her and her mother's dream with this cold friend.  
Until the last journey.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
A.F. 315, January, @2
Dr. Sylvia suggested that I jot down my thoughts in a notebook to help train my memory. Everyone says doctors know a lot and can surely help me. So I'm counting on it; tomorrow, I mustn't forget the purpose of this notebook! 
 Today, I completed the production and assembly of the exhaust pipe. Now it can exhaust air without interfering with the auxiliary hose's movement. This should be the last step, right? At least I haven't found any issues so far, but I can't rule out forgetting a screw somewhere... That's right, I must have forgotten something—either a screw or a pipe, or maybe bearing lubrication? Engine oil? Honestly, I've always had this nagging feeling that something's off with the Phantom. It's always... lacking? Is that the word? It misses a crucial component, even more critical than the engine. Without it, I can't set off, can't embark on that journey into the unknown, because that is ours. @2 
 Why am I saying ours? Who am I with? Did I... have someone? I remember, it seems like... @2 @4 @3 
 Uh, my head hurts so much. 
 But this notebook feels quite useful; that way, I can continue recalling from here tomorrow, and who knows, maybe I really will remember? 
 — The only entry in Nautila's diary.

profile.title: Nymphaea
profile.introduction: Applies Poison and Weakness to continuously torment opponents while ensuring your team's safety.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Quickly stack {Poison} on enemies using Command Cards, then trigger the enemy's {Poison} effect with Exalt to deal massive DMG.
The DMG and {Poison} from {Midnight Tide} will increase as you play Symptom Cards.
The {Poison} applied by Exalt will increase with each use.
In this battle, bring Nymphaea with a Level of 50, Skill Level 5, and 3 Enlightened, and Obtain 3 Symptom Cards at the Start of the Battle.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: This projected body remains in the form of a girl who passed away prematurely. With tattered clothes, pale skin, and eyes filled with shadows of the past, it silently tells of a history entwined with illness and suffering.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
This is a souvenir Professor Dexter brought back from the Febvre Coast.
The mild ocean climate of Febvre is very pleasant. The century-old Sevada Sanatorium is located by the seashore, attracting countless vacationers each year.
In this glass bottle, wrapped in seaweed, are some translucent powders. It is neither a message left by shipwreck survivors nor a local sand art piece. Professor Dexter has noticed that the bottle contains silver essence powder, meaning it houses a spirit.
He said he bought it from a local fisherman who hesitantly narrated recent strange encounters. A few days ago, early in the morning, the fisherman encountered an unusual fish swarm while fishing. It was neither the breeding season nor the wintering season, yet the swarm lingered. When the heavy nets were pulled up, they found a strange bottle. The fisherman, curious but uneasy, feared the bottle might bring bad luck or a curse. After this, no fish were caught in this area for several days.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
She treats every person and every Awakener with great kindness, never showing any signs of aggression. Most of the time, she stays quietly in her room, gazing out the window. At her bedside, we discovered several well-worn classical novels, along with handwritten essays and poems on notepaper, written in a delicate, graceful hand.

"Compared to that Maiden, I am clearly the lucky one." She whispered to the shimmering campus pond, facing the setting sun.

She rarely takes part in outdoor activities—though rather than saying she is unwilling, it might be more accurate to say she simply doesn't know how. That Maiden-like Awakener seems to lack a great deal of common knowledge about everyday life.

On several occasions, we also witnessed her conversing with the fish in the fountain in a strange sound—like bubbles being exhaled, or the hoarse gurgle of someone drowning. Yet her expression remained calm and serene, as if she were exchanging comfort with a dear friend. In that moment, we all believed she had found true peace.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
One step, then another, I slowly walked into the water. The nurse at the sanatorium never allowed me to go this far. I hope a patient who will soon be unable to afford the sanatorium's fees doesn't cause her too much blame.
The sunset's afterglow reflected on the sea. Waves approached gently, lifting my skirt softly. The seawater's body temperature felt warm like a mother's embrace.
My body had never felt as light as it did at this moment; a desolate volcano lay within my chest, and now only the sea breeze remained there.
"Let's go home."
I heard the sea calling me in my parents' voices. They went to the other side of the sea to raise money for my treatment. If I hadn't been given such an unfortunate body, they wouldn't have had to struggle so much. If only I could vanish quietly into the sea on a moonlit night... I had thought this countless times, but thinking of my parents' sorrowful faces, I could only give up.
But one day, on a moonlit night, the ship my parents were on quietly disappeared into the sea.
"Let's go home."
I used all my strength to quicken my pace. The sea always calmly accepts all the suffering souls, and for me, the place where my parents are is the eternal refuge.
When the darkness descends from all sides, I was finally able to breathe freely for the first time in my life

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
I love the sea. Febvre's sea. The Prussian blue sea. The sea with warm, moist breezes. The sea that looks like a painting from the sanatorium's window. The sea with returning sails. The sea nurturing countless lives like a mother.
Now, I've finally returned to the tranquil sea, back to before birth, back to being enveloped in my mother's amniotic fluid. The currents gather the unfortunate Fragments, piecing me together among bones and coral. The deep-sea fish swim past, listening to the laments of fate's suffering.
Mother, if I could be born again, would I be happier?

profile.title: Ogier
profile.introduction: Condenses a formless barrier from viscous slime, then raises a spear to signal teammates and initiate the charge.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Every word and action embodies loyalty, every move reflects virtue.
Although "knowing propriety and righteousness, being strict with oneself" is deeply engraved in the soul, this virtuous knight undoubtedly still bears the appearance of a child.
But please do not worry, even if the price is eternal demise, Ogier will spare no effort to fulfill his lord's commands.
May that day never come.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
A.F.2@2, November, a major earthquake struck the Beren region.
The next day, two professors were ordered to head to the disaster area to search for the missing Mythag investigation team.
The team was last active along the Mare Internum coast, primarily tasked with exploring, excavating historical relics, and searching for possible leads related to the "D-phenomenon." 
On November 8, at the location where team members were reportedly last seen, Professor @2 discovered an unrecorded palace ruin and recovered a Mythag-standard silver key, numbered I-@2, but did not find any team members.
On November 9, two Silvers were unearthed at the site, which were later taken back to Londinium and sealed due to their historical value, to be Awakened on another day.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"If it's your first time carrying out an investigation mission, go find little Ogier to accompany you!" 
In response to the anxiety of new investigators, experienced seniors always offer this advice. Ogier's popularity among students is evident from this. 
Although his old-fashioned personality occasionally causes minor incidents, they support each other and lift one another up. Ogier always achieves the most harmonious rhythm with the investigators. In recent years, having Ogier accompany new investigators on their first missions has become a consensus among everyone at the school. Jokes like "Ogier prepares a cloak for female investigators" and "Ogier blushes when he sees legs" have also spread among the students. 
The title of "Death Knight" has gradually been forgotten, drowned in trust and warmth, leaving only a tiny thorn embedded in the young boy's heart.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Few people in school remember that the boy was once called the "Death Knight". 
Only at midnight does the scenery of that day transform into a nightmare, infiltrating the boy's dreams.
The dissoluted bodies invading the campus, the collapsed bell tower, the barely surviving "Keeper". 
"Don't cry, Ogier, it's not your fault..." 
The iron cavalry invading the home, the tilted palace, the black blood flowing from the princess's fingers.
"Don't sacrifice yourself for me, Ogier, you've done enough already..." 
The grains of memory entwined with pain wash over the boy's spirit.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
— How can this not be his fault? What he has done is far from enough.
With such extreme self-blame, Ogier executes his tasks at twice the frequency of other Awakeners, burying "Death" under universally praised "Virtue".
The excessive work and the words of gratitude from young Investigators seem to wash away the pain etched into the Soul.
Chronic ailments are pushed into the Shadows of the heart, emitting a rotting smell, secretly leading the knight towards the deep black mire.
"Sorry? Hello?"
Someone came to Ogier with a ray of sunshine, or a gust of wind.
"Senior said Ogier will join me and Ramona to carry out my initial Investigation task, right?"
He couldn't protect the princess for a second time, but Heaven gave him another chance to protect the "Keeper".
"My name is Ogier, and it is an honor to form a Contract with you. I will guard your Life with my Soul. Today's vow will be remembered forever."

profile.title: Pandia
profile.introduction: Strikes rivals while gaining a large amount of Counter, leaving any would-be retaliators in excruciating pain.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Through {Rouse} and Command Cards, you can quickly stack {Counter}. The higher the DMG dealt, the more {Counter} you Obtain.
The higher your Current {Counter}, the greater the AoE DMG caused by Exalt. If there is only 1 enemy, Exalt will also deal an additional instance of DMG.
In this stage, Pandia will join the battle at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlighten. Enemies will gain Enhance on their first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: A tinkling sweet laugh echoes hollowly, and a petite figure appears in dim candlelight. She is both the fear and desire of the condemned, the dew and poison for the dying, bringing sweet pain and romantic madness. The "snap" from her ever-present whip on the skin is an evil symphony playing in the darkness.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Awakening Material: Silver Core #G752a, unearthed from a festering mud heap at the ruins of Londinium's Newgate Prison. Investigator @2 accidentally slipped on a shattered skull while walking through the dim, grimy prison corridor and stumbled upon it by chance.

The awakening result deviated from expectations—what emerged from the black D-Slime was not some vicious death-row convict or a hulking executioner, but a petite young maiden with a sly smile.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"Be wary of Pandia. Her danger is not reflected by her appearance. She's a demon."
These are the heartfelt words of countless investigators, born from bitter experience.
Pandia often creates sweet traps, luring other researchers or Awakeners into master-slave Contracts, then mercilessly manipulating their emotions and Souls.
Conversations with Pandia always end fruitlessly. She insists that she is merely spreading joy with her new lovers, and the Contracts are voluntary. After all, she also lets her slaves taste the sweet fruits.
However, there is someone at the school who can counter Pandia. Whenever Sorel passes by Pandia's dormitory, Pandia falls silent, no matter the topic. Rumor has it that Pandia once suffered a major setback while trying to beguile Sorel, but the truth remains a mystery.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
"O sinners, this is the abyss of Desire, the bottom of Hell. The black hound roars here, the stench spreads here, and the death knell tolls here. You have gathered here due to your indulgent desires and cruel actions, and what awaits you is suffering and death."
Pandia once served in Newgate Prison, the most oppressive, gloomy, and sunless filthy corner of Londinium. Once bloodied heavy criminals were shackled and stripped of their dignity, kneeling to her in the Maze-like cell area, kissing her leather boots, humbly calling themselves her servants.
Whenever a new execution took place, she would summon new death row inmates, asking them to appreciate their own demise. As the executed squirmed in the hemp rope, she liked to sit high up, watching the worst of the worst tremble in fear and desperation.
But there was also a "Hell Cat" who observed that Pandia came to the iron gate of Newgate Prison countless times, tiptoeing to gaze outside London, as if waiting for a visitor from afar.
"In fact, I too can only be, a part of the miserable dark and damp dungeon that I can never escape."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
"Master, whip me."
The fire of desire burned in her eyes that should have dried up, melting wax dripping like tears.
Thirst, pain, curses, filth—all the humiliation she craved, all the pleasures she offered, finally arrived upon her in this moment.

profile.title: Pickman
profile.introduction: Flexibly create different Paintings, and greatly enhance the Exalt effect of other Awakeners.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
This artist uses human remains as his medium, and his macabre creations provide various spiritual enhancements to his team. As he sketches, he accumulates creative energy. Once his inspiration peaks, it erupts in a frenzied artistic banquet.
In this stage, a level 50 Pickman enters the battle alongside Lotan, Ogier, and Doll. Each turn, Pickman gains an additional 100 Aliemus, while other allied Awakeners gain 30.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
A half-human, half-ghoul artist, he possesses an otherworldly talent that remains forever out of reach for ordinary souls.
His ghoul artworks dissolve the fragile boundary between the painted world and the real one, pulling viewers into a total, and often terrifying, immersion.
Yet Pickman vehemently rejects the title of mad surrealist.
In his own declaration, he has always been, and will only ever be, a realist—for he paints nothing but the world as it truly is.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The courses of the Painting Department in the Academy of Fine Arts are tedious and mundane, most of which have left no trace in my life, but I can still recall the mimetic painting class in A.F. 3@2.
Mr. Matthew is renowned in the art association, his classroom is crowded with people. We huddled in front of Matisse's "Luxury, Calmness, and Delight," and at that moment, I noticed a thin young man in the corner madly smudging in a sketchbook. I approached him, ready to see his mimicked painting, but saw a chaotic stone sculpture of a graveyard.
"Look! The ghoul queen is giving birth, preparing for a blood feast for her child... how mad, how elegant!"
He excitedly looked up at me, his pupils glowing with a strange blood-red under the gaslight, as if it was a coffin covered in red moss. I felt fear and dizziness, but I couldn't take my eyes off it. In those eyes, I saw images completely unworthy of this world.
Pickman, from the first time I saw him, I knew he was different from us, his life was destined to be difficult and extraordinary.
After getting acquainted, I occasionally visited his studio. I witnessed him using decaying corpses as models and his own blood as paint. Standing in front of those crazy artworks, I felt as if I could hear the gnawing sounds coming from the canvas.
No matter which faction in the art world, no one recognized Pickman's creations; all exhibitions and salons turned him away. He appeared unfazed, still optimistically pursuing his art, but his purity made it impossible for him to hide his loss and pain. His talent was both shocking and frightening, and no one could truly accept it, including myself.
In the final days before leaving, Pickman fell into a state of great pain and excitement, which I originally thought was a psychological issue that was bound to occur, until one day, I saw his twisted limbs and claws, and witnessed the mad world in his eyes. That was a fear I couldn't contend with, and I could only try to forget it. Before I could completely end my connection with Pickman, he proactively found me to say goodbye.
Now, standing in the modern art museum's pristine gallery, looking at the genuine Pickman's work discarded in the corner, I can still hear the sound of nails scraping from deep within the canvas. "Underground Carnival," that was the painting he created when I first met him, and also the farewell gift he left me. Pickman might hold some taboo puzzles that should not be approached, but I still kept this painting. No matter what happened to him that I shouldn't know, one thing is certain - he is the most talented artist I have ever met.
Last night, I dreamed again of that painting class room in my freshman year. The young Pickman was in the corner of the room, laughing loudly at me.
"Ghoul? No, Ever, what I painted from start to finish is the truth, is humanity!"
The moonlight split into countless bloody points in his eyes. That was the first time the Abyss smiled at me. 

—Excerpt from <Italic:"The Memoir of Painter Avery @2">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
A.F.3@2 Feb 12th
During the visit to the statue studio, the completed sculptures were painted with colors. The color design is quite innovative, but it contradicts the original design system of the sculpture set.
Afterward, I agreed to take on all the coloring work for the statues for free. Perhaps there will indeed be opportunities for us to collaborate in the future.
Witness: Sanga
...

A.F.3@2Apr 9th
Attempted to dig up corpses in the Keygrave as models, but failed. Captured on site, invited the patrol personnel to pretend to be corpses.
Witness: Ogier
...

A.F.3@2 Apr 11th
Stealing kitchen ingredients as reference objects for a painting. Although the ingredients were successfully saved, the timing of the spice marination and the flavors of the ingredients have been completely ruined, losing their value.
As for the attributes and sources of the ingredients... it's not convenient to disclose that for now.
Witness: Doresain
...

A.F.3@2Jun 29th
Illegally infiltrating the school's library, hanging your own massive painting in the center of the wall. At midnight, influenced by the school's Silver energy density, a bizarre creature emerged from the painting, causing damage to some unprotected artifacts.
Afterwards, he voluntarily applied for maintenance work, during which the number of damaged artifacts exceeded the damage caused by the monsters. The school has urgently suspended his maintenance activities.
Witness: Czort

...

A.F.3@2 Jul 23rd
Diving into the Black Pool, attempting to use the pool water as paint. Tawil did not disturb him, but the power of the Black Pool itself caused him significant harm.
I am actually looking forward to what kind of scene he will create with the Black Pool?
Witness: Alistair
...

—"Pickman Records: Violation Log

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Before receiving that invitation from Necrovia, Pickman thought there would never be a place in this world for someone like him.
The extreme madness he witnessed at the royal banquet table kept him tossing and turning in sleeplessness. Those eyes that could see scenes from different worlds found true fullness at this feast.
When he awoke from the Dream and Necrovia faded from his sight, Pickman's regret quickly transformed into an endless longing—he yearned to return to that dream city, to witness once more the infinite madness of that breathtaking scene. Following this desire, he attempted every mad act. In the end, he leaped into the D-Rift, painting with Dissolution, completely exposing himself to that horrifying darkness.
Everyone has their own tasks to accomplish. He has eyes that can see the mad truth of the world, and he firmly believes that he was born to burn for art; this is his mission.
As he wished, Dissolution brought him madness and pain, allowing him to once again witness the scenes he experienced in Necrovia. The twisted world in his eyes became even wilder and more vibrant under the corrosion of Dissolution. Amidst the prolonged suffering, he picked up the paintbrush, painting day after day without pause. He is a devoted realist painter, and he must record these visions that only he can see.
The pain intensified, and when Pickman could no longer grip the brush in his hand, he knew his time was near. After completing the final stroke of his last painting, he lay down in the coffin he had long prepared, waiting for death to arrive.
The pain and chaos brought by Dissolution clouded Pickman's mind. In the cold embrace of death, he peacefully closed his eyes. But the next day, the bright moonlight awakened him once again. The traces of Dissolution and pain miraculously faded away. After several days of growth, his limbs became long and curved, and his canine teeth and claws became hard and sharp. He sensed the scent of graves and spirits, and the mad, chaotic sights of the world became clearer in his eyes than ever before.
He gazed joyfully at his half-human, half-ghoul body, a tremendous sense of happiness swelling in his heart. Fate had bestowed upon him a curse, but it also compensated for his rare talent. The experiences in Necrovia awakened the faint ghoul blood within him, and under the catalyst of dissolution, he completed his transformation.
The tombstones around him danced clumsily like a crowd celebrating together. The enormous Bloody Moon opened its crimson eye and smiled at him. His art does not belong to this world; now, he knows where he is destined to go.
Pickman lay atop his own coffin, spreading his arms wide, joining in this lonely revelry.
He is about to leave the city of humanity and go to the promised land that belongs to him.
He knows that everything he seeks is waiting for him there.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
A monster. A freak. Demon with the blood of monsters. Madman who desecrates art...
Before Pickman, a canvas unfurls, and the curses mix with other sounds, smearing brilliant colors across the canvas.
He leaps into this dazzling world, wandering in the mad colors. He eagerly hopes to lead others into his painting, to let them witness the most authentic visage of the world.
The sharp spikes of reality pierce his heart countless times; mutual understanding between people is more challenging than opening the boundaries of two worlds.
From humans to ghouls, from human cities to Necropolis, the road is long and winding. He has been wounded time and again, yet he rises again and again. He has long understood that there is neither truth nor falsehood. Pain and madness are the truth of the world. And his duty is to depict all of this faithfully.
The meaning of depiction lies only in the act of depicting itself.
He dedicates all his life and passion to painting. His expectations rarely receive a response; his pursuit never ceases.

profile.title: Pollux
profile.introduction: Periodically enhance card effects using Pollux's Radiant Light. The authority of the Divus can judge all dark evils.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
A pious and noble Divus of the Lightbearer, his {Sacred Heart} perpetually yearning for the dawn. He harnesses divine brilliance to periodically enhance card effects and wields the authority of a Divus to pass judgment upon all sins that dwell in shadow.
In this stage, Pollux debuts at level 50 with skill level 5 and enlightenment 3, gaining 50 aliemus and 1 "Insight" each turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
He is flesh born from a twisted existence, made up of skin that has been cut and sewn, an empty chest cavity, a tainted heart, and invisible chains.
He is placed on a pedestal, worshipped at his feet, claimed to possess a pure soul.
What exactly is he? Lost in countless dreams, he asks in confusion; among the dark green lake water and the empty fog, only his voice echoes lonely.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Considering the potential relationship between Pollux's "Sacred Heart" and the Church of the Lightbearers' divine ritual, Mythag formed a research team to conduct a series of inspections and experiments on the heart.
 To Pollux's regret, the "Sacred Heart" had entirely replaced his original heart's function, becoming integrated into his body, making direct removal or replacement impossible.
During observational experiments, researchers discovered that the "Sacred Heart," which resembles an eye pupil, often exhibited subtle tremors and rotations independent of Pollux's will, much like a real eye.
However, light source testing proved the "Sacred Heart" did not possess visual functions. Instead, this activity appeared to be sensitivity to other living beings or objects. In comparative trials with varied items, proximity to items related to dissolution and the silver core triggered the heart's activity more easily.
Its activity was particularly pronounced in front of Dissolutes and Awakeners. After Pollux actively used the "Sacred Heart's" power, the physical and chemical properties of ordinary items remained unchanged. However, when directed at dissoluted items and creatures, a remarkable expansion of dissolution occurred. Living organisms visibly in the early stages of dissolution rapidly accelerated towards the late stages. The more severe the dissolution originally suffered by the organisms, the faster this spread occurred.
In experiments with strict protective measures on Awakeners, the subjects reported a sense of approaching dark danger when faced with the "Sacred Heart's" power, prompting the experiment to be quickly halted.
After a stern explanation from the school, Pollux promised not to use his power against Awakeners. Due to Pollux's cooperation and excellent self-control, Mythag did not impose mandatory controlling items on him, but instead arranged regular health checks and power training.
It is said that after hearing the experimental results, Awakener Winkle almost fainted screaming and posted notices prohibiting Pollux from approaching on the doors of her laboratory and dormitory. The school repeatedly tried to persuade her to no avail, and Pollux graciously stated that he would respect Winkle's wishes.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Compared to Castor's active and studious attitude, Pollux's approach to the world is one of quiet vigilance.
He observes, he thinks, he doubts. It was his meticulous consideration and caution that once enabled their successful escape from the church's heavy control. From a young age, he learned to gauge the intentions of others, to lie, and to hide his true thoughts behind a disarming smile. Even with his intimately close brother, he would subconsciously use a relaxed demeanor to soothe and reassure.
"It's okay."
"It will be alright."
Beneath the shadow of a giant, the weak must exhaust every means to grasp a single glimmer of hope.
Having endured a long period of forced forgetting and deception, he now tolerates no danger or mistakes. Even after heeding Thais's advice and coming to Mythag, he remained profoundly vigilant for a long time.
To other Awakeners, Pollux appears as a quiet, patient, and friendly individual who often offers a gentle smile. But seasoned elders who see him can only sigh at the profound difference between the two brothers.
According to Thais, even in her presence, Pollux will intentionally or unintentionally position himself between her and Castor—since reclaiming his memory, he no longer wishes for Castor to always be his sole protector.
Jenkin once earnestly complained to the Keeper about Pollux's duality, claiming that when she "accidentally" picked up an item belonging to Castor, she witnessed his expression turn terrifying in an instant. "His usual good temper must be fake! He's the type who would eat children in private!"
However, after witnessing the Keeper's true power, Pollux's attitude underwent a complete transformation. He claims to have learned the truth of the world from that great soul and has now taken the Keeper as his new, unwavering faith.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
In the original design of the God of Fate, Pollux was probably never meant to be born.
Initially, only Castor had been successfully created in the artificial Twin Divi experiment. Just as this single, clueless embryo was to be disposed of, Pollux struggled and twisted himself free from his brother's flesh. He absorbed his life force, yet in doing so, he also saved it—from that moment on, their flesh, souls, and fates were irrevocably intertwined.
In the monotonous routine of the church's teaching, their lives and destinies were identical. But with the start of the "Holy Transformation" ritual, the pain and ruthless torment awakened something within Pollux—a defiance of his ordained fate as a Divus. The two children committed the boldest act of their lives: they sketched a world map from an outdated book and escaped from one false world to another imagined one.
After the fragile, fleeting beautiful dream shattered, the cruel punishment that followed did not crush their souls, yet that merciless fork in fate had already driven them apart.
Juliette ordered the two to be separated and "re-educated." Castor, submerged in the pool of sin, remained unaware that the tortures they had imagined were never inflicted upon Pollux. Instead, with an air of near mercy, Juliette ended the physical punishment and soon released Pollux from the dark room's confinement, steering him instead toward intense and rigorous theological teachings.
Pollux's unyielding doubts transformed into a deep-seated unease and fear under Juliette's utterly calm gaze; she must have mastered a method of control he could not resist, to appear so completely at ease.
He struggled, he disguised himself, and he never gave up. It seemed he did so many things... but what, in the end, had he truly accomplished? In the mirror, only the soul of a stranger remained, dutifully wearing the crown of the Divus.
He, and all their shared memories, had been completely forgotten.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
His entire life was lived under manipulated strings, and the script written long ago was a laughable lie that made him constantly lose, forget, and miss out. 
 When he was young, what he desired most was to freely flap his wings and see the world beyond that lake. 
 It wasn't until he lost everything for this wish that he could no longer regard such a light and carefree life as his ultimate dream. 
 Endless rage ignited that pitch-black heart, and the pain and hatred he repressed like blood needed to be washed away with the deaths of those false believers.

profile.title: Ramona
profile.introduction: Provides your team with essential card support, then pierces through rivals with gradually accelerating sword techniques.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
The Awakener's file manager has transferred her file to the student administration several times, but she has repeatedly sent it back herself, burying it under a mountain of documents.
"Just leave it here. It will eventually need to be delivered."
Her tone remains as calm as ever, but her gaze flickers unpredictably.

What monumental emotions lie beneath this calm?

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
When Ramona first appeared on campus, it stirred up a huge commotion.
At Mythag University, where Awakeners walked everywhere and peculiar people were common, having silver hair and heterochromia was not noteworthy. What made Ramona the topic of conversation was her identity as the "professor's daughter." 
Everyone crowded outside the mathematics department's office, hoping to catch a glimpse of Miss Dexter. There were so many people that the professor had to seek help from Miss Lotan to disperse the crowd. 
Once Ramona grew a bit older, and the professor finally felt comfortable letting her run around campus, those students who were sent away by Lotan found opportunities to speak to her. 
"What kind of person is the Noble Lady? Sorry, that character doesn't exist yet. I'm an orphan; my father kindly adopted me." 
To escape the entanglement, the little girl calmly revealed the truth, leaving everyone in guilt as she gracefully walked away.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
At the age of ten, Ramona became a regular at the university training grounds. Daring to accept training from the Black-clad Swordsman despite being human, she earned the admiration of many investigators.
After officially beginning her prerequisite courses, her eidetic memory, perfect test scores, and elegant yet deadly swordsmanship elevated her status at Mythag. Discipline and excellence became her defining traits.
However, many were puzzled as to why, despite her outstanding abilities, Ramona never received solo missions. Having been admitted early, she spent a full five years in prerequisite courses, watching cohort after cohort of investigators enter the world of mysticism.
What was she waiting for? What mission had been entrusted to her?
The answer came when the new Keeper enrolled.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Dreams are projections of memories, reflections of daily life, and a summation of human existence.
Since childhood, Ramona has often had the same dream.
Immersed in warm, turbid water, she felt an intimate connection with something unknown, while hollow and monotonous drumbeats rippled through the water.
Upon waking, she always felt an emptiness, as if something had been taken away or erased from her life.
But to avoid worrying her father and troubling her already busy teachers, Ramona swallowed the questions that nearly escaped her throat and returned to her daily routine.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
She knows everything.
She knows the bell tower rings in emergencies, that the Eridanus meteor shower will return in ten years, and that she possesses a unique power.
She knows nothing.
She doesn't know the secret spice in the cafeteria's curry recipe, how to calculate the radius of a planet after its annihilation, or the direction her future will take.
She finds it hard to let go of everything around her, yet cannot resist the path her intuition reveals.
