On the journey toward truth and normalcy, Ramona needs steadfast companions to help her secure the road back to everyday life.

profile.title: Ryker
profile.introduction: Draws a large number of cards and win Chaotic Legacy Relics by obtaining Black Sigils.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Ryker's Exalt and Skills yield randomized results; the better your luck, the greater the rewards.
{Rouse} grants or allows selection of upgrades for {Chaotic Legacy}.
In this stage, Ryker and Lotan will be deployed at Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlighten Level 3. Ryker initially has a 25% Crit. Rate boost, all {Chaotic Legacy} Relics, and an additional set of Cards. The enemy receives Temporary Strengthening in the first round.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Ryker is known as the "Lucky Detective" by his detective friends.
As the name suggests, he is a lucky person: whenever he places a bet, he wins ten times the stake; and whenever he guesses, he chooses the right answer.
The "Lucky Detective" doesn't have to be an actual detective; he stumbled upon solving many cases just by chance, and so he was embraced on this path.
He doesn't solve cases by deduction, but relies on luck. Just a tour around the crime scene and he stumbles upon the culprit; just walking along the road, he picks up the key evidence.
With such good fortune, he has spent twenty-five smooth years.
He never worries about yesterday, nor frets about tomorrow.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The Silver from Ryker's awakening was excavated from the interior of the renowned Remouahe monument, the Triangular Giant Tower. It is streamlined and perfectly bullet-shaped, matching the size and form of bullets used in the SAA revolver, embedded within the skull of a young male corpse.
Touching this Silver evokes magnificent and grand visions of the Triangular Giant Tower, causing the body to instinctively curl up and crawl. If the toucher resists desperately and looks ahead, they will see the blurred figure of a crowned man backlit, followed by an instant interruption of consciousness, causing the Silver to naturally slip from their fingers.
"The tip of the Triangular Tower has not yet been eroded; it should be a scene soon after its completion."—Researcher B.R. Longman
"I can hear horrifying whispers from dark souls."—Researcher K. Waters
"I must denounce the inhumane experiments arranged for us by Mythag. The side effects of repeatedly touching this Silver are enough to cause complete loss of bodily control. In short, I refuse to touch it again."—Researcher L. Lopez

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
After the @3@1 Event, Ryker became a special advisor at Mythag University.
Ryker is a competent advisor. From the mysterious disappearance of professors or inexplicable damage to instruments, to trivial matters such as searching for cats and dogs, Ryker can patiently and swiftly deliver satisfactory results.
Soon, due to his unique appearance, dignified and elegant demeanor, and helpful habits, Ryker became an idol figure beloved by countless young men and women in Mythag.
Professor Dexter once lamented, "The spotlight has been stolen by the youth."
But as he gradually became integrated into the campus, everyone noticed that Ryker's time in front of people began to decrease.
Meanwhile, in a certain abandoned basement of Mythag, the sound of dice rolling began to occur frequently.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
At the age of twenty-five, Ryker encountered a strange suspect while investigating a murder case in a locked room.
After obtaining an absolutely complete chain of evidence, the suspect mysteriously vanished from the police station, with the handcuffs still intact. Of course, this was the responsibility of the on-duty police, but Ryker still received his reward.
Ryker took this reward and traveled overseas for a vacation. He then encountered the fleeing suspect by a stall. The suspect was placing bets, and upon seeing Ryker, remained unfazed, merely inviting him to join the game and promising that if Ryker won, he would willingly surrender.

Ryker didn't win a single round.

He lost his pipe, lost his tie, lost his hat, and lost his trench coat.
When he was completely naked, the suspect returned all his clothes to him, stating that those stakes were too small. Without hesitation, Ryker asked what the suspect wanted to bet on and readily agreed when the other proposed wagering an arm.

After several additional stakes, the suspect pulled out a revolver. Facing a life-or-death gamble, Ryker picked up the gun without hesitation and aimed it at his temple.
After a gunshot, Ryker collapsed. The suspect summoned back his undead to mock his ignorance. However, Ryker's first words were, "What are we betting on next?"

His arrogance finally piqued the demon's interest. The suspect, whose true name was "N@2@3," decided to let the wager continue.

"N" produced three top hats and told him to make the "best choice" that would allow him to restore his body.
Ryker chose a hat, and "N" opened one that was empty, asking if he wanted to change his choice. Ryker shook his head.

- Don't you understand probability?
- I just believe in my luck.

"N" revealed the third top hat, which was also empty. Finally, he lifted Ryker's hat, and inside was a mass of strange black mist. The mist surged toward Ryker, transforming into a bizarre beastly form. "N" replaced him with the body that had already been wagered.

- For one with the keen sense of a beast, isn't this incarnation a perfect match? So, this is the "best choice"!

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
When the mountaineer reaches the peak of Elworth.
When the competitor lifts the world's number one trophy.
When the devout listens to the Revelation in dreams.
When victory comes too easily, the accompanying satisfaction may turn into bland inertia.
Looking around, there are no opponents, no fun at all.
On the surface, Ryker yearns for an exhilarating game, craving the heart-pounding moment after an ALL-IN, desiring the surge of adrenaline.
"There is no need to say those things. Only I understand you. What you truly desire is simply failure.
Let me grant you the sweetest failure."

profile.title: Salvador
profile.introduction: Accumulates infinite benevolence in the Crimson Furnace, providing emergency support when an ally is injured.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Unique {Crimson Furnace} accumulation from Command Cards, HP, and even enemy Damage.
Can flexibly convert furnace recovery into substantial Shields or Damage.
In this quest, a level 50 Salvador with skill level 5 and Enlighten 3 will join, starting with 50% Crit. Rate, gaining 20 Aliemus each turn, but enemies receive 150 Temporary {STR} in the first two turns.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
You need not fear his black eye patch; the smothering darkness will not obscure his boundless compassion. The sightless body may have perished, yet the will for redemption endures. Before him, everything should be loved, and everything will be loved in turn.
The words of the Our Father are forever engraved onto the Tablet, and through these blessed flesh and bones, He will bestow upon you "The Blessing".

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Report on Anomalous Events in the Londinium Church District (Confidential)

A.F. 315, February 3rd: During the anomalous event in the Londinium Church District, Bishop Salvador utilized the power of "The Blessing" to heal the Mythag Keeper and the residents of Londinium, exposing his identity as an Awakener in the process.
Due to the insufficient spirit power of the linker, Bishop Salvador chose to exhaust himself to sustain "The Blessing" until the Keeper and the Awakener Ogier shattered the core of said anomaly—the Ritual Mirror. Subsequently, the Keeper took over Salvador's link, and "The Blessing" healed all individuals present, preventing any casualties.
It was discovered that Bishop Salvador's original linker was the former Bishop of the Church District, now the Archbishop of Londinium ■■■■■.
In A.F. 300, the Archbishop had accidentally awakened Salvador after coming into contact with the Holy Ash. Over the next five years, Salvador had served as an assistant priest in the Church District, succeeding him as the Bishop of the Church District following ■■■■■'s promotion.
After the incident, the Keeper agreed to keep Bishop Salvador's Awakener identity confidential; thus, this information was not shared with the Mystia Treaty Committee. Apart from the core personnel of Mythag University, no one is currently aware of his identity as an Awakener.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Every time His Excellency the Bishop visits Mythag University, he is warmly welcomed by teachers and students alike.
The young Awakeners would tug at his hand, asking him to tell more stories from before the Flood. Students would also seek his counsel, inquiring if the Bishop would lend an ear to their troubles and woes. Even the seemingly cold and strong Awakeners and knowledgable professors approach the Bishop when he has free time: either to share their nightmares or to discuss the truths of the Soul.
His patience is unending, never tiring; Anyone may find acceptance and warmth with him.
"Of course, my child, is there aught I can do for you?"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
"...In the southern Constante Federation, there was told a legend of 'The Blessing Bishop.' Before the Flood, 'The Blessing Bishop' came from the Central Church, and in his devotion and kindness, established the only orphanage in the area, sheltering the many sick and displaced refugees during a devastating plague.
The plague was fierce, sparing noone from its wrath. The bishop prayed day and night, eventually receiving the Father's blessing—a life-giving tablet. He performed miracles, resurrecting the dead and healing those gravely ill from the plague, using his own flesh and blood as divine offerings. Yet regardless of his miracles, this benevolent bishop was framed as a cohort of demons, and ultimately sentenced to death by burning at the stake.
Coincidentally, during the same period, there were reports of a 'Blood Heretic' in the central @2 (now near Bondos in the southern Constante Federation). According to the 'Continental Inquisition Heresy Records,' this heretic held a tablet marked with demonic symbols, performing rituals with flesh and blood, and conducting wicked ceremonies on moonless nights. The heretic was captured by the Inquisition in 107 BD,and in his sincere repentance to the Father, was also sentenced to death by burning at the stake.
 Undeniably, there are many similarities between 'The Blessing Bishop' and the 'Blood Heretic': The same era, same region, same use of tablets and rituals. Considering the Inquisition's strong subjectivity, it is likely they stigmatized certain individuals as heretics. If we were to believe coincidences, the legendary 'Blessing Bishop' might have indeed been real, possibly even the 'Blood Heretic' recorded by the Inquisition..."
—Excerpt from 'Our Gods: Religion and Folk Beliefs in Southern Constante Federation'

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
"The benevolent Father once said: Cherish all things within the sky, on the ground, in the water, and in the soil. For all beings are my offspring.
"Thus, I say: Render all suffering unto me. Let those tormented by life or plagued by disease be made whole. Let all pain and madness melt as frost before the rising sun. Let you, my brethren, receive joy everlasting. 
"Burn me, grind my bones to dust, and rend my flesh asunder. For as long as you can find solace, I have little complaints."
—B.F. 1@1, May 30, Sal @1doMartin @2I @1as, left at @3 the Tribunal

profile.title: Sanga
profile.introduction: Creates stone sculptures to defend against subsequent attacks, with "The Lost Art" dealing more damage as the Shield increases.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Has powerful Defense and the ability to permanently generate Tentacles; more Tentacles mean stronger defense.
Exalt deals AoE DMG, and Shield increase the amount of DMG dealt.
In this quest, a level 50 Sanga with skill level 5 and Enlighten level 3 will join. Enemies will be strengthened in the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
The artist from Cité cares not for other people. She only pursues the absolute form of purified beauty, which etching the ephemeral moments into eternity.
As her inspiration surging forth, her chisel never ceases; as her works witnessing completion, her body submerges in coral-colored shades. In the corner of her workshop which she pays no heed, her agony and delight intertwine, sprouting from her very existence.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
"In the year of A.F.3@2, the Marine Ecology Team of Mythag University discovered an oddly shaped coral reef while conducting species observations in the southern seas...
"Have found peculiar fragments; probably consist of elements wholly unspotted on Earth. Flexibility preserved in water, but hardened quickly after exposed to air. The joint research conducted by the Biology and Astronomy departments suggests that it is highly likely to be classified as unknown extraterrestrial material..."

"Awakened at A.F.3@1, awaken code-name Sanga.
The sculpting behaviour has been performed since awakening. The resulting art style, known for its bizarre surfaces and grotesque lines, is difficult to attribute to any specific genre. Though constantly unsettling its viewers, those works stay far from helping the dissolution research."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"At the day of @1 @1, the university cemetery was vandalized again. This marks the third grave-robbing incident this month.
After weeks of investigation, the Awakener Sanga has been preliminarily identified as the suspect by the association.
No doubt anyone visiting her workshop would be shocked by those various grotesque and blasphemous ocean-themed sculptures before questioning their material sources: the shell necklace Natasha wore for her burial; Aberdeen granite that shared similarities with tombstones. Unlike Pickman, who loves explaining his artistic concepts, Sanga is more elusive—our all queries resulting in nothing more than a smile.
Don't touch her chisel, and don't stay alone with her in her workshop—Professor Dexter warned us. However, radical measures are necessary to uncover the truth behind the grave robberies.
It's 00:45 on @1 @1. Member @2 of the Detective Enthusiast Association has sneaked into Sanga's workshop. Wish me good @3"
—Excerpt from "Detective Enthusiast Association, Investigation Record S-618"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Cité Art Salon Exhibition, A.F.2@1.
"Let me take her home!" The assailant shouted while hammering at the sculptor August's final work, "The Nameless Ms. Coral," before hastily stopped by the congregation. However, this unstrung old man had surprisingly turned out to be none other than the long-reclusive August himself.
For a time, speculations and discussions about "The Nameless Ms. Coral" flooded in.
Some people said it was inspired by a female apprentice named Sanga, who was the actual owner of August's best works. August is merely a shameful plagiarist.
Some people said that Sanga came from an illustrious family—her father was a renowned writer, and her brother was a diplomat. In order to pursue the sculpture art, she disengaged with her family and set alone to Paris, living penuriously until her death.
Some people said Sanga suffered from a strange illness. Ominous black ooze streamed on her body, and her each carving transformed a part of her flesh into coral. "The Nameless Ms. Coral" was actually her coraled appearance, not a sculpture at all.
Other people said...
The apprentice, the devoted, the patient. Words pile upon Ms. Coral, sculpting her as the crowd dispersed from the exhibition hall. She blinks in the pouring moonlight. She's still waiting, and waiting—for her moment of returning home.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Watched by countless nights, the sculptor suffered from Dissolution slips beneath the murmuring waves, falling into writhing dreams:
As Lemuria's spirals inflamed by her kindreds, its majestic walls arise in her hands.
As the Father and His servants slumbering into epics, their statues find immortality under her carving.
Her senses dull. Her body stiffens. Her chisel nearly escapes.
Coral-like white mists have crept to her forearms. Hands tremble under her gaze. A smile flickers. Her life will fade like an extinguishing candle, while her body will step beyond eternal's door.
Her wish fullfilled as she sinking onto the seabed. Coral shattering to foam, black slime of Dissolution threads into the ocean—she finally embraces the City long-lost in currents, and mingles with its splendor—together, ever after.

profile.title: Sorel
profile.introduction: Deals high-frequency damage with a graceful waltz and heals itself with the enemy's blood.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
The lower the HP, the stronger the DMG and Healing.
Stockpile embryos in advance, and unleash a burst of Aliemus through infinite {Devour} to deal massive DMG at once.
In this level, Sorel will be brought into battle with a carrying Level of 50, Skill Level 5, and 3 Enlighten, starting with 40% HP.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
A silver tongue is the nectar exuded by budding flowers, and courtiers are the bees and butterflies that flit around their swaying petals.
Thorns born of desire have unknowingly entwined the flower-decorated window tightly.
Even if her character is condemned by the world and her hands bleed from the cuts of the crown, she will never relinquish the power she has embraced.
After all, this is the true nourishment that allows the rose to bloom.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
"The Salon" has quietly risen in the upper class of Londinium at an unknown time.
In this mysterious salon, some have had the chance to receive portraits painted by masters, some have dined with the Prime Minister, and others have even received a promise from the Queen herself.
The organizer, the venue, and the timing of the event remain a mystery. People only know that everyone can obtain what they desire most in the salon.
But no one knows what kind of guest will earn the host's favor and receive an invitation.
Even the eloquent Lord Dexter was turned away by the salon's master.
"I'm very sorry, but the host currently prefers to invite artistic luminaries."
"Nobles? Please await future invitations."

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
At first, it was just a glass of grape soda that replaced red wine.
In an unnoticed corner, Sorel appeared pitiful, drinking with two or three students.
Then, it was a small afternoon tea.
In the student cafeteria, Sorel hesitated over the macarons pushed towards her, revealing a hint of reluctance and sincerity.
Finally, it was a grand salon, the blooming of a rose.
The lounge was adorned with flowers and jewels, brilliant roses mingling with naive students, and everyone enjoyed themselves.
The newly appointed Keeper witnessed all this and silently prayed that her next step would not be "becoming the principal of Mythag University."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Sorel knows she is beautiful, and in her heart, she regards this beauty as a gift from heaven.
As long as she pleads with tearful eyes, people will show her pity; by simply acting pitiful, those who admire her will unconditionally take her side.
However, beauty is worthless in the face of true power.
For the first time, facing parents who wish to trade her to nobility for their own future, Sorel's tears tasted the bitterness of failure.
Is there anyone who can help her? Anyone who can take her away from her ignorant and backward fate?
Is there something, somewhere, that can transform her beauty into a real weapon?
As Sorel sobs with her face buried in her hands, an agent of deep destiny arrives before her.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Even now, as an Awakener, Sorel cannot forget those nobles who fawned over her; she cannot forget their faces, one after another, etched with flattery.

How utterly nauseating those smiling faces were: slick, mercenary, every one of them oozing desire and calculation, yet she had no choice but to weave a gentle web around each one.

She would weave the most meticulous of webs, reaching into every dark corner, winning over every person, seizing every piece of leverage. She had to be like water slowly brought to a boil, letting those people sink deeper and deeper, so that by the time they wished to remove their masks, they would find there was no escape.

But when she finally came to her senses, the one with no escape turned out to be herself.

No escape. No rest. No surrender.

Sorel has long since lost the right to turn back. Aside from standing above everyone, there is no other path for her to walk.

profile.title: Tawil
profile.introduction: Tawil knows all and can utilize any Skill for their own purposes, using Keyflare as a resource to launch attacks.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
The all-knowing sage, possessing infinite wisdom, skillfully masters all skills, searching for the best path among countless possibilities.
Can provide a large amount of Keyflare for the team and also convert Keyflare into winged strikes against enemies.
In this level, Tawil and Lotan will participate at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3, gaining 100 Aliemus and increasing Keyflare Regen by 50 at the start of battle.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: Tawil does not exist in the current world. Their existence would draw the attention of the 'Supreme Will,' so They cannot and must not exist. Wisdom and silence are all The Gatekeeper can offer to these tireless seekers. In Their daily vigil, Tawil quietly bestows Their wisdom, courage, and silent blessings upon the fate of humanity.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
In a mysterious dream, I first saw Them.
In an endless and foggy space, They guarded a majestic and eternal Gate linking countless vast universes. Beyond the deep cracks of the Gate, strange illusions flickered like undeciphered signals. Upon catching a signal, I felt my body vanish, aimlessly floating among the stars, enshrouded by an incredible darkness. I was terrified; the dream violently shook, the heavy Gate irresistibly opening, with countless sphere-like objects shining like stars beyond it. The vast and unfathomable scene made me dizzy... and the Gate's guardian—Tawil—silently and eternally watched me.
—Ward Phillips, Silver Key Notes

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Tawil has manifested in Mythag only three times.
The first time, Tawil ignited the spark of "Wisdom." The Gatekeeper opened the passage to dream, imparting rare Knowledge to the ignorant founders of Mythag. They provided Mythag's original "think tank," along with clues and insights about Dissolution.
The second time, Tawil delivered the prophecy of "Change." The Black Pool churned, welcoming the Arrival of the one who would "Transform" the world.
The third time, They transmitted the signal of "Finale." Their cries and wails resonated across the earth, yet could not prevent the inevitable descent of Chaos and Destruction.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Kadath is not the only dream Tawil seeks to protect.
The Gatekeeper has witnessed the birth and destruction of countless planets and galaxies, with the histories of past and future sedimented into knowledge, absorbed into the Ultimate Gate behind Them. With a gaze full of compassion, Tawil looks down upon these scenes, which dissolve into fleeting glimpses in Their dreams, vanishing into the darkness of the boundless abyss.
Tawil once stepped onto a piece of land, but in the moment They glanced back, everything They cherished crumbled beneath the breath of the "Supreme Will."
After endless repetitions, They finally realized a harsh truth: Their very existence serves as the prelude to a nightmare; Their immense presence alone draws the gaze of the "Supreme Will." Their interference would only bring deeper misfortune to the world They hold dear.
Thus, Tawil retreated. Tawil fell silent.
Until They perceived a glimmer of change emanating from "It" within the drifting fragments of consciousness in the infinite expanse...

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content: As the Aeon of Sagacity's Proxy and Gatekeeper, Tawil has not lacked the desire to explore Their own essence. Tawil yearns to walk the earth like those The Gatekeeper watches over, witnessing the changes in civilization and the vicissitudes of history, gathering all They see into a treasury called 'Omniscience.' But Their existence does not allow Them to abandon Their post. Deep in Tawil's heart, perhaps what They envy most is the girl who carries a thread of Their will...

profile.title: Thais
profile.introduction: She gives birth to scions of the Holy Tree to allow more Devour triggers, while her Ritual of Abundance produces copies of skill cards.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Thais boasts the unique ability to copy Cards, and those copied Cards come with a reduced Arithmetica Cost.
Upon {Rouse}, gain {Scion of Purity}. When consumed by {Caro} characters' Exalt, it triggers the Devouring effect twice. When unleashing Posse, convert {Embryo} into {Scion of Purity}.
In this stage, you'll deploy Awakener "Thais" and "Ramona" (Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlighten Level 3), alongside the Tiny Wish Posse.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Adorned with jewels, draped in delicate veils, her heart concealed beneath layers of beauty.
Many think Thais is a vain and frivolous person, but perhaps this is merely one of the masks she wears.
"Hey, what are you writing? Let me help you—"

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The flood that swept across the world carried away the sediment of civilization but also washed away the dust of history. 
After the disaster, people caught glimpses of fragments of forgotten history from the ruins of Mosul. 
The "Tree" stands at the confluence of two rivers, flowing with bright red sap, irrigating civilization as it flows downstream. 
The "Tree" is eternal, evergreen for millennia. 
Scholars of ancient history ultimately concluded that the "Tree" does not exist as a physical entity; rather, it is a metaphor for ancient civilization and a spiritual symbol. 
In unseen corners, seeds take root everywhere in the world.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Mythag awakens and contains many Awakeners, but none are as unique as Thais.
She helps female students choose makeup for dates, she helps the disheartened regain hope, and she helps Investigators who are mentally crushed step out of the shadows.
Like a lover, a sister, and even more like a mother, she always manages to construct a quagmire called "Love", quietly drawing people in and making them unconsciously willing to give everything for her—
After preventing students from drinking the mysterious liquid provided by Thais for the third time, the Awakener Management issued a restraining order against her.
"Thais is strictly prohibited from approaching all students without the escort of a Keeper."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
@2This name has been recorded multiple times in history.
Priests, mothers, queens, and witches... this name has been adorned with too many different titles, making it difficult for historians to determine which identity truly represents the Awakener we know today.
Thais herself does not hesitate to share her past with us — it is all her, yet not entirely her.
"A noble birth defines her."
Thais will always be the most radiant girl in the Temple, as the child of the High Priest, she is as graceful as a swan and as gentle as moonlight.
"Kneeling before the Tree defines her."
Thais is the brightest leaf on the "Tree," her devotion has earned her the blessings of the "Tree." 
"Having her blood fully drained defines her."
In the face of disaster, when the buildings collapsed, the ancient and ignorant civilization chose the best among them — her.
"Flowing with tree sap in her veins defines her."
The revered "Tree" makes its own choices. Its branches take root within her, granting believers nearly eternal life.
"But desiring the destruction of civilization does not define her."
What Thais desires has never been the end of the world.
"But Miss Thais, what do you truly desire?"
"Ah, didn't we agree? When the fruit between us ripens, you will know the answer."

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
It is real.
It is a gaze cast by God, a branch extending into the universe.
It delights in the grandeur of history and hungers for the prosperity of civilization.
It seeks to transcend withering and overcome death.
She desires deep chanting, sandy soil and salty wind, silver leaves, and Mosul's moonlight.
Harlot, witch, sorceress... These epithets from mankind mean nothing.
If even a single drop of blood escapes the black encroachment, they will reclaim eternity.

profile.title: Tinct
profile.introduction: Uses transcendent singing to reduce the Arithmetica Cost of a large number of cards in hand and boosts teammates' battle morale.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
{Rouse} can grant Tinct all Command Cards with random effects, randomly obtaining {Weakness}, {Vulnerable}, Draw, Arithmetica, and Aliemus abilities.
Exalt can reduce the Arithmetica Cost of five Hand cards by 1 while also enhancing Tinct's base "Strike" DMG.
By accumulating Strength and base "Strike" DMG, using "Strike" can deal massive Damage.
In this level, you will carry a Level 50 Tinct with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlightenments, gaining 15 Aliemus each turn, and the enemy will gain Enhance on the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
She is the singer among the stars, the shimmering stardust.
She is the bird trapped in a gilded cage, the discarded phonograph.
When her home planet collapse into the void, she is compelled to embrace the hope of life, becoming a wanderer in the unclaimed depths of space, an eternal seeker singing in search of home.
"Sister... Though I am the one who can walk freely among us, you have always been the one supporting me."

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
In the "P-063 Opera House @2 Event," a metal box was discovered in a corner of the burnt backstage, and the Silver inside was recovered.
The current instruments at Mythag University are unable to identify the material of the box. After consulting @2 documents and analyzing its decorative engravings, Professor Doll concluded that the box might originate from an extinct civilization that perished in an explosion centuries ago.
The Silver remains quiet and unremarkable while inside the box but emits a continuous humming sound when removed. Professor Doll suspects that the metal box functions as a signal blocker, preventing special frequency signals from resonating with the Silver.
The model of the resonating Silver has also been identified.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
After reuniting with Faint, Tinct settled down at Mythag University. After being injured again in the P-063 @ 2 Event, Tinct became even more fearful of strangers, leading her to become reclusive. Unlike her powerful and melodious singing voice, her everyday conversational tone is sweet and clear, but few have had the chance to hear it.
Some well-meaning individuals attempted to promote Tinct's performances on campus, even finding posters from her early opera house performances and plastering them around the campus.
Tinct is unwilling to cater to these wishes; such enthusiasm only stirs up memories of a past she wants to forget.
"End this absurd tragedy. As long as I'm by your side, I have no other desires. If possible, I wouldn't mind being forgotten by this world."
"Don't, Tinct. The days we will spend together are still long ahead. This is no longer our home planet, and I am no longer your burden. You should find the things you want to do and live for yourself."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Unlike her obviously petite sister, Tinct's deformity manifests in other ways.
Whether it be mistletoe or hermit crabs, Tinct resembles a spirit body unable to survive without other individuals.
On the desolate planet, everything she did was to cure Faint's illness; she was willing to try any possible way to sustain her sister's life, even at the cost of enduring what ordinary people could not bear.
She paid no heed to the nobles' mockery and humiliation, always conducting unequal trades with them calmly and placidly, even pleading for their mercy—which granted her a seat to escape the desolate planet. The moment she secured it, she gave it to Faint without hesitation.
Yet, at the last moment, Faint pushed Tinct onto the star stream fleeing the desolate planet—hoping her sister could finally free herself and start anew on a new planet.
However, after the desolate planet's annihilation, Tinct embarked on a selfless drifting journey to find Faint, uncaring of being manipulated by others. Such Tinct was discovered by the @2 Church, and subsequently ended up in @3's hands.
Tinct never resisted. Rather, she hoped someone would tell her what was best to do—even if it were lies, deception, or an ephemeral illusion, it was better than standing paralyzed with no clue what to do.
At least, that suggested there was still hope for Faint's future.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
At this point, discussing "my own life" with me may be asking too much.
I have grown accustomed to dissipating into the high skies and concealing myself among the stars.
The person the onlookers are gazing at is not me; I am not the one being played with like a music box, nor the one drifting through the desolate starry sky. That is merely a tool selling its voice for survival, a walking corpse wandering in search of Faint. Only by believing that others' value far surpasses my own can I find meaning in all this effort.
So, at this point, please don't make me notice myself anymore.

"But, dear sister, don't dwell on those memories that have already passed.
Open your eyes and take a look—your one-of-a-kind song has already resounded throughout the universe."

profile.title: Tulu
profile.introduction: Commands Tentacles to subdue enemies and rapidly triggers explosive Tentacle damage.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Use cards and Exalt to trigger Tentacle growth bursts.
Utilize the abilities of the Tentacles to deal massive DMG to enemies.
Obtain additional Arithmetica and Aliemus in this stage.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
The young King slumbers upon his throne. Upon waking, he issues his decrees, his authority reaching every corner of the world.
Yet before the sun has set, the King is already weary. He has grown tired of his own power and transcendent wisdom; tired of the fanatical worship of his followers, and of the long dreams that have stretched across aeons.
None know what he witnessed during his long sleep, but they should be grateful for the bliss of their own ignorance.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Until now, the oldest known human civilization was believed to have originated in Mesopotamia, a fact supported by a wealth of solid archaeological evidence.
However, humanity is never content with the known. When facing the vast, black seas of infinity, we do not hesitate to set sail into the unknown.
Perhaps this is not the first time we have been granted such warnings or hints. Theosophists once proposed a chilling hypothesis: that the cosmos is an awe-inspiring cycle, and our current world—and humanity itself—is but a transient link within it.
Far too many things have perished silently in these cycles, leaving not a shred of evidence behind. This is, in fact, a mercy; some forces are so ancient and extreme that even their lingering vestiges could exert an immeasurable and devastating influence on our era.
Take, for instance, the accidental discovery made by the crew of the Vigilant on the floor of the Southern Ocean. It has pushed our understanding of prehistoric civilization back by tens of thousands of years.
From this, we have learned of a civilization—complete with its own state, monarch, and peculiar faith—that was swallowed by a global deluge and has long lain dormant beneath the waves.
Were it not for the Mystia Treaty Committee's assistance in containment and censorship, human history might have been rewritten. But the world as it stands is not yet prepared for such a cataclysmic revelation.

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Some Awakeners struggle to adapt to their new vessels and lives, while others embrace the change, pivoting eagerly into their second life.
Tulu belongs to neither category. Never has any Awakener displayed such blatant, naked indifference toward the modern world.
His aptitude is peerless; perhaps he has already peered through the veil of current crises and global shifts. Yet, he prefers to lose himself in the depths of sleep.
Despite having no desire for fame, he has become a figure of renown among both humans and Awakeners at the university. Researchers scrutinize his every move, churning out paper after paper on his behavior. The Departments of Archaeology and Astronomy frequently send invitations to academic symposia, almost always requesting him by name.
Tulu's influence among his fellow Awakeners is equally profound: Murphy grows sullen at the mere sight of him; Goliath seems perpetually locked in a silent, private rivalry with him; and Miriam treats his every word as divine law.
To all of this, he maintains a singular lack of affect—harboring no expectations, making no demands. He grants the occasional request only as the whim strikes him; otherwise, he remains unreachable.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
When he was chosen from among thousands of children and ascended the throne, Tulu was not even as tall as the blue stone throne.
Yet, no one dared to question his indifference or his perpetual slumber; all understood these to be the signs of a King listening to the divine.
The young King's decisions invariably solved every hardship, and his prophecies never failed to pass.
Tulu accepted this life: entering the dreams of a "God," meeting an existence beyond comprehension, ruling his nation through oracles, and sinking once more into sleep.
The gears of this civilization turned on the will of the divine. He was content to simply follow the current—to be nothing more than the sole "messenger" for a higher power.
However, Tulu held one prophecy back, the only one he never spoke: that the nation would be destroyed by a deluge, and their civilization would be entombed beneath the sea.
Because he knew all, he knew that their destruction was immutable. But he resolved that, in their final hours, they should not live in terror and despair.
This was his final decree—made not as an "Divine Envoy," but as a King.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
His transcendent wisdom exacts a heavy toll on his body; he must sleep for more than fifteen hours a day, and even in his waking hours, he is haunted by a persistent lethargy.
Within his dreams, he sometimes feels the fury of the gods; at other times, he glimpses the intersection of fractured timelines. Occasionally, he even wanders into the furthest depths, coming face-to-face with the Divine itself.
He has never sought their revelations or prophecies, yet no matter how he tries to evade them, the visions always come to pass.
He never asked for offerings. To be worshiped, to be feared—these were burdens thrust upon him by others, and they hold no interest for him.
To be reborn was nothing more than an unwelcome interruption of an eternal dream.
No need for invocation. No need for prayer.

profile.title: Uvhash
profile.introduction: A berserker who fights relentlessly and grows stronger, becoming even more powerful as their HP drops.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Exalt deals high AoE DMG and enjoys high {STR} bonus.
When Exalt triggers {Devour}, the subsequent Exalt DMG and {STR} Buff in this battle are increased.
The lower the HP, the higher the Damage.
In this level, Uvhash will join the battle at Level 50 with Skill Level 5 and 3 Enlightenments, gaining 30% {Embryo Fusion} each turn, and enemies will be Enhanced for the first two turns.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
How could we forget the hero Uvhash? He was the finest of men. The warrior knelt in the sunset sands, his warm blood soaking him. Vultures circled above, singing his dirge: 
No one will honor you with tears, no one will bury you with cries, for you have already made your name in Rome.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Silver Core Number: B-005
Excavation Time: A.F.1@1 Year@3 Month
Excavation Location: In the countryside of Flandre, in some nameless Roman tomb ruins
Details Recorded: The Silver Core holder was named Ludwig Plin, sentenced to burning by local religious judgment due to a summoning incident. Plin claimed to have obtained this Silver Core on the battlefield of the ninth holy crusade, and then went near Brabant to hold a bloody sacrifice for summoning "The Great Unspeakable One" with "The Extraterrestrial Servants" and "The Invisible Companions".
Recorder: Ward Phillips
— "Awakener Silver Core Acquisition Report: B-005"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
"4:30 Ran 6 miles along the Isis River. Argued with a passerby elderly woman and kicked a poodle.
......
12:00 Lunch: Honey pancakes, olive appetizers, and... five servings of roast lamb? Unbelievable.
13:00 Went to the Trout Fighting Club. Record: 10 wins, 0 losses, fastest time 3 seconds—opponent forfeited out of fear.
19:00 Watched "The Shepherd King" at Covent Theatre with the Keepers, got kicked out for loud political remarks.
22:00 Found loitering near St. Marian Hospital, likely planning to steal blood packs.
Observation ends, danger level: SSS."
—Excerpt from "Hannah's Awakener Observation Notes"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Caligula's court alchemist, Marcus Antis, created the "golem" at the cost of many living people.
Due to the imperfection of the alchemical ritual, he had to drink a large amount of blood daily. He killed political enemies for Caligula and helped him ascend as Roman Emperor.
The tyrant Caligula was murdered less than four years into his reign. He was also locked in the underground of the destroyed altar by enraged and fearful mobs, forced to endure hunger and thirst for nearly a thousand years.
Throughout the long years, he survived by feeding on the blood of rats, the thirst inherited from the depths of his bloodline driving him mad.
Not enough, far from enough...
During the @2 period, a Levantine wizard found him, promising to help him escape, provided he killed Lemuel warriors on the battlefield.
What a great deal!
He completed the task with ease, but there was a small hiccup: in his excitement, he accidentally killed everyone on the battlefield and drank their blood — including the wizard and himself.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
@2 Years, the ancient city of Akka.
After a millennium of hunger and thirst, it finally erupted—killing, drinking blood, killing, drinking blood. In the end, all that remained before him was an endless desert and countless dried corpses. Even vultures feared to approach him.
Kneeling in the yellow sand, he laughed heartily: Exhilarating! Truly @1 exhilarating!
Now, he himself would bring this massacre to a perfect end.
The warrior bit his wrist, drained his own blood, and collapsed into the crimson pool.

profile.title: Wanda
profile.introduction: Commanding deadly thorns, she establishes a formidable defense by stacking Counter effects, then unleashes them to deal DMG.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Wanda possesses powerful {Counter} abilities and unique effects that trigger {Leap}.
Play Cards to accumulate {Dreamlure}, then flexibly consume it to enhance her abilities. Stack {Counter} to weaken enemies and boost damage.
In this stage, "Wanda" (Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlighten Level 3) will be deployed.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content: Wanda is smart, rich, and beautiful. As she says, she lives for pleasure. She turns men into her slaves and places her moonlit, cold feet on their backs. The crowned Venus, she is the embodiment of evil and dreams; in this dark shadow, her slender figure shines.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Travel info: Curious History Exploration - Luxurious 7-Day Tour to Cambrian Castle
Date: A.F.3@2 June 18th-25th
Guide: Thomas @2@1
Brief itinerary:
Come on, uncover the mystery of the most infamous haunted castle from 300 years ago—Cambrian Castle!
We're tired of reading about the seductive lady and her twenty-five attendants in old books and silent films; it's all the same—so boring!
According to a Mythag expert who wishes to remain anonymous, there's been a new discovery in the area! @2@1 covered in thorns...
Call quickly @2@1@1. This trip will be unforgettable, chills guaranteed!
—Excerpt from "Worster Tour Group Disappearance CaseFile Summary: Evidence S-145"

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
On her first day at Mythag, Wanda was severely accused of "arbitrarily trampling on others".
According to a student who wished to remain anonymous, he just happened to pass by the library and accidentally made eye contact with Wanda, and was then thrown to the ground. @2 was put on @3 (the student refused to disclose more details).
The process of Damage took no more than five minutes, and the situation was severe and appalling.
The Mythag Disciplinary Committee (hereinafter referred to as MDC) expressed high concern and deep sympathy regarding this matter. A specialized task force has been established to conduct thorough ideological and behavioral reform for Wanda, in order to help her quickly integrate into campus life.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
At the onset of the terrible plague, Wanda ordered the castle gates to be welded shut, retreating into a life of complete isolation and indulgence.
There were jesters jesting and poets reciting spontaneously. There were musicians playing, as well as food and wine.
All joy and peace resided within the castle, while the outside was a world shrouded in @2 darkness.
Just as the area outside the walls became a wasteland, Wanda held an exceptionally luxurious masquerade for her hundreds of followers.
It was a three-day, three-night extravagant and debauched revelry. As the midnight bell tolled on the third day, some guests went completely mad, while others turned into puddles of black goo. Wanda's face cracked like porcelain, black @2 oozing from the seams of her mask.
Frightened followers huddled next to Wanda, trembling, but she remained calm.
With a whip, she commanded those who had gone completely mad to continue drinking and playing music without pause.
The bizarre masquerade ended on the fourth morning, and the opulent hall was filled with black @2 sludge and red blood.
No one knew what had transpired here.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
"Indulgence and lust lead to eternal fire as a warning..."
For 23 days, the monk stood outside her gate, reciting monotonous scriptures.
Wanda had him tied up and brought in.
The monk said, 'Why worry about suffering? Where the Lord's spirit is, there is freedom.'
She stripped him.
The monk said, 'Why worry about clothes? The lilies of the field neither spin nor bow.'
She severed his limbs.
The monk said, 'Why worry about blood? All flesh dies.'
Wanda laughed, 'I will not only make you bleed, but also turn you into my slave, forever.'
'Why?' the monk lifted his head, 'Shouldn't you show mercy to your companion as I showed mercy to you?'
Wanda studied the monk. His eyes were as blue as the clear sky.
She gouged out his eyes.
Unable to bear the torture, the monk eventually died. The local parish feared her wealth and power, and no one dared to accuse her.
Before dying, the monk said, '@2 is near, you should...'
Years passed, and Wanda had long forgotten the monk's face and name, only remembering his eyes. On the night she was about to be devoured by @2, she recalled the monk's unfinished words.
'@2 is near, you should...'
In her blurred consciousness, she speculated on the monk's last words, but found nothing."

profile.title: Winkle
profile.introduction: Provides Aliemus to other allies and spends all cards in hand to generate a large amount of Shield.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Possesses strong defense and the ability to provide Aliemus.
Exalt grants a significant amount of Aliemus to another Awakeners, enabling them to release more Exalts or even multiple Exalts in one turn.
{Rouse} grants {Counter} when applying Shield, providing {Counter} against multi-hit enemies.
In this quest, Winkle and Lotan, both at Level 50 with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten Level 3, will join. They gain benefits during trial battles, while enemies are strengthened in the first turn.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Winkle's office is known as the North Star of Mythag University. It is always brightly lit, with every potentially shadowy corner illuminated by various light sources—from lanterns to kerosene lamps, tungsten bulbs to candles.
Her fear of darkness even causes her to avoid sleep, so she sits beneath the largest lamp, writing incomprehensible symbols on scattered draft papers, day and night.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
Time: A.F. 299, @1, @2
Location: Lab UG-C03, @2@1, @4, Londinium.
Participants: @1, @2, @4, @3
Test Subject: W
Subject Info:
The body is an adult human female, born on June 18, A.F. 280, origin unknown. She died of illness while studying at @2 University and was subsequently occupied by the extraterrestrial spiritual entity @1, reviving her to continue her previous life.
@1 possesses the abilities of spiritual transfer and replacement and can visually perceive dissolution. Although human bodies cannot perceive dissolution, the abilities of @1's race allowed the subject to perceive its existence on a spiritual level. Therefore, when the subject first observed dissolution 2 years and 8 months ago, she attempted spiritual transfer to avoid further erosion.
Despite @1 retaining the ability to perceive dissolution, the subject could not escape its impact, resulting in failed spiritual migration. After multiple attempts, she actively contacted @3 University, hoping to become an Awakener, which directly led to this experiment.
—Excerpt from <Italic:"Experiment Record: Feasibility of Silver Extraction from Living Bodies">

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Hanna: How did you know that Mythag University could help you? You see, the general perception of us has always been "a remote ordinary university." Was it Ramona who told you?  
Winkle: Oh no, no, it wasn't. Even though she did lead me in, I don't have a deep relationship with Ramona. My race, @1, has the ability of cross-temporal and spatial mental migration, so we know many things... Knowledge about Mythag is just one of them.  
Hanna: So, you learned about the way to cross the Mist and directly knock on the door through that "Knowledge"?  
Winkle: I admit, I didn't know about the Mist. I was just walking, and somehow ended up here. Speaking of the Mist, do you know how terrifying that section of road by the entrance is? There are no streetlights, and when the fog settles, even the sunlight grows weaker—all those dark shadows...  
Hanna: Is that also the reason you don't like leaving the office—fear of shadows?  
Winkle: Shadows? No, no, you don't understand. Besides shadows, there's so much blackness, so many places where it could hide... it.  
Hanna: It?  
Winkle: Shh! Lower your voice! You'll bring it here—no, it's not intelligent, but why, why is it that even though I'm here @2@3@1  
Hanna: Miss Winkle, are you okay?  
Winkle: @2@3@1...  
Hanna: Miss Winkle?  
Winkle: Enough, get out! I don't want to be interviewed! Your shadow recorder notes are all black, black, black, black—get out, get out, get out!  
Hanna: Miss Winkle—  
—Excerpt from Hanna Peasley's recording titled "0810, Winkle Interview (Failed Attempt 1)"

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Winkle once had a name of her own.
That name was imbued with her parents' love,
Repeatedly written by friends as cherished memories,
Marked on the calendar alongside her birthday.

Winkle once had a face of her own.
That face grew under her parents' watchful eyes,
Expressing joy and sorrow with friends,
Captured in memories through photographs.

Winkle once had a home of her own,
Far from and unlike Kadath.
It vanished along with her parents' existence,
Devoured by a black @2 and then completely disappeared.

Winkle harbors a deep, overwhelming fear.
It is dark, terrifying, devouring everything, irrational.
It dissolves, erodes, and wears everything down completely,
When touched, existence itself turns to ash.

Winkle only retains a name she once had.
A human body cannot pronounce it, nor mimic it,
She closes her eyes in extreme fatigue,
Praying for a dream where she hears that familiar call once more.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Survival is both instinct and an inescapable burden. In the face of a death that erases memory, she alone is proof they ever existed—her parents, her friends, everything from her hometown.
So, she fled to an untainted world, to this ocean-covered planet, finding new life in the body of a deceased human girl.
But the black death has infinite patience.
Human eyes cannot see the Dissolution, but she knows what the cold at the edge of consciousness means, what the darkness in her mind represents—what humans call Dissolution has caught up, unknowingly binding her Spirit and Body.
She tried to flee over and over, only to fail each time, until utterly exhausted, her Spirit torn to its limit, she dragged her Body to the one place she hoped might offer salvation.
No matter what, she wanted to live.

profile.title: Xu
profile.introduction: Her intoxicating charm shatters the enemy's will to fight, and the bone-deep poison seeping through their body erupts violently the moment they succumb.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
An elegant and alluring Eastern "deity." When she lowers her fan, all beings fall entranced by her lethal charm.

The pleasure born of desire clings like a bone-deep {Poison}, luring mortals to willingly dissolve into silence within the vows of passion she bestows.
In this stage, you will deploy Xu and Ramona at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten Level 3. At turn start, place 1 "Adv. Insight" and 1 {Embryo} into hand; inflict 1 stack of {Spellbound} on all enemies.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Elegant, alluring—an Eastern "deity" of fatal charm.
Legend has it that when she lowers her fan, you will see the face of your most beloved reflected beneath.
Yet legends are merely legends. Pray that her gaze never falls upon you. Once she seeps into your memories like a pervasive night mist, nothing will remain of your mind but a delirious, hollow obsession.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
In A.F. @16@1, a cargo ship with no traceable voyage records or authorized paperwork silently docked at the Londinium Freight Harbor.
The vessel was later confirmed to have originated from a port in the ancient Panlong Empire of the Far East. On the night of its arrival, harbor workers and nearby residents reported a collective dream of an Eastern woman with raven hair, obsidian eyes, and skin like ivory.

Witnesses described her as both "fascinating" and "hauntingly familiar." Notably, several male witnesses exhibited catatonic symptoms while providing their accounts.
Mythag's Investigators searched the ship immediately. Strangely, despite pervasive sounds of giggling and high-level Gnostic Responses, no trace of a Silver Core was initially found.
The following day, Lord Dexter entered the hold. Inside a ceramic vase that had already been searched multiple times, he discovered a Silver Core enveloped in a thin, clinging mist. As of A.F. 314, this Silver Core was deemed "unawakenable" by all known methods.

It was not until the arrival of the current Keeper, @3, that the Silver Core awakened spontaneously. The resulting entity identifies herself as "Xu" and has requested a permanent link with @3.

—Excerpt from "Mythag University Silver Core Storage Records

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
Xu appeared on campus just as naturally as she once drifted through palaces, royal tents, and various societies in the Far East. No one can say for certain when she first appeared.
To all who meet her, she feels instinctively familiar—a long-lost friend, or perhaps a cherished lover—even if they have never shared a single moment of their lives.

Only a rare few among the Awakeners maintain a preternatural wariness of her. Or, more accurately, only those of exceptional power can detect the anomaly: Xu, clinging and pervasive like a mist, seeping into their memories.
 The Mythag Administration once debated whether she required strict containment, even considering the development of specialized countermeasures. Yet, Xu dissolved these anxieties with a series of clandestine deals and a single promise:

"My intentions do not lie with these little ones."

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
...To offer sacrifice where none is due is named Profane Rites. It is a delusion that devours the wealth of the people, persisting despite every decree of prohibition.

The coastal folk are steeped in the habit of venerating false gods, driven by a passion for raising rustic shrines and unhallowed altars. Vast fortunes are squandered while fields and looms lie fallow, yet the hamlets remain blind to the rot within. Time and again, the magistrates raze these dens to the ground, only for them to sprout anew the moment the garrison withdraws.

Most grievous are the foreign vagabonds. Settling among our villages, they have taken their own sacred icons and turned them upside down, distorting them to resemble the sign for a "Well"—the character "井".

The deities enshrined therein—both the masters and their attendants—possess verifiable histories; they are largely women who met with violent or unjust ends. In the beginning, the flock consisted of mere peasant women, stage-players, and common streetwalkers.

But soon, wife brings husband, mother brings son, and the prostitute brings her patron. Having swollen in rank, they form shadow factions for private gain. It has become a dark wonder to see entire clusters of villages rally behind a single figure—some to infiltrate the halls of officialdom, others to raise private militias to wage war upon their neighbors.

@4@4@3@2
The following handwriting is illegible, except for several lines of very neat handwriting in @3. Translated by the Linguistics Department, the text reads:

<Italic:"Shameless old wretch. His views are so warped, his prose so utterly tedious. I was bored, so I raised a few crickets to watch them fight—and still, he must wag his tongue and prattle on about it..."
<Bold:"It's none of your damn business.">>

—Excerpt from a handwritten copy of <Italic:Wild Fox Study Gazetter>.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Have you ever felt the world grow so deathly quiet that you felt the urge to shatter something, just to hear the sound?

Lust is the silver vase she hurls; the prey startled by its chime... they will slaughter for love and be slaughtered by love, until their spirit is bled dry and only a hollow shell remains.

Then, those drained husks will offer her ever greater dominion, staining everything in sight with a bloody, delicious chaos.
A chaos that never ends.

profile.title: Vortice
profile.introduction: A cheerful and forthright girl. Her heavy weapon channels vortex energy to unleash devastating destruction.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
A cheerful girl yearning for friendship. Inspired by her allies' Exalt, she can unleash coordinated attacks as {Pursuit}.
Her massive cannon, frighteningly disproportionate to her slender frame, devours energy hungrily. When her restraints are released, the vortex that cannon generates will instantly level everything around it.

In this Stage, Vortice, Ramona, and Ogier will join the battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlighten 3. At the Start of each turn, all Awakeners gain 50 Aliemus, draw 3 cards, and gain 3 Arithmetica.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
Language barriers? Not a problem—gestures work just fine. Uncontrollable power? At this school, that's par for the course. A few days in the lab usually resolve the issue.
With her beaming smile and a personality so blunt it borders on feral, Vortice quickly wins over the other Awakeners. Sure, she often wreaks havoc because she forgets what she's capable of (spare a thought for that fountain, rebuilt three times by now). But come on—she doesn't mean it!
...Probably.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
The North Atlantic whirlpool Moskstraumen was first observed in AF 1■■. Over the following centuries, it steadily grew in size. By AF 29■, Moskstraumen had evolved into a massive whirlpool roughly 260–300 feet (80–90 meters) in diameter, with currents stretching about 5 miles (8 kilometers) wide.
Beyond the powerful suction of the currents and devastating effects (claiming both marine life and fishing vessels in the vicinity), a greater threat lurked beneath Moskstraumen—a D-Rift. After prolonged observation and research conducted by Academy members, it was confirmed that the formation of Moskstraumen was directly linked to the D-Rift beneath it. In 1898, a formal operation was launched to purge it.
Within the D-Rift, the Academy recovered vast quantities of biological remains and shipwreck debris, along with a Silver Core later designated O-1841.

—Excerpt from <Italic:The Moskstraumen Incident Records>

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
The condition of Awakener Vortice is highly irregular. In an unrestrained state, her abilities do not activate at her own will. Irregular energy fields resembling the Moskstraumen vortex spontaneously form around her, pulverizing anything within range.
Physical contact with water-containing objects or people (including blood and other bodily fluids) accelerates field formation at the point of contact. Given both the involuntary trigger and the manic state she endured early in her Awakening, she was classified as "Extreme Danger."
To address this, the Awakener Department collaborated with the Physics and Chemistry Departments to develop "Vorticesteel"—a specialized synthetic alloy capable of withstanding her destructive power. This metal was fashioned into a muzzle and restraints for her to wear all the times.
Vortice's mental state began to stabilize after Researcher Caldwell's 11th "O-1841 Contact Experiment." During this trial, Vortice successfully internalized Moskstraumen's influence, significantly reducing the threat to her surroundings.
However, she cannot maintain this state without the "Vorticesteel" gear. Only when properly restrained can she stably contain Moskstraumen's effects within her core and channel its power for combat.
Upon regaining sanity, Vortice has revealed a cheerful disposition and an unexplained fondness for the Awakener Nautila. This likely stems from a shared experience tied to their spiritual origins.
—Excerpt from <Italic:Awakener Profile: Vortice>

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
Even if she could speak in complete sentences, she couldn't tell you where she was born. In truth, regardless of which "home" you mean, all she can recall now is blinding white sand, dense jungle, and a boundless sea.
But if you ask her where home is, she will tell you in broken phrases and passionate gestures: Home is on the sea, drifting wherever the currents take it.
Long-time isolation kept her from understanding loneliness. Yet on that ship, she first knew the warmth of a family and the bond of friendship: She shared grilled fish and iridescent shells with her companion whose name was derived from Nautilus, sharing dreams of land, of voyage, of adventure. And in the end, they shared a death swallowed by black waves and the whirlpool.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
Out on the boundless ocean, in the salty breeze, the ship gave her a family and friends beyond blood ties before the torrent swallowed everything. For the first time, she truly understood what loneliness was, and she never wanted to feel it again.
Yet for her best friend, her little family, she was willing to return to that pitch-black silence.
After the darkness covered everything, she was reborn in a new form. Sure, this new life comes with inconveniences—she keeps forgetting how destructive her power is—but the chance to make more friends still fills her with pure joy.
Of course, it would be even better if her best friend could remember the stories they once shared.
"Even better than better—super-duper better!"

profile.title: Arachne
profile.introduction: The Weaver of Fate spins the threads of destiny to safeguard the peace beneath her wheel, using the power of severance to excise all corruption.
profile.awakerIntroduction:
Bound by destiny, the {Weaver} grows more powerful through her continuous weaving, though she must bear an ever-increasing cost. By weaving the web of fate, she protects her allies and grants them Prismatic Power, allowing the accumulated {Final Verdict} to tighten and purge filth at the decisive moment.
In this stage, you will deploy Arachne and Nymphaea at Level 50, with Skill Level 5 and Enlighten 3.
During this battle, the effectiveness of {Poison} applied by all Awakeners is increased by 200%. At the start of each turn, all Awakeners gain 30 Aliemus, 1 stack of {Weaver}, 3 additional Aliemus, and draw 3 cards.

storySections.title: Basic Information
storySections.content:
The weaver ensnared by her own web; the playwright and eternal spectator of the Theater of Fate. 
Threads cascade from Arachne's fingertips, twining into curtains, scripts, and cages—each strand spun by her own hand, only to shroud her in an inescapable cocoon. 
The auditorium remains forever vacant, with none but herself to gaze upon the solitary performance onstage—a drama repeating without end, a play that knows no final curtain. She is the phantom haunting the theater, and the very incarnation of Fate itself.

storySections.title: Story: I
storySections.content:
In the late autumn of A.F. 3@7, I traveled to the "Theater of Fate" in the old district to audition for a mezzo-soprano position.	

I arrived at 4:17 PM. The sky was gloomy, yet no rain fell. The main entrance was unlocked, and the auditorium stood empty; there was no sign of any interviewers.	

Yet at the center of the stage, a puppet show was unfolding—there was no puppeteer, no music, only several pale humanoid marionettes dancing in silence. Their movements were unnervingly precise, and the faint clicking of their joints as they turned was spine-chilling. As for the strings... those threads descending from the dome seemed alive; they trembled slightly, tightening and slackening as if keeping time with an invisible rhythm.	

Just as I turned to leave, a woman’s voice drifted down from Box Five.	

<Italic:"Welcome, Christine.">

The voice was neither young nor old; it sounded like an echo seeping through dust-laden silk curtains sealed for centuries—eerie and terrifying.	

A cold shiver ran through me. I hadn't even introduced myself; how did she know my name? And these puppets...	

I bolted for the exit, but just as my hand reached for the handle, countless spiders swarmed from the darkness. Their eyes glimmered with ghostly light as they spewed out silvery threads, attempting to block my escape. More horrifying still, the spiders began to speak in human voices, chanting over and over: <Italic:"Sing, my angel... sing for me...">

I screamed. I tore at the webs. I ran with all my might. Somehow, I managed to escape.
...
Since then, I could not bear the thought of any gaze cast down from VIP Box Five, and so I requested that the opera house have the box sealed.	

And yet... now, whenever I stand beneath the spotlight and sing, I feel the faint tug of strings at the tips of my brow.	

Have I, too, become a puppet manipulated by invisible strings? I dare not think on it further.

—Excerpt from <Italic:The Memoirs of Christine, the Singer>

storySections.title: Story: II
storySections.content:
The curtain had not yet fully risen when the stage lights cascaded down like a relentless tide.

The audience of Mythag held their breath, waiting for the hero of fate to take the stage.

The stage was small—so small it could barely accommodate the heavy tread of Oedipus. Yet, as the boy in the school uniform raised his hand, his voice pierced the silence of that modest stage, his opening line falling like a divine oracle: <Italic:"It is I, the wretched man, doomed to slay my father and wed my mother.">
...
<Italic:"What law of the mighty gods have I transgressed?
 Why look to the heavens any more, tormented as I am?
 Whom to call, what comrades now? Just think, my reverence only brands me for irreverence!" >

So questioned the young Antigone, challenging the injustice of divine law.
...
In the castle of Denmark, Hamlet paced in contemplation. <Italic:"To be, or not to be, that is the question: Whether 'tis nobler in the mind to suffer the slings and arrows of outrageous fortune, or to take arms against a sea of troubles, and by opposing end them?">
...
Before the high bench, John Proctor slowly lifted his head and declared his answer: <Italic:"Because I am not worth the dust on the feet of them that hang! How may I live without my name?">
...
Those words concerning fate had coalesced into today’s performance.

Arachne stood in the wings, her fingers digging unconsciously into her palms.
The tears shed in empathy for the characters, the thunderous applause at the final curtain call...

As she took in the myriad of faces in the audience, she suddenly felt that her heart—whose beating she had long ceased to regard—had finally found its rhythm.
At this very moment, her voice, her very existence, was etched into the vast current of fate. She was no longer a mere witness to destinies; she was now the unfolding lines of a brand-new script. Audience, performer, playwright—their fates had become inextricably bound through this living drama...

The triumph of the heart, it turned out, was this quiet—and this deafening.

storySections.title: Story: III
storySections.content:
At first, Arachne did not know she could see Fate.

The world, like everyone and everything around her, was blurred, clamorous, and ruled by chance.
At the age of two, she spoke her very first word.

It was not "Mama," nor was it "Papa." Instead, standing beneath the porch, she whispered a single "Goodbye" to the old family butler.

That night, the butler died of a heart attack. Clutching the pocketwatch he had left behind, she wept herself to sleep outside the lodge. She did not yet understand death or destiny, but she had already tasted the bitter sting of a future revealed too soon.

At five, amidst the flushed faces and raucous cheers of banquet guests, she mimicked her mother, rising on tiptoe for a clumsy waltz.

Her red dancing shoes were as vivid as blood—an omen in plain sight. When she tripped during a turn, no one noticed the flash of a vision in her eyes, as if the fall itself had been destined.

On her seventh birthday, her father gifted her a spinning wheel named "Moirae," after the three Goddesses of Fate. She ran through the woods, her laughter bright and unguarded—until she tripped, and heard the crack: the spinning wheel was broken.

She knelt among the fallen leaves, tracing the fracture with her fingers, and she suddenly saw it: countless threads surging from the break, coiling around her fingertips and stretching into the invisible distance.
Then came the "firsts"—her first time in an opera house, her first true friend, the first time she buried a beloved dog...

Every "first" was haunted by a silent final act flashing before her eyes. She began to understand: every opening scene already carried the shape of its finale.

From then on, her gaze could no longer rest on surfaces. Human faces became mere veils, through which she could peer at the destinies churning beneath. She knew who would wither at the end of summer, who would hang themselves on the eve of their wedding, and whose child would bury them ten years hence. Surprise died; anticipation withered. 

The "future" was nothing more than an obituary she had opened ahead of time.
She tried, at first, to warn those around her of the misfortunes to come. But every time the words left her lips, they only hastened the prophecy's fulfillment.

Seeing the terror and recoil in their eyes, she finally grasped the truth: To foresee is not to forestall.
The Web of Fate was already woven. Her "foresight" was merely a stray beam of light slipping through the mesh—it illuminated the end, but was powerless to alter the weave.

And so, she took up the spinning wheel named "Moirae" again.

The break had been mended, but the scar remained. Day after day she spun, and her gift for fate merged itself into the wheel in ways she could not explain.

From that moment, the destinies of all flowed and intertwined through her fingers, woven into a colossal web spanning space and time.

Imperceptibly, she too was ensnared within this eternal web. She bore witness to laughter and grief beyond counting; infinite turning points, infinite possibilities, each one dragging its foredoomed destruction to the stage again and again...

Yet Arachne would not stop—not to weave, but to sustain. Should she cease, the entire Web of Fate might unravel, dragging countless lives into nothingness along with it. But she also saw that when the web was finally complete, Its awakening would bring devastation nothing could avert...

She is the weaver and the captive, the spectator and the prisoner, the playwright and the player.

Is it a blessing or a curse to see the ends of others?

Arachne stopped asking long ago.

storySections.title: Story: IV
storySections.content:
As the Weaver of the Web of Fate, Arachne governed the threads of countless lives, with past and future flowing through her hands. Yet, she harbored a hobby that bordered on the frivolous. 

She loved the theater. Not for the twisting plot or the well-turned phrase: those were already etched into her mind. As the creator of the script, she knew every turn of the story and every line of the actors by heart. 
Yet, time and again, she stepped into the theater. 

What she truly craved was the immersive sense of "presence"—the feeling of truly being there. 
When the curtain rises and the audience sinks into darkness, the stage becomes the only true world. In that moment, time is no longer a river running in a single direction, but an enactment within a confined space—past, present, and future overlap here. Everything ceases to be a script upon a page and becomes a tremor of life unfolding in the living present. 

And she, at last, is no longer the exile gazing at the threads she can never touch. 

Inside the theater, she can briefly seal away her memories. She can step onto the stage as the girl gasping out her final words, or sit among the crowd, granting the actors their freedom. Casting off the prophet’s burden, she allows herself to simply tremble, weep, and hold her breath at every stirring pulse of the performance. Not as a seer, but as a living soul—adopting the posture of the ignorant, breathing in sync with the characters, shattered by a single line. Even knowing the end is written, even knowing the tragedy is foreordained. This illusion allows her a fleeting escape from her sentence as an eternal spectator. 

No longer exiled from fate, but welcomed into it—even for a single instant, even as a phantom. 

This small indulgence was already the most luxurious grace fate could bestow upon Arachne.
